 P. 5. अथास्यामुपनिषदि etc.—In the नारायणीय section (No. XIII of नारायणोपनिषत्) following the दहरोपासना section (No. XII of the उपनिषत्), नारायण is very highly praised as परब्रह्म, परतत्व and so forth; such a description of नारायण naturally militate against the statement that नारायण was the उपासक. The anthor answers this objection by saying नारायण, being a devotee of शिव and having attained at–one–ment (सायुज्य) with शिव, well deserved the praise in such high terms. All devotees of शिव do become शिवऽ themselves after सायुज्यमुक्ति and are indistinguishable from शिव in that

condition, though they can reappear at the bebest of शिव, only to serve a particular purpose. But their re–appearance a not rebirth.

 P. 6. तद्भावभावनापन्नस्य etc.—In this annection अप्पय्यदीक्षिता's remarks are to be noted:—काले शिवं ध्यायतो नारायणस्य शिवोहम्भावभावनाभावितः शिवभाव एव ब्रह्मभूत इत्यनेनोक्तः। ततोऽन्यस्य तेनप्राप्यस्य ब्रह्मभावस्याभावात्। अत एव तपसा प्रसन्ने शिवे प्रत्यक्षे सति नारायणेन बहुधा परब्रह्मधर्मैः स्तवः कृतः। ......इत्येवं नारायणं शिवाहम्भावभावनाभावितशिवभावं व्यञ्जितवानित्यर्थः। \[see भारतसारसङ्ग्रह page 19).

 P. 6. केचित्तु etc.—This might refer to either वसवपण्डिताराध्य, the commentator of नारायणोपनिषत् or to श्रीपतिपण्डित, author of श्रीकरभाष्य, a very extentive commentary on the ब्रह्मसूत्राऽ. The former says—नारायणपरं ब्रह्म इत्यत्र नारायणात् परं यः शिवो वर्तते स एव ब्रह्म। बृहत्वधर्माश्रयः। नतु नारायण इत्यर्थः। the latter says—"नारायणपरं ब्रह्म" इति वाक्ये नारायणत्परं ब्रह्मेत्युपदिश्यते। Hence the compound नारायणपरं is to be dissolved as पञ्चमीतत्पुरुष and not as a कर्मधारयसमास. On the authority of these two the author proceeds to argu. "अत एव नारायणः परं ब्रह्मेत्यनुक्त्वा नारायणपरं ब्रह्मेत्येवाभिहितम्." This argument seems very strange. It does not necessarily mean that the compound was intended to be पञ्चमीतत्पुरुष, simply because it is not explicitly stated as नारायणः परं ब्रह्म. The wording could as well have been नारायणात्परं ब्रह्म instead of नारायणपरं ब्रह्म. It is seen that such a kind of reasoning is due to the high sectarian spirit. (see श्रीकरभाष्य page 103).

 The author may also refer to श्रीकण्ठाचार्य, the author of श्रीकण्ठभाष्य, in which भाष्य "नारायणपरं ब्रह्म" is discussed and and explained as नारायणात्परं ब्रह्म in सूत्राऽ 5, 6, 7 (of पाद II अध्याय। of ब्रह्मसूत्राऽ as follows:—शब्दविशेषात्॥५॥ नारायणपरं ब्रह्म इति शब्दविशेष एव नारायणात् परिमिति ब्रह्म प्रतिपादयति। etc. etc.

 As has been already remarked दहरविद्या or दहरोपासना conscious worship of ब्रह्मन् seated in the हृत्कमल) is considered to be of utmost importance for attaining सायुज्यमोक्ष. This विद्या has been established by बादरायण in his ब्रह्मसूत्राऽ in दहराधिकरण (पञ्चमाधिकरण contained in the 3rd of पाद of the 1st अध्याय).

सायणाचार्य in his commentary qutoed above refers to the very अधिकरण. All teachers of वेदान्त philosophy like शङ्कर, रामानुज, मध्व, and श्रीपतिपण्डित, the last a वीरशैव, interpret the अधिकरण in accordance with the tenets of their school Shaiva and Veerashaiva philosophers interpret this अधिकरण agreeably to the शक्तिविशिष्टाद्वैत philosophy. The Veerashaiva philosophers establish the principle of the worship of शिवas प्राणलिङ्ग (ज्योतिर्लिङ्ग) as the intermedate stage in their षट्स्थल philosophy. परमशिवेन्द्रपरस्वती has written a treatise called दहरविद्याप्रकाशिका, in which he establishes the worship of शिव seated in the heart of नारायण. Readers will find the treatise great interest. All this goes to prove that the search of ब्रह्म seated in the heart is indispensable for attaining मोक्ष. The following श्लोकाऽ, taken from तात्पर्यसङ्ग्रह, also called चतुर्वेदतत्सर्वसङ्ग्रह, will be of immense interest. (see हरदत्ताचार्या's तात्पर्यसङ्ग्रह)

**यस्मै नमो भवति यस्य गुणाः समग्राः नारायणोपनिषदा यदुपासनोक्ताः।  
यो नः प्रचोदयति बुद्धिमधिकृतौ यस्तं त्वामनन्यगतिरीश्वर संश्रयामि॥१॥**

**मन्त्रार्थवादविधिभिर्बहुधा विभक्तो यः शब्दराशिरनपेक्षतया प्रमाणम्।  
सोऽयं विमत्सरविमृष्टपदार्थतत्वो वेदः प्रकाशयति केवलमीश्वरं त्वाम्॥२॥**

**शब्दोऽयमीश्वर इति श्रुतिगोचरः सन्नानीलकण्ठमवतंसितचन्द्रलेखम्।  
त्वामेव बोधयति नाथ निजार्थपोषादन्यं पुनः प्रकरणादिनिपीडनेन॥३॥**

**पूजा तमुष्टिहिमता विहिता महेश मन्त्रेण वाङ्मनसकायनिबन्धना ते।  
सर्वाभिरप्युपनिषद्भिरुपासनोक्ता नारायणोपनिषदा परिभावयामः॥४॥**

**वाक्यं यदाह दहरान्तमुपासनं ते यद्वा समर्पयति रूपविशेषमस्मै।  
अन्तर्गतैरपि तदौपयिकाभिधेयैर्भाव्यं वचोभिरिति वाक्यविदां प्रवादः॥५॥**

**नारायणस्य यदुपास्यतयाभिधानं यद्वा फलं तदिह नोपलभे कथञ्चित्।  
उत्पत्तिशिष्टभवदन्वयमन्यगामि शक्यं महेश्वर न कर्तुमुपासनं ते॥६॥**

**नासावुपासनमुपास्यकतैव दूरे तस्मादुपासकतया परिशेषसिद्धः।  
अन्तर्व्यवस्थितगिरा परमात्मनस्ते शब्दादृतेऽपि विहितं तदुपासकत्वम्॥७॥**

**यस्य स्थितोऽपि हृदये परमस्त्वमात्मा युक्तं न तस्य परमात्मतयाभिधानम्।  
स्वेन स्वयं मनसि वन्हिशिखान्तराले तिष्ठन्नुपास्य इति हास्यपदं न कस्य॥८॥**

**नारायणप्रकरणे हृदयं यदुक्तं तस्यैव तद्भवितुमर्हसि नापरेशम्।  
दृष्टं यतिप्रकरणे यतिभिर्गृहीतं पूर्वानुवाकविहितं यदुपासनं ते॥९॥**

**नारायणस्य हृदयं सुषिरं महाग्निरग्नेः शिखा च परमात्मपदं यदुक्तम्।  
सर्वोऽप्युपासनविधेरयमर्थवादः श्रद्धाविशिष्टपुरुषाचरणाद्विधेयै॥१०॥**

**गीतासु योगजविभूतिविभावनाय नारायणः शिवपदं प्रतिवाचमूचे।  
योगं निशामय महेश्वर गोचरं मे दिव्यं ददामि तव चक्षुरिति प्रसन्नः॥११॥**

**त्वामन्तरेण न कथञ्चिदुपासनीय मन्यं ब्रवीति स शिवः श्रुतिरन्तकारे।  
यद्ब्रह्मशब्दसमभिव्यवहारयोगात् संहारकारिणि हरे शिवशद्वमाहुः॥१२॥**

**विश्वाधिकस्त्वमसि विश्वमयं मुरारिः यज्ञेश्वरस्त्वमसि यज्ञममुं गृणन्ति।  
आद्या जगत्प्रकृतिरेष भवान्निमित्तमात्रं कथं नु युवयोर्भगवन्नभेदः॥१३॥**

**स्थानं यथान्तरमुपास्तिरुपासको वा या सा परा परतया विविधा विभूतिः।  
यद्वा पुनर्विषमनेत्रमुपासितव्यं नारायणोपनिषदाह तदत्र सर्वम्॥१४॥**

 These verses fully bear out the discussion contained in the text from तैत्तिरीयके तु दहरं विपाप्म……… to प्राहुरलमतिविस्तरेण।

 The first three श्लोकाऽ state that शिव alone is praised by all Vedas. Verses 4, 5, 6 maintain the internal worship of शिवलिङ्ग as ज्योतिर्लिङ्ग. The third verse substantiates उपक्रमोपसंहारमध्यपरामर्शशिवैकान्तिकत्व Verses 7, 8, 9 maintain that नारायण is the उपासक. The verse 10 says यतिऽ are not the उपासकाऽ but नारायण is the उपासक. The remaining verses elaborate the same and conclude that नारायणोपनिषत् lays down the उपासना of शिव. (see तात्पर्यसङ्ग्रह pp. 1, 2, 9, 10, 11, 12).

 Many more extracts of शिवार्चन in दहरपुण्डरीक may be given: but the following three will be enough:—

दहराकाशे शिवोपलब्धिः।

**(a) अस्मिन्ब्रह्मपुरे वेश्म दहरं यदिदं मुने।  
पुण्डरीकं तु तन्मध्ये आकाशो दहरोऽस्तितत्॥  
स शिवः सच्चिदानन्दः सोऽन्वेष्टव्यो मुमुक्षुभिः॥**  
         (see पञ्चबह्मोपनिषत्—शैवोपनिषदः ).

**(b) हृप्तण्डरीकमलं रजोगुणविवर्जितम्।  
विशुद्धं पापराहित्यात् विशोकमतिनिर्मलम्।**

**विचिन्तयेत् स्वहृदयं तस्य मध्ये महामुने।  
दहराभिधानमत्यन्तसूक्ष्मं सुषिरमस्ति हि॥**

**तस्मिन्नचिन्त्यविभवमव्यक्तं परमाद्भुतम्।  
अनन्तरूपं सर्वेशं शिवं परमकारणम्॥**  
x x x

**उमासहायं विश्वेशं लोचनत्रितयोज्वलम्।  
सकलं परमेशानं नीलकण्ठमनीश्वरम्॥  
मुनिर्गच्छति तं ध्यात्वा पञ्चभूतैककारणम्॥**  
         (व्यासोक्तवसवपुराण pages 45–46).

**(c) आधारे दहरेऽव्यक्ते स्वर्णस्फाटिकवैद्रुमम्।  
निरन्तरानुसन्धानात् तदन्तर्धारणे विदुः॥**

**चतुर्दलं द्वादशारं व्द्यश्रमव्यक्तकं शिवम्।  
दहरेऽङ्गुष्ठमात्रं तमुमाकान्तमहर्निशम्॥**

**अनिराकारमात्मानं धृत्वा यान्ति परं पदम्॥**

(page 378 सदानन्दोपनिषत्—Seventy unpublished Upanishads, Adyar, Madras).

 Here ends the section on the wearing of Linga internally i. e. प्राणलिङ्ग. And the next section on the wearing of इष्टलिङ्ग on the body begins from एवमस्यामुपनिषदि etc.

 P. 6. सर्वलिङ्ग स्थापयति (पाणिमन्त्रं पवित्रं)—This forms the closing part of the 16th section of नारायणोपनिषत्. The section begins with निधनपतये नमः and addresses salutations to लिङ्गाऽ in different forms, which the author explaius later as the षट्स्थललिङ्गाऽ and concludes that सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति lays down placing i. e. wearing of Linga on the body.

 इष्टलिङ्ग is स्थूललिङ्ग, the third modification of शिव in the course of the evolution. The wearing of इष्टलिङ्ग on the body and its worship is a step to अन्तर्लिङ्गधारण and अन्तर्लिङ्गोपासना, though both बहिर्लिङ्गधारण and बहिर्लिङ्गोपासना and अन्तर्लिङ्गधारण and अन्तर्लिङ्गोपासना are mutually helpful and complimentary, as

may be noted from—बहिर्यागविहीनश्चेत् अन्तर्यागो न सिध्यति इति आगमवाक्यात्। अन्तर्लिङ्गानुसन्धाने रुचिर्बाह्ये न जायते इति सिद्धान्तशिखामणिवाक्यात्। इष्टलिङ्गं प्राणलिङ्गम् एकीकृत्य समर्चयेत् इति शिवरहस्यवाक्यात् अन्तरङ्गोपासनापूर्वकं बहिरङ्गोपासनं बहिरङ्गोपासनापूर्वकम् अन्तरङ्गोपासनं वीरशैवैः कर्तव्यम्। (वेदान्तसारवीरशैवचिन्तामणि page 6). Hence the devotee has to begin with बहिर्लिङ्गधारण for बहिर्लिङ्गोपासना Hence the author proceeds to establish बहिर्लिङ्ग Or इष्टलिङ्गधारण.

 सदानन्दोपनिषत् also says that Linga is to be worn both internally and externally for अपवर्ग as follows:—अथैनं सदानन्दः संवर्तो जैगीषव्यश्च नीललोहितं रुद्रमुवाच। भगवन् किमपवर्ग साधयतीति। स एतेभ्यो भगवान् नीललोहितः प्रोवाच। अन्तर्बहिर्धारितं परम्ब्रह्माभिधेयं शाम्भवं लिङ्गम्।

 The following will make the matter very clear,

**बाह्ये जाग्रदवस्थामनुभूतार्थ एव हि।  
अन्तः स्वप्नदशायां तु तथैव परिदृश्यते॥**

**तथान्तर्यजनं नित्यं बहिर्यजनपूर्वकम्।  
बहिर्यागविहीनश्चेदन्तर्यागो न सिध्धति॥**

**तब्दाह्याङ्गे चान्तरङ्गे परमप्रातिपूर्वकम्।  
कर्तव्यं स्वेष्टलिङ्गस्य पूजनं च मनीषिभिः॥**

**गवां सर्पिः शरीरस्थं न करोत्यात्मपोषणम्।  
निःसृतं कर्मणा बाह्ये पुनस्तासां तु भेषजम्॥**

**एवमन्तःशरीरस्थः परात्मा परमेश्वरः।  
विनार्चनां बहिर्देवो हितं न कुरुते नृणाम्॥**  
        (वीरशैवानन्दचन्द्रिका chapter iii)

 सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति is explained by वसवपण्डिताराध्य as सर्वे लिङ्गं स्थापयन्ति. He thus differs from the author. सर्वलिङ्गं is a compound. Mr. S. D. Pavate of Hubli dissolves it as सर्वव्यापकं लिङ्गं. But I think it is better dissolved as सर्वप्रकारकं लिङ्गं. सर्वप्रकारकं means भावप्राणेष्टादिप्रकारकं, which are further modified into six kinds as महालिङ्ग, प्रसादलिङ्ग etc. My explanation agrees with the author's explanation of the passage निधनपतये नमः etc. as the अर्थवाद of these दशविधलिङ्गाऽ. (see pp. 14–16).

 P. 6. ननु अत्र स्थापयतीति etc.—an objection to the assertion, that "सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति" enjoins the wearing of Linga, begins here and ends with समुच्चयासम्भवे परिसङ्ख्यायोगात् इति पूर्वपक्षः। the objection is a very long one, thorough and exhaustive, and says that the sentence "सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति" does not lay down the wearing of Linga (इष्टलिङ्ग) on the body.

 P. 6. लडन्तत्वेन—because the verb is in the present tense (लट्). The objector says that everything that is enjoined should be expressed by the imperative or the potential mood. But here the verb स्थापयति is in the present tense; hence it lacks the nature of an injunction.

 P. 6. ब्रीहीन् प्रोक्षति etc.—All Vedic sentences are significant and never unmeaning or superfluous. Vedic sentences, according to मीमांसकाऽ, express either a विधि (injunction), मन्त्र (incantation), नामधेय (desiguation), निषेध (prohibition), or अर्थवाद (missinnary assertion or eulogy). Though an injunction is expressed by imperative or potential mood, sometimes it is enjoined by a verb in the present tense, as in ब्रीहीन् प्रोक्षति. In the same way the verb स्थापयति might express an injunction. The objector says that the verb स्थापयति cannot do so, as it lacks the nature of an originative injunction (विधिप्रयोजकरूपाभावात्). The following discussion maintains why.

 P: 6. विधिरत्यन्तमप्राप्तौ etc.—This is a couplet that defines विधि (अपूर्वविधि), नियमविधि, and परिसङ्ख्याविधि, three forms of injunction. The stanza is taken from कुमारिला's तन्त्रवार्तिक; and defines succinctly the outstanding features of these three. Here विधि is defued as (विधेयस्य) अत्यन्तमप्राप्तौ, which is explained by the author as प्रमाणान्तराप्राप्तस्य, i. e. a Vedic injunction lays down something, which would otherwise have never occurred by any other means of proof. Such an injunction

is called an अपूर्वविधि, as in यजेत स्वर्गकामः। The author gives व्रीहीन् प्रोक्षति as an instance of अपूर्वविधि, in as much as no where else and by no other means of proof the प्रोक्षण of व्रीहिऽ is obtained.

 नियमविधि is a restrictive injunction defined by तन्त्रवार्तिक as (विधेये) पाक्षिके सति, i. e. where there are more alternative ways of doing a thing, the नियम restricts the operation to one only; for instance ब्रीहीनवहन्ति. Here it is enjoined that the व्रीहिऽ should be unhusked by thrashing only, and by no other method of doing it, like नखविदलन्, is to be resorted to. This नियमविधि is, therefore, पक्षे (अवहनने) प्राप्त and पक्षे (नखविदलने) अप्राप्त. The difference between नियमविधि and अपूर्वविधि is that the latter is अत्यन्तमप्राप्त, while the former is पक्षे प्राप्त and पक्षे अप्राप्त.

 परिसङ्ख्या is an injunction of exclusion, i. e. certain things are enjoined to be excluded. It is defined as तत्र चान्यत्र च प्राप्तौ, explained by the author as समुच्चयप्राप्तौ, which is the same thing as युगपत्प्राप्तौ (अर्थसङ्ग्रह). परिसङ्ख्या is etymologically explained as having the notion of exclusion, as परिशब्दः, अत्र वर्जनार्थः। सङ्ख्या बुद्धिः। This definition is well explained by विज्ञानेश्वर in मिताक्षरा, as एकस्य अनेकत्र प्राप्तस्य अन्यतो निवृत्त्यर्थम् एकत्र पुनर्वचनम्। the stock instance of this परिसङ्ख्या is पञ्च पञ्चनखा भक्ष्याः—only five animals of five nails are to be eaten, (namely, शशक, शल्यक, गोधा, खड्गी, and कूर्म). By this all other animals (अपञ्चनखाऽ as well as other पञ्चनखाऽ like cats and dogs) are to be excluded. This is तत्र चान्यत्र च प्राप्तौ—अनेकत्र or युगपत्प्राप्तौ, because all पञ्चनखाऽ as well as अपञ्चनखाऽ come across people for satisfying their appetite (रागतः प्राप्ते). नियम, therefore, restricts the operation to one of the alternatives; while परिसङ्ख्या lays down the exclusion of some things. Therefore it is an injunction of exclusion or excluding injunction.

In नियमविधि the alternatives are such that when one is adopted others are set aside; all are not simultaneously possible. But in it परिसङ्ख्या all alternatives are simultaneously possible. Therefore it enjoins exclusion.

 P. 7. प्रकृते "सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति" इत्यत्रः—The objecter applies the test of all the three विधिऽ to सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति and proves that the sentence cannot be any of the three विधिऽ. He first applies the test of अपूर्वविधि, which is अत्यन्तमप्राप्त, and says that सर्वलिङ्ग स्थापयति does not stand the test; because लिङ्गधारण is already enjoined by श्वेताश्वतरोपनिषत् in the hymn "सर्वाननशिरोग्रीवः etc." and therefore it is otherwise obtained.

 In connection with this objection the remarks of शिवकुमारपण्डित are noteworthy:—

 यद्यप्येकस्यैव सन्ध्यातर्पणादिधर्मस्य अनेकासु स्मृतिषु एकस्यैवाग्निहोत्रधर्मस्य अनेकासु शाखासु विधिः दृश्यते तथापि कस्यापि स्मृत्यन्तरशाखान्तरबोधितार्थबोधकत्वेन विधित्वं न व्याहन्यते। सर्वेषामेव वाक्यानां तत्तत्स्मृतितत्तच्छाखाध्येतारं प्रति अज्ञातज्ञापकत्वेन विधित्वोपगमात्। एवं श्वेताश्वरोपनिषद्वाक्येन सर्वलिङ्ग स्थापयतीत्यस्य विधित्वव्याघात आशङ्कितुं न शक्यते। तथापि सामान्यविशेषभावापन्नार्थबोधकत्वेन परस्परापेक्षितार्थकत्वमेव व्यवस्थापयितुं विधित्वव्याघातशङ्कोत्थापनमिति मन्तव्यम्।

 This relation of सामान्यविशेषभाव between सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति and सर्वाननशिरोग्रीवः etc. is established later by the author on page 11 and says that the former is the उपजीवक and the latter उपजीव्य; and therefore, no objection of अत्यन्तमप्राप्त can be advanced against the विधि enjoined by सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति.

 P. 7. त्रिविधपरिच्छेदरहितः—all–prevading and eternal; or unlimited by time, space, and form. This has been well explained by पण्डितशिवकुमार as follows:—

 परिच्छेदस्य तिस्रः विधा कालपरिच्छेददेशपरिच्छेदवस्तुपरिच्छेदश्चेति। कालपरिच्छेदो यथा "शतायुर्वै पुरुषः" इति। देशपरिच्छेदः "शतयोजनविस्तीर्णोऽयमालयः" इति। वस्तुपरिच्छेदो यथा इदं पार्थिवमिदं जलीयमिति। एतैस्त्रिविधपरिच्छेद–

रहितः। अर्थात्सार्वकालिकसर्वदेशवृत्तिसर्ववस्त्वात्मकः शिव इत्यर्थः। अयमत्र निष्कर्षः। प्रागभावप्रतियोगित्वे सति ध्वंसाप्रतियोगित्वं कालपरिच्छेदशून्यत्वम्। अत्यन्ताभावप्रतियोगित्वं देशपरिच्छेदशून्यत्वम्। भेदाप्रतियोगित्वं वस्तुपरिच्छेदरहितत्वमिति।

 The hymn सर्वाननशिरोग्रीव etc.—is taken from श्वेताश्वतरोपनिषत् 3–11. But it is to be noted here that the 1st half of the 2nd line is "सर्वव्यापी च भगवान्"; while in some editions, e. g. निर्णयसागर edition and भागवत's edition, the reading is "सर्वव्यापी स भगवान्". The differerence is in च for स as adopted by the author. The reading च seems to be appropriate, as it well explains the contrast, namely, the limitedness and omnipresence of the Oversoul, shown by the author later on page 8 in continuation of discussion in this very connection.

 P. 7. ननु सर्वाननशिरोग्रीव इत्यस्य etc.—This is the first objection raised against the position of the objecter. Here the objection is that सर्वाननशिरोग्रीवः and शिव, the qualifier and the qualified, being in grammatical equation must be identical, as the subject and the predicate must be. Hence there can be no relation of आधाराधेयभाव between शिव and आनन etc. But the explanation of given above shows that आनन etc. are the अधिकरण of शिव. i. e. there is आधाराधेयभाव of आननशिरोग्रीवः and शिव. There results from this the inconsistency of identity and आधाराधेयभाव. Therefore the position of the objectier being unsound, his objection is to be rejected. The objecter overrules the objection by quoting the authority of नीलकण्ठाचार्य, who preves that अधिकरणत्व of a thing for another (that is of the two things) was not inconsistent with their identity. It may be noted that on the next page the identity of Linga and मन्त्र is established on account of their being in grammatical quation. Later

also on page 37 the identity of वज्र and आज्य, that stand in apposition to each other, is established.

 P. 7. समानविभक्तिकयोरपि अधिकरणताप्रत्यायकत्वस्य......... नीलकण्ठाचार्यचरणैरङ्गीकृतत्वात्—It is difficult to understand to what नीलकण्ठाचार्य the author refers, on whose भाष्य he alleges to base this assertion. There is a भाष्य, said to be written by नीलकण्ठाचार्य, on the ब्रह्मसूत्राऽ, printed in Kanarese script and published by मुरगेन्द्रपण्डित. But the भाष्य does not contain the सिद्धान्त as alleged by the author, as may be noted from the following extract from the भाष्यः—तस्मादानन्दमयः परमेश्वरः। नन्वानन्दमय एवं ब्रह्मेति चेत् मयटो विकारार्थत्वात् विकारार्थस्य स्वप्रतियोगिसमानाधिकरणविजातीयाल्पत्वसापेक्षत्वात् दर्भमयी रशना पर्णमयी जुह्ररित्यादिषु दृष्टत्वात् ब्रह्मणः परमेश्वरस्य नित्यशुद्धत्वेन तस्मिन् अल्पदोषसम्बन्धो वर्जनीय इत्याशङ्कायामाह।

**विकारशद्वान्नेति चेन्न प्राचुर्यात्।**

 अन्नमयादीनां यथा विकाररूपत्वं तथात्रापि। यथा मृद्विकारो मृण्मयो घटादिः तथा परमेश्वरे विकारित्वसम्भवात् अयं जीव एवेति चेन्न मयट्प्रत्ययस्य प्राचुर्यार्थस्वीकारात्। अन्नमयो यज्ञः ब्राह्मणमयो ग्राम आनन्दमयं ब्रह्म इत्यादिषु प्रकाशमयश्चन्द्रः प्रकाशमयो रविरित्यादाविव स्वव्यधिकरणसजातीयाल्पत्वसापेक्षत्वस्य प्राचुर्यपदार्थत्वेन जीवीयानन्दाल्पत्वस्य बोधकत्वात् आनन्दमयः परमेश्वरः एव।

 Some think that this available भाष्य is not the genuine नीलकण्ठभाष्य but a spurious one, being a rehash of the real one, which the editor has not been able to trace. In as much as the author refers to and quotes from नीलकण्ठभाष्य in authority, there must have been a different नीलकण्ठभाष्य, which unfortunately is still unavailable to the public.

 P. 8. लौकिकेऽपि etc.—This is another reason that अधिकरणत्व does not militate against identity. The compound बहुकपालः is dissolved as बहवः कपालाः यस्य सः Here कपालाऽ are the अधिकरण of घट and are identical with घट. In the same way आननशिरोग्रीवाः are अधिकरणाऽ of शिव and are identical with शिव.

 P. 8. न चाननशिरोग्रीवः...वाक्यविरोध इति वाच्यम्। This is another objection raised against the position of the objecter, after the first objection was met and after it was concluded that आधाराधेयभाव was not inconsistent with identity. Here it is objected that if Linga (आधेय) was limited to various seats (आधाराऽ viz. आनन, शिरः, and ग्रीवा), it would contradict its सर्वव्यापकत्व (तस्मात्सर्वगतः शिवः). This objection is refuted by the following (भक्तानुग्रहार्थं etc.) The Linga, though naturally all–pervading, comes to be restricted to artificial limitations as आनन, शिरः, and ग्रीवा etc., like आकाश, which, though all–pervading, comes to the spoken of in its limited forms, as घटाकाश, मठाकाश etc.

 P. 8. भक्तानुग्रहार्थं—लिङ्ग is the उपास्य and अङ्ग is the उपासकः The परमात्मन, शिव, becomes both लिङ्ग and अङ्ग, उपास्य and उपासक respectively. The following extracts from अनादिवीरशैवसारसङ्ग्रह chap. I and वातुलागम will make this clear:—

 (a) तद्घनप्रहालिङ्गे स्वयमेव द्विधा भूत्वा, लिङ्गाङ्गभक्तजङ्गमरूपं भूत्वा, स्वयमेव आराध्यः आराधकः सन् अद्वितीयत्वात् वीरशैवम्।

 (b) स्वलीलया स्वयमेव द्विविधा भूत्वा स्वयमेव स्वयं पूजयितुं गौरवमिति स्वयमेव तन्महालिङ्गम् एवं षट्स्थललिङ्गाङ्गसङ्गी वीरशैवोऽभूत्। (pp. 11 and 14)

(**c) यद्यत्स्वरूपमाधत्ते गुरुत्वेनाम्बिकापतिः।  
शिष्यत्वेनायमात्मा च तत्तद्रूपमास्थितः॥**

**उपास्योपासकौ पूज्यपूजकौ साध्यसाधकौ।  
गुरुशिष्यौ शिवात्मानौ निगद्यते महात्मभिः॥**

**यद्यत्स्वरूपमाधत्ते साध्यत्वात्परमेश्वरः।  
साधकत्वादसावात्मा तत्तदास्थाय तिष्ठति॥**

****(वातुलागम quoted by अनादिवीरशैवसारसङ्ग्रह page 21)

 (d) परमशिवस्य पूज्यपूजकलीलायाम् etc—

(शिवाद्वैतमञ्जरी page 27 )

 P. 8. चकारः सार्थकः—Here the significance of च, already referred to above, is established. The author maintains that the first half of the hymn gives the limited forms of शिव and the latter half states the all–pervasive forms of शिव; and that both are properly connected by the conjunctive particle च.

 P. 8. अथैवमपि—Here is stated another (third objection) objection against the position of the objecter. The objection is that Linga may be limited to various आधेयाऽ. But its limitation to आनन (i. e. its residence in आनन) cannot be proved; because इष्टलिङ्ग is never placed in the month, on the authority of सिद्धान्तशिखामणि and शङ्करसंहिता, which unmistakably state that Linga is to be worn on the head, round the neck, on the stomach, breast, or on the palm. And as आनन is not stated as a seat, it is impossible that it should be a seat of Linga.

 P. 8. इत्यनाकलितरमणीयम्—Here begins the answer to the objection. It means that the objection looks well for want of real grasp of the truth. The truth, the answer says, is that मन्त्र is Linga, both being in grammatical equation, as the expressor and expressed.

 P. 8. मननात्रयते etc.—These verses are taken from स्कन्दपुराण–शङ्करसंहिता. The first verse gives the meaning of मन्त्र to be Linga derivatively. And the second states their identity on account of their being in grammatical equation, as the expressor and expressed. Being in grammatical equation they are one and the same.

 P. 9. व्यासनिरूक्त्या—व्यास is considered to be the author of the 18 Puranas, of which स्कन्दपुराण is one. The derivation, therefore, given of मन्त्र is his, शिवरहस्य also says:—

(See अनादिवीरशैवसारसङ्ग्रह vol. II pp. 74, 76)

**मननं सर्वपक्षेषु त्राणं संसारसागरात्।  
मननत्राणधर्मत्वात् मन्त्र इत्यभिधीयते॥**

**अभिधानाभिधेयत्वान्मन्त्रात्सिद्धः परः शिवः।  
एतावद्धि शिवज्ञानम् एतावद्धि परं पदम् ॥**

so also सिद्धान्तशिखामणि—vol. I p. 122

**अशेषजगतां हेतुः परमात्मा महेश्वरः।  
तस्यवाचकमन्त्रोऽयं सर्वमन्त्रैककारणम्॥**

**तस्याभिधानमन्त्रोऽयम् अभिधेयश्चसस्मृतः।  
अभिधानाभिधेयत्वात् मन्त्रात्सिद्धः परः शिवः॥**

 P. 9. नमः पीठमिति प्रोक्तं etc.—These verses are taken from सिद्धान्तसारावलि of त्रिलोचनशिवाचार्य. They explain how शिवलिङ्ग, that is worshipped either on the altar or on the body, is constituted. The latter is the miniature of शिवलिङ्ग, the amorphous representation of शिव. स्वप्रभानन्दस्वामी explains in his शिवाद्वैतमञ्जरी (page 32) that this amorphous representation of शिव contains in it the sixfold forms of the Linga, namely, आचारलिङ्ग etc. in the followin way:—तत्पीठं ह्याचारलिङ्गं, खण्डिकावरणे गुरुलिङ्गं, वर्तुले शिवलिङ्गं, गोमुखे जङ्गमलिङ्गं, योनिस्थाने प्रसादलिङ्गं, गोलके महालिङ्गं, इत्येवं षड्विधलिङ्गस्य प्रकाशमानत्वात्।

 P. 9. अत्र व्रीहिभिर्यजेत etc.—Here two kinds of corn व्रीहि or यव are laid down for preparing the cake (पुरोडाश), which forms यजनवस्तु; while व्रीहि or यव are the material for making it. The two kinds of corn are laid down alternatively for the preparation of the cake. And it does not matter whether it is prepared from either of them, so long as the object, worship, is served. In the same way the purpose of wearing Linga is मोक्ष. And if the end (मोक्ष) is served by wearing Linga, it does not matter whether the Linga is worn on the head, round the neck, or in the mouth. Hence the hymn सर्वानन etc. lays down alternatives.

 P. 9. अपि च क्षरं प्रधानममृतं etc:—This hymn क्षरं प्रधानं etc. is taken from श्वेताश्वतरोपनिषत् (I–10). In extenso it is,

**क्षरं प्रधानममृताक्षरं हरः क्षरात्मानौ ईशते देव एकः।  
तस्याभिध्यानाद्योजनात्तत्त्वभावात् भूयश्चान्ते विश्वमायानिवृत्तिः॥**

 The objecter maintains that the hymn lays down the wearing of Linga, both internally and externally, as borne out by the verses from सिद्धान्तशिखामणि (quoted by the author), which bases its statements on the Vedas. The internal wearing is laid down by the words तस्याभिध्यानात्, i. e. meditation of Him worn internally. योजनात् lays down the wearing of Linga externally on the body as इष्टलिङ्ग. If the wearing of Linga is laid down, says the objector, the placee of wearing Linga should need be stated in sequence. This is done by the hymn सर्वाननशिरोग्रीवः etc., which follows the hymn क्षरं प्रधानं etc, later. The devotee expects to know where it is to be worn. If the places of wearing Linga, as expected by the devotee, are not stated, the devotee would be at a loss to know where to wear it. The hymn सर्वानन etc. is significant, as it expressly states the places of wearing Linga on the particular parts of the body.

 आधारे = आधारचक्रे. हृदये = अनाहतचक्रे. भ्रूमध्ये = विशुद्धिचक्रे.

 P. 9. In corroboration of the verses quoted from सिद्धान्तशिखामणि. the following verses from सदानन्दोपनिषत् may be noted:—

**अन्तधारणशक्तेन ह्यशक्तेन द्विजोत्तमाः।  
संस्कृत्य गुरुणा दत्तं शैवं लिङ्गमुरस्थले॥**

**धार्यं विप्रेण मुक्त्यर्थे शिवतत्त्वविदो विदुः॥  
येनाचिरात्सर्वपापं व्यपोह्य परात्परं पुरुषमुपैति विद्वान्।**

****(See Unpublished Upanishads, Adyar, Mardas)

 P. 9. सन्दर्भविरोधः–going against the context. i. e. failing to fulfil this expectancy raised by the previous context. Sach failure would be very undesirable. The hymn सर्वाननशिरोग्रीवः is significant in that it fulfils the expectancy. Hence there is no अपूर्वविधि in सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति as asserted by the सिद्धान्तिन्

 P. 10. यद्येवं नाङ्गीकरोषि etc.–Here the objecter maintains his position finally by saying that the hymn सर्वानन शिरोग्रीवः etc. would be unmeaning, except in the light of the explanation given in the foregoing discussion. If the explanation, the author says, is not accepted, the hymn would either mean अभेद ( identity) of शिव with आनन, शिरः, ग्रीवा, or His residence there. But both these alternatives would be untenable, as they would surely conflict with the statements made about शिव, which are given next, viz. सर्वे ह्येष रुद्रः and तस्मात्सर्वगतः शिवः।

 P. 10. सर्ववाक्यं सावधारणं—This is taken from महाभाष्य of पतञ्जलि. This is well explained by पण्डित शिवकुमार as follows:—

 वामेनाक्ष्णा पश्यति इत्युक्तेरेवकारं विनापि वामेनैव पश्यतीति अवधारणप्रतीतेः। एतादृशस्थले इतरव्यावृत्तिलाभस्य औत्सर्गिकत्वादित्याशयः। तदेवोक्तं सर्ववाक्यं सावधारणमिति। एवमेव अस्मिन्ग्रामे क्षत्रियो धनिक इत्युक्तौ क्षत्रिव एव धनिक इत्यादिप्रतीतिरप्युदाहर्तव्या। यद्यपि देवदत्तस्य चत्वारः पुत्राः सन्तीत्यत्र देवदत्तस्यैवेति न प्रतीयते तथापि बाधनिश्चयोपपत्त्या तदंशेऽवधारणाप्रतीतावपि चत्वार एव सन्त्येवांशान्तरे अवधारणप्रतीतिरस्त्येव स्पष्टं चेदम्। ...............इत्यादि ग्रन्थेन एवकारं विनापि तदर्थपरत्वप्रतीतिस्थलप्रदर्शकमहाभाष्ये तच्चेति अधिकरणताबोधकपदं चेत्यर्थः।

 P. 10. ननु सर्वाननशिरोग्रीव इत्यत्र etc.—This is the fourth and the last objection raised against the position of the objecter. The objection is that if there had been a word expressive of अधिकरण (seat of शिवलिङ्ग viz. आनन, शिरः, and ग्रीवा), the assertion of शिव residing there would be established.

But as there is no snch word the assertion is meaningless. This objection is answered by the objecter by saying that the अधिकरण is not expressed but indicated, when the तद्धित affix अच् expressing possession or having the things (आनन etc.) as seats is appended. Except for this the whole thing सर्वाननशिरोग्रीवः etc, would be meaningless. But instead of making the whole thing meaningless it is better, says the objecter, to assume अधिकरण by indication, according to the rule (वरं हि वाक्यवैय्यर्थ्यात्पदमात्रस्य लक्षणा) established in पूर्वमीमांसा in connection with the पशुयाग, as is discussed next.

 P. 10. मत्वर्थीयाच्प्रत्यनेन—the compound सर्वाननशिरोग्रीवः is dissolved by the author as—सर्वेषां लिङ्गधारणयोग्यानाम् आननशिरोग्रीवाः अधिकरणं यस्य सः। this is a बहुव्रीहिसमास. But the author in stead of taking this compound as a बहुव्रीहिसमास says that the word is obtained by the possessive affix अच्. पण्डित शिवकुमार remarks in this connection as follows:—

 यद्यपि बहुव्रीहिणैव तादृशबोधो निर्वहति तथापि एकार्थीभावानङ्गीकर्तृमते उत्तरपदेऽन्यपदार्थलक्षणाया एव स्वीकारेण तत्स्थानीयकल्पान्तरस्याप्यवसरोऽस्तीत्याशयेन एवमुक्तम्। वस्तुतः सहस्रशीर्षा पुरुषः इतिवत् सर्वतः पाणिपादं तत् सर्वतोक्षिशिरोमुखम् इतिवच्च सर्वेषाम् आननशिरोग्रीवं यस्येति व्यधिकरणबहुव्रीहिणा सर्वात्मकत्वबोधकमेवेदमपीति बोध्यम्।

 P. 10. अभ्युपगतं च पूर्वतन्त्रे—The rule referred to just above, has been established in पूर्वमीमांसा (in अ. 10 वाद 7 अधि. 20). in सूत्राऽ—

 यथाश्रुति इति चेन्न। चोदनैकत्वात्।—According to text if you say; it is not so, by reason of there being one injunction.

 पार्थसारथिमिश्र comments on these सूत्राऽ as follows:—

**वपायामेव किंवान्येष्वपि पञ्चावदानता।  
अपेद्दिशादाद्यपक्षोऽवदानोद्देशतोऽन्तिमः॥**

 "एकादश वै पशोश्वदानानि तानि द्विरवद्यति" इत्येतत् द्व्यवदानवचनमिदमत्र सिद्धान्तस्य मूलयुक्तिः। वपामात्रविषये हि पञ्चावदाने प्रकृतित एव द्विर्हविषोऽवद्यतीति प्राप्तत्वात् द्व्यवदानवचनम् अनर्थकं स्यात्। सर्वविषयं तु पञ्चावदाने सङ्ख्यासम्पत्तये हविषस्त्र्यवदाने प्रात्पे द्व्यवदानविधानम् अनर्थवत् भवति। सकलवचनानर्थक्यात् वरमेकस्य वपापदस्योपलक्षणार्थत्वम् अङ्गीकृतम्। तस्मात्सर्वविषयं पञ्चत्वं तच्चाङ्गेषु द्व्यवदानवचनात् उपस्तरणविवृध्द्या अभिधारणविवृध्द्या वा सपन्द्यते तस्मादङ्गेष्वपि पञ्चावदानम्। (शास्त्रदीपिका—page 770 निर्णयसागर edition).

 P. 10. प्रकृतिवद्विकृतिः कर्तव्या—Here it is necessary to understend what is meant by प्रकृति and विकृति. प्रकृति is a fundamental rite or sacrifice. i. e. an Archetype (of a sacrifice) and विकृति is a modification or a derivative. प्रकृति and विकृति are defined by अर्थसङ्ग्रह as follows:—यत्र समग्राङ्गोपदेशः सा प्रकृतिः। यथा दर्शपूर्णमासादिः। तत्प्रकरणे सर्वाङ्गपाठात्। यत्र न सर्वाङ्गोपदेशः सा विकृतिः। यथा सौर्यादिः। तत्र कतिपयानाम् अङ्गानाम् अतिदेशेन प्राप्तवात्। In प्रकृति or the Archetype all rules about subsidiary matters are specified. But in derivatives rules regarding the subsidiary matters are not so specified. So it is laid down that all subsidiary matters of a विकृति are performed by the rules prescribed for the Archetype. The rules of a प्रकृति are transferred to the विकृति by the principle प्रकृतिवत् विकृतिः कर्तव्या (a derivate should be performed in the same manner as an Archetype). This principle of transferring the rules is called अतिदेश (application by protraction or transfer). The meaning of अतिदेश is made clear by शबर as follows:—अतिदेशो नाम ये परत्र विहिता धर्माः तमतीत्य अन्यत्र तेषां देशः। यथा देवदत्तस्य भोजनविधिं कृत्वा शालीसूपमांसापूयैः देवदत्तः भोजयितव्यः इति तमेव विधिं यज्ञदत्ते अतिदिशति देवदत्तवत् यज्ञदत्तो भोजयितव्यः इति। श्लोकमपि उदाहरन्ति।

**प्रकृतात्कर्मणो यस्मात् तत्समानेषु कर्मसु।  
धर्मप्रदेशो येन स्यात्सोऽतिदेश इति स्थितिः॥**

 P. 10. चोदकेन—प्रकृतिवत् विकृतिः कर्तव्या इति चोदकशब्दातिदेशवाक्येन (रामेश्वरभिक्षु's अर्थसङ्ग्रहटीका).

 P. 10. द्विरवदानप्राप्तौ.......साधितमिति।—The whole thing has been well explained by पण्डित शिवकुमार as follows:—पाशुके हविषि श्रूयते "एकादशपशोरवदानानि तानि द्विरवद्यति" इति पाशुकयागे पशोरेकादशाङ्गानि तानि प्रत्येकं द्विरवद्यति तेभ्यः अवदानपूर्वकं हविरववयं गृण्हातीत्यर्थः। अस्य च कर्मणः सोमविकृतित्वेन प्रकृतिवत् विकृतिः कर्तव्या इति. चोदकवाक्येनैव एतदीयहविष्षु द्विरवदानप्राप्ततया द्विरवद्यति इति वाक्यस्य वैयर्थ्यं भवतीति पूर्वपक्षे अस्मिन्नेव प्रकरणे यद्यपि चतुश्वत्ती यजमानः पञ्चावत्ता वपा कार्या इति वपायाः पञ्चावदानानि विधीयन्ते। पञ्चावदानस्थले च प्रथमावदानमुपस्तरणार्थम् आज्यस्य क्रियते। अन्तरावदानत्रयं हविषः क्रियते। अन्तिममवदानमभिधारणार्थं पुनराज्यस्य क्रियते। एवं पञ्चत्वसङ्ख्यापूर्यते। अत्र तानि द्विरवद्यतीति वाक्यवैयर्थ्यपरिहाराय वपापदस्य पाशुकविःसामान्ये लक्षणया हृदयादिहविःष्वपि पञ्चावत्तं प्राप्नोति। तत्र चोपस्तरणाभिधारणाभ्यां द्वित्वसङ्ख्याया निष्पन्नत्वेन पञ्चत्वसङ्ख्यापूर्तये हृदय दीनां त्रिरवदानं प्राप्नोति। तद्वाधाय द्विरवद्यतीत्युच्यते। तेन हविरवदानेषु पूर्वप्राप्तत्रित्वस्य द्वित्वेन बाधात् पञ्चावतैववपाकार्येति प्राप्तपञ्चत्वसङ्ख्यायाः पूर्तिः उपस्तरणार्थमवदानस्य वारद्वयकरणेन अभिधारणाय वा वारद्वयकरणेन करणीया इति सिध्यान्तितम्। अत्र यथा तानि द्विः अवद्यति इतिवाक्यस्य वैयर्थ्यवारणाय वपापदस्य हविः सामान्ये लक्षणा स्वीक्रियते तथा सर्वाननशिरोग्रीवादिवाक्यस्य वैयर्थ्यवारणाय सर्वाननशिरोपदस्य सर्वाननाद्यधिकरणके लक्षणा स्वीकर्तव्येति तात्पर्यम्।

 P. 10. पूर्वाचार्यास्तु—It is not possible to determine to whom the author refers as पूर्वाचार्याः, whose opinion is quoted in support of the objecter's position. The editor has been unable to trace them. The objecter strengthens his own position by the quoting the opinion of the former philosophers, who said that the principle of wearing Linga was established in पाणिमन्त्रं पवित्रं. They, therefore, held that लिङ्ग धारण was not altogether unobtained. So far as the text goes, it is not clearly said that सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति did not contain an अपूर्वविधि on account of पाणिमन्त्र पवित्रं containing the same; still the objector takes advantage of their assertion and says

that because पाणिमन्त्रं पवित्रं conatined a विधि, the position of the सिद्धान्तिन् that सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति contained an अपूर्वविधि was not sound and therefore fell to the ground.

 P. 10. वक्ष्यमाणरीत्या—This (पाणिमन्त्रं) has been explained later on page 12 of the text in words पाणिमन्त्रभित्यस्य etc.

 P. 10. ननु मास्त्वपूर्वविधिः etc.—Unill now the objecter was at pains to prove that there was no अपूर्वविधि in सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति, as the विधि was already laid down in सर्वाननशिरोग्रीवः etc. Now he proceeds to prove that there is neither नियमविधि nor परिसङ्ख्या in सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति First he proves that there is no नियमविधि. But the instances of नियमविधि and परिसङ्ख्याविधि are given in reverse order, as remarked by पण्डित शिवकुमार—अस्मिन्प्रकरणे नियमपरिसङ्ख्यापदयोः प्रयोगौ वैपरीत्येन अनवधानतः कृतौ। For instance, the instance given of नियम is "याजनाध्यापनप्रतिग्रहैर्ब्राह्मणो धनमर्जयेत्". Here the anthor says that याजन etc. are restricted to ब्राह्मणाऽ as the ways of earning money. But धनार्जन is रागतः प्राप्त as the means of सुख and दुःखाभाव as is said:—

 द्रव्यार्जने रागः प्रवर्तकः दृष्टः इति। तत्र विधिर्नापेक्ष्यते। फलं च क्षुन्निवृत्यादिरूपं दृष्टमेव। अत एव स्मर्यते।

**षण्णां तु कर्मणामस्य त्रीणि कर्माणि जीविका।  
याजनाध्यापने चैव विशुद्धाच्च प्रतिग्रहः॥**

(जैमिनिन्यायमालाविस्तार—220).

 It is not, therefore, पक्षे प्राप्त and पक्षे अप्राप्त but युगपत्प्राप्त.

 Proceeding from the simile the author says that भस्मधारण, रुद्राक्षधारण, and लिङ्गधारण are पक्षतः प्राप्त. But they are not पक्षतः प्राप्त but युगपत्प्राप्त. Anyhow the author proves by quoting from सिद्धान्तक्षिखामणि that all the three are necessary for attaining मोक्ष and not लिङ्गधारण alone.

 P. 11. भस्मच्छन्नो etc.—This verse is not found in any of the printed editions of शातातपस्मृति now available to the

reading public. It is probable that there might be a different redaction of the स्मृति in South India, from which the author took the श्लोक. Our suspicion that there must have been a different edition of this स्मृति in South India is confirmed by the fact that भट्टभास्कर quotes this very verse with introductory words इत्याहशातातपः। But this श्लोक appears in स्कन्दपुराण, रुद्रप्रशंसाखण्ड as follows:—

**स्तेयं कृत्वा गुरुदारांश्च गत्वा।  
सुरां पीत्वा ब्रह्महत्यां च कृत्वा।  
भस्मच्छन्नो भस्मशय्याशयानो।  
रुद्राध्यायी मुच्यते सर्वपापैः॥**

 P. 11. ननु तर्हि etc.—Lastly the objecter proves that there is no परिसङ्ख्या, as लिङ्गधारणस्य कर्तव्यता and अकर्तव्यता cannot, being contradictory, go together so as to be alternatives for positive action. अकर्तव्यतायाः स्वत एव निषेधात् नियमादेः अपेक्षा नास्तीति भावः। (पण्डित शिवकुमार).

 P. 11. अत्रोच्यतेः—Here begins the refution of the foregoing long objection, which is met by the सिद्धान्तिन् saying that सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति does contain an अपूर्वविधि, which he ultimately proves. There remains, therefore, no necessity for the सिद्धान्तिन् to refute that there is neither नियमविधि nor परिसङ्ख्या in सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति, which go automatically. The सिद्धान्तिन् says that the words सर्वाननशिरोग्रीवः etc, though they contain an injunction to wear Linga only indirectly, lay down specifically the places of wearing Linga on the body. सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति on the other hand, enjoins directly स्थापन(wearing) of Linga. But the main thing being स्थापन, the places of wearing Linga follow स्थापन in expectancy. Hence the sentence सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति is the sustainer (उपजीव्य) and the sentence सर्वाननशिरोग्रीवः is the sustained (उपजीवक). On account of this relation between them it cannot be said,

says the सिद्धान्तिन्, that the sentence सर्वाननशिरोग्रीवः should affect in the least the nature of सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति of being an अपूर्वविधि.

 P. 11. तस्याभिध्यानाद्योजनात्—Here there is the word योजनात्, which lays down the योजना (संयोग) or wearing) of Linga. This is the prima facie objection to the सिद्धान्तिन्'s assertion that सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति is the sustainer and सर्वाननशिरोग्रीवः is the sustained. सर्वाननशिरोग्रीवः, theretore, says the objecter, is dependent on क्षरं प्रधानं ete, and not on सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति.

 P. 11. वैषम्यात् etc.—This is the refutation of the foregoing objection. Here the सिद्धान्तिन् says the sentence सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति has the great advantage of having the words "लिङ्गं’ and "स्थापयति', directly expressive of the thing to be worn and the wearing. While योजनात् is not so directly expressive of wearing Linga like स्थापयति. Moreover योजनात् is a noun but स्थापयति is a verb. The word योजनात्, therefore, expresses the wearing of Linga only remotely (विलम्बोपस्थायिक).

 P. 12. लोकवेदाधिकरणन्यायेन—This न्याय is discussed and established in पूर्वमीमांसा in 1-3-30. The सूत्र is:— 

 चोदनाभावादर्थैकत्वमविभागात्ः—By reason of apprehension of the meaning from the utterance of words, there is the unity of sense, because there is no distinction. The सूत्र is well explained in a few words by रामेश्वरसूरि in his जैमिनिसूत्रवृत्ति, as follows:—लोकवेदयोः शब्दार्थौ भिन्नौ उताभिन्नाविति संशये शब्दार्थौ भिन्नौ। देवासः भवाति कर्णेभिरिति रूपभेदात्। उत्ताना हि देवगवा वहन्ति इति चार्थभेदादिति वहिः पूर्वपक्षे सिद्धान्तमाह प्रयोगेति। पूर्वमुभयोः (लोकवेदशब्दाथर्योः) भेद इति पूरणीयम्। प्रयोगस्य वाक्यस्य चोदना उच्चारणजन्यार्थप्रतीतिः तदभावात् अभावप्रसङ्गात्। अयं भावः। वैदिकशब्दार्थयोः भिन्नत्वे तयोरपूर्वत्वेन शक्तिग्रहासम्भवात् अर्थज्ञानं न स्यादिति। अतः शब्दानाम् अविभागात् अभेदात् अर्थैक्यमपि। लोके गृहीतशक्तिकत्वात्तदुपपत्तिः (यूपादिशब्दानां तु प्रसिद्धपदसमभिव्याहाराच्छाक्तिग्रह उपपद्यते इतिभावः).

 माधवाचार्य in his जैमिनिन्यायमालाविस्तर makes the same conclusion in words—तस्माद्ये लौकिकाः पदपदार्थाः ते एव वैदिकाः। He quotes from वार्तिक the principle (न्याय) in words "लोकावगतसामर्थ्यः शब्दो वेदेऽपि बोधकः". It is to be noted that रामेश्वरसूरि interprets चे\।दना as उच्चारणजन्यार्थप्रतीतिः। and not as an injunction. While पाणिनि Office, Allahabad, translates it as "by reason of direction as to action.''

 From the foregoing it is clear that वैदिक words and लौकिक words (popular words or words in currency) are one and the same in meaning or import, though they might differ in form. The popular words have, therefore, the power of elucidating the Vedic words and language and helping people to understand the Vedic words rightly. This is made clear by पण्डितशिवकुमार who remarks—

 लोके व्यवहारादिना गृहीतशक्तिकानां पदानां वेदेऽपि सत्त्वोपगमे अर्थबोधकत्वमुपपद्यते। वैदिकशब्दानां लौकिकशब्देभ्यो भिन्नत्वभिन्नार्थकत्वोपगमे तु शक्तिग्रहाभावादर्थबोध एव तेभ्यो नस्यादिति व्यर्थान्येव वाक्यानि भवेयुरिति अवश्यमेकार्थत्वं लौकिकवैदिकशब्दानां मन्तव्यम्।

 The popular words are, hence, mainly dependable for the right understanding of the Vedic words and language. But the popular words derive their power of conveying the right meaning of sentences from grammar in their syntactical connection. The objector, therefore, says that the words, through they have to depend upon grammar for their expressive power, are still capable of laying down something, as the words पशुना यजेत do. In the same way, says the objecter, the words सर्वाननशिरोग्रीवः etc., the sustained, can lay down, even though it has to depend upon सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति, the sustainer. The position of the objector is finely explained by पण्डितशिवकुमार, who comments—

 पशुना इत्यत्र तृतीयानिष्ठा यजेत इत्यत्र यजनिष्ठा लिङ्निष्ठा च शक्तिः कर्तृकरणयोः तृतीया विधिनिमन्त्रणामन्त्रणेत्यादिशास्त्रैः बोध्यते इति तत्सापेक्षत्वमत्र बोध्यम्। तद्वदिहापि किन्न स्यादिति। यथा पशुना यजेतेति वाक्यस्य व्याकरणसापेक्षत्वेऽपि विधित्वं न हीयते एवं सर्वाननशिरोग्रीव इति वाक्यस्य सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति इतिवाक्यसापेक्षत्वेऽपि विधित्वं कस्मान्न भविष्यतीत्यर्थः।

 P. 12. व्याकरणादीनां etc.—This is refutation of the objection by the सिद्धान्तिन्, who maintains his position by saying that, though the words have to depend upon grammar for their power to lay down something, their dependance ends there; and they have not to depend upon other words for what they lay down, as for instance the words पशुना यजेत do. But the case of सर्वाननशिरोग्रीवः etc. is different, in as much as they have to depend upon other words (सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति) for making the injunction of लिङ्गधारण manifest. The position of the सिद्धान्तिन् is very well expressed by पण्डित शिवकुमार, who says—पशुना यजेतेत्यत्र प्रकृतवाक्यार्थबोधोपयोगिवृत्तिज्ञानाधीनपदजन्योपस्थित्यादिघटितकारणकलापसद्भावे स्वजन्यत्वेन ताप्तर्यविषयीभूतबोधे जननीये वाक्यान्तरस्य कस्यापि अपेक्षा नास्ति। सर्वाननशिरोग्रीव इत्यनेन तु स्वघटकपदजन्योपस्थित्यादिघटितकारणसमुदायसत्वेऽपि आधेयांशबोधाय सर्वलिङ्गवाक्यस्य अपेक्षा अस्ति एव।

 विधायकत्वासिद्धैः is another reading for विधायकत्वसिद्धेः।. But the latter reading seems to be quite correct, as it forcibly brings about the contrast, as intended by the author, between the two sentences सर्वाननशिरोग्रीवः etc. and सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति. पण्डित शिवकुमार takes the former reading and makes a strained commentary in explanation of the same.

 P. 12. यच्चोक्तं etc.—This is the refutation of the objection contained in पुर्वाचार्यास्तु etc. on page 10. The objection was that सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति did not contain an अपूर्वविधि, as the विधि to the effect of wearing Linga on the hand was

contained in पाणिमन्त्रं पवित्रं. The सिद्धान्तिन् now refutes that objection by saying that सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति laid down the wearing of Linga in general; while पाणिमन्त्रं पवित्रं laid down the wearing of Linga on the hand specifically. The former simply laid down the wearing of Linga. But the latter lays down the place of wearing. Hence पाणिमन्त्रं पवित्रं was उपजीवक and सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति was the उपजीव्य, The objection, therefore, was futile.

 P. 12. अत्र अग्निहोत्रं जुहोतीति etc.—अग्निहोत्रं जुहोति is the sentence to establish the designation of a sacrifice, according to तत्प्रख्यन्याय, established in पूर्वमीमांसा of जैमिनि in I-4-4. (तत्प्रख्यञ्चान्यशास्त्रं). तत्प्रख्य means अन्यशास्त्र or तद्गुणप्रापकशास्त्र, by means of which तत् (गुणद्रव्य, आधार or देवता) is determined by the help of other text (शास्त्र). Hence अग्निहोत्रं जुहोति is only a नामधेय. The मीमांसकाऽ argue that, if according to the objecter, अग्निहोत्रं जुहोति is not नामधेय, it should state a गुणद्रव्य. On the one hand अग्नि can be taken as the आधार of होम, the गुणद्रव्य, by dissolving the compound as अग्नौ होत्रं; or अग्नि may be taken as the देवता of the होम by dissolving the compound as अग्नये होत्रं. In the first case it means that we should offer oblation to fire (element) and nothing else. In the second case it means that we should offer the होम to अग्नि as देवता and not the element. So the विधि अग्निहोत्रं जुहोति, can be a गुणविधि satisfying either आश्रयाकाङ्क्षा or देवताकाङ्क्षा. But says the मीमांसक that अग्निहोत्रं जुहोति cannot do either. Because आश्रयाकाङ्क्षा is satisfied by another विधि—यदाहवनीये जुहोति, which gives the आहवनीयाग्नि as आश्रय (अधिकरण). And so long as the text आहवनीये जुहोति is there, the interpretation of अग्निहोत्रं जुहोति as गुणविधि for आश्रय becomes superfluous (निरर्थक). In the same way देवताकाङ्क्षा is served by a different text—अग्निर्ज्योतिर्ज्योतिरग्निः स्वाहा. So अग्निहोत्रं जुहोति is only a नामधेय and आहवनीय expresses

आधार or अधिकरण. This is discussed in अर्थसङ्ग्रह as follows:—

 अग्निहोत्रं जुहोतीत्यत्र अग्निहोत्रशब्दस्य कर्मनामधेयत्वं तत्प्रख्यशास्त्रात्। तस्य गुणस्य प्रख्यापकस्य प्रापकस्य शास्त्रस्य विद्यमानत्वात् अग्निहोत्रशब्दः कर्मनामधेयमिति यावत्। नन्वयं गुणविधिरेव कुतो नेतिचेन्न। यद्यग्नौ होत्रमस्मिन्निति सप्तमीसमासमाश्रित्य होमाधारत्वेन अग्निरूपः गुणः विधेयः तदा आहवनीये जुहोति इत्यनेनैव प्राप्तत्वात् तद्विधानानर्थक्यम्। अग्नये होत्रमिति चतुर्थीसमासमाश्रित्य अग्निदेवतारूपगुणोऽनेन विधीयते इति चेन्न। तद्देवतायाः शास्त्रान्तरेण प्राप्तत्वात्। किं तच्छास्त्रमितिचेत्.....अग्निर्ज्योतिर्ज्योतिरग्निः स्वाहेति।

 The author applies this न्याय to the matter under consideration and says that सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति lays down the स्थापन of लिङ्ग in general but not आधार, which is laid down by पाणिमन्त्रं पवित्रं, as आहवनीये जुहोति does to अग्निहोत्रं जुहोति.

 P. 12. पवित्रशद्वेन etc.—Here the occurrence of the word पवित्र is justified; because the word पवित्र shows that the Linga is always pure and cannot be made impure even when it is worn at impure times like menses etc. Later the author vindicates at fuller length the position of Linga remaining pure at all times in another section. यज्ञोपवीत—is the sacred thread to be worn at all times by द्विजाऽ after the उपनयन ceremony. The thread is always pure and is not defiled even during the times of impurity. Similarly Linga remains pure at all times. But यज्ञोपवीत is interpreted by नञ्जण्णार्थ as लिङ्ग itself in the following way:—अत्र यज्ञोपवीतमित्यस्यार्थः। नारायणस्य शिवलिङ्गशक्तिपीठत्वं पुराणेषु प्रसिद्धम्। "यज्ञो वै विष्णुः' इति श्रुतेः यज्ञनाम्नः पीठस्य उप सामीप्येन विशेषेण इतं लिङ्गं यज्ञोपवीतमित्यर्थः। उत यज्ञम् इति पूजा। तस्मात् पूजया उपवीतं (लिङ्गं) इत्यर्थः। (Bracketed लिङ्गं is mine.)

(see वेदान्तसारवीरशैवचिन्तामणि page 175).

 P. 13. नचानेकविधाने वाक्यभेदः—This is an objection that the sentence पाणिमन्त्रं पवित्रं lays down two things, namely, लिङ्गस्य पाण्यधिकरणत्व and पवित्रत्व. It thus commits the fault of

वाक्यभेद (a sentence making diversity of statements). The principle that one senenece must convey only one sense,i. e. there must be no वाक्यभेद, has been established by जैमिनि in सूत्र "अर्थैकत्वादेकं वाक्यं" in II-1-14.

 P. 13. अप्राप्तत्वे विधीयन्ते बहवोऽप्येकयत्नतः—This is the reply to the fault of वाक्यभेद. The सिद्धान्तिन् says the fault is removed by taking the sentence as laying down a विशिष्टविधि or a qualified विधि, as पाण्यधिकरणविशिष्टपवित्रत्वविशिष्टलिङ्गपूजनविधिः। This principle of laying down a qualified विधि is established in पूर्वमीमांसा (I-4-9). The सूत्र is—तद्गुणास्तु विधीयेरन्नविभागात् विधानार्थे न चेदन्येन शिष्टाः (on the other hand the action and the qualities are laid down because they cannot be separated for the purpose of laying down otherwise the qualities will be known by another). The commentary of रामेश्वरसूरि is as follows:—यदìग्नेयो अष्टाकपाला अमावास्यायां चाच्युतौ भवतीत्यत्र वाक्यभेदभिया आग्नेयादिशब्देन न गुणविधिः किन्तु कर्मनामेति बहिः पूर्वपक्षे सिध्यान्तमाह तद्गुणा इति। तच्च कर्म च देवताद्रव्याष्टाकपालरूपा गुणाश्च विधीयरन्। विधानार्थे विधीयमानेऽर्थे यागे उक्तगुणेषु अविभागात् क्रिया एव विधेया इति नियामकाभावात्। अन्येन अन्यवाक्येन शिष्टा विहिता न चेत् सर्वेऽप्यपूर्वा अतो नियमासम्भवः। न च वाक्यभेदः। गुणविशिष्टक्रियाविधिसम्भवादिति भावः।

 This principle of विशिष्टविधान is finely summarised in the couplet as follows (of which the line under discussion forms the second line:—

**प्राप्ते कर्मणि नानेको विधातुं शक्यते गुणः।  
अप्राप्तत्वे विधीयन्ते बहवोऽप्येकयत्तः॥**

 such a विशिष्टविधान is well explained by अर्थसङ्ग्रह as—

यत्र तूभयमप्राप्तं तत्र विशिष्टं विधत्ते यथा "सोमेन यजेते" त्यत्र सोमयागयोरप्राप्तत्वात्सोमविशिष्टयागविधानम्। सोमपदे मत्वर्थलक्षणया सोमवता यागेनेष्टं भावयेदिति वाक्यबोधः। न चोभयविधाने वाक्यभेदः प्रत्येकमुभयस्याविधानात् किन्तु विशिष्टस्यैव विधानात्।

 P. 13. ननु एवमपि efc.—This is the second objection to the assertion that सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति पाणिमन्त्रं पवित्रं enjoins the wearing of Linga on the body. The objecter says that सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति enjoins the installation of Linga on the ground i. e. an altar in a temple; because the word स्थापयति prominently and strikingly expresses the स्थापन of a Linga on an altar. Moreover the striking and prominent sense is corroborated by the very common custom of the people of installing Lingas in temples. Hence स्थापन cannot be taken to mean the placing of Linga on the body.

 P. 13. पाणिना अभिमन्त्रितं षडध्वशोधनादिना संस्कृतं—This is the explanation of the word पाणिमन्त्रं so as to suit the explanation from the objecter's standpoint.

 P. 13. षडध्यशोधनादिनासंस्कृतम्—षडध्व or the six अध्वन्ऽ, according to Shiva philosophy are, मन्त्राध्व, पदाध्व, वर्णाध्व, भुवनाध्व, तत्वाध्व, and कलाध्व. These are the कार्याऽ of the कारण, शिव and his शक्ति. Being कार्य and being affected by मलत्रय they are अशुद्ध. Their शुद्धिकरण consists in making them free from मलत्रय. शुद्धीकरण of these is treated in शिवपुराण (see, for instance, वायुसंहिता). But this is treated in शारदातिलक (pp. 181-182, 183) briefly as follows:—

**शोधनं नाम तत्वानां कारणैकत्वचिन्तनम्।  
वर्णादीनां कलानां च तस्यां विद्वैकचिन्तनम्॥  
मन्त्राध्वा च कलाध्वा च वर्णाध्वा चेति शब्दतः।  
भुवनाध्वा च तत्त्वाध्वा कलाध्वा चार्थतः क्रमात्॥**

 आणवकर्ममार्यायमलरहित्वं शुद्धत्वम्। तत्र आणवो नाम सदाशिवस्य स्वस्यानवमर्शः। कार्मो नाम पुण्यपापवानहमिति प्रतीतिः। मयीयो महदहङ्कारादौ आत्मबुद्धिः। उक्तं च वाययीवेः—

**शिवः शक्तिस्ततो नादः तस्माद्विन्दुः सदाशिवः।  
तस्मान्महेश्वरो जातः शुध्दा विद्या महेश्वरात्॥**

 कारणत्वेन मलत्रय राहित्यात् शुद्धत्वम्। कार्यरूपत्वेन तदुक्तत्वादशुद्धम्। शोधनप्रकारः। पदे कलाध्वानं स्मृत्वा पद्गुह्यवक्रशिरःसु स्वबीजादिकाः कलाः विन्यस्य पश्चात्कलाध्वविशोधनम्। एवं तत्वाध्वानं स्मृत्वा विलोमेषु पूर्वस्थानेषु तान्विन्यस्य पञ्चाशप्तत्वाध्वशोधनम्। एवं भुवनाध्वानं नाभौ स्मृत्वा अनन्तस्थानेषु स्वबीजाधानान् विन्यस्य पश्चात्तच्छोदनम्। एवं हृदि वर्णाध्वानं संस्मृत्य शुद्धान्वर्णान् तद्देशे विन्यस्व पश्चात् वर्णाध्वानशोधनम्। एवं मूर्धनि मन्त्राध्यानं संस्मृत्य सप्तमन्त्रान् तत्तत्स्थानेषु व्याप्य पश्चात्तत्तदध्वशोधनम्।

 P. 13. अत्रवदन्ति—This is refutation of the foregoing objection by others. The author's refutation of the objection begins on the next page with अत्र वूमः etc. It is not possible to understand to whom the author refers and whose refutation he reproduces here.

 P. 13. सन्दिग्धे तु वाक्यशेषात्—This न्याय or principle is established in पूर्वमीमांसा I-4-19, सूत्र 29. The सूत्र means in doubtful meanings by what follows रामेश्वरसूरि explains this सूत्र in his commentary as follows:—

 वाक्यशेषेण सन्दिग्धार्थनिरूपणम्। यथा अक्ताः शर्कराः उपदधातीत्यत्र येन केनचिदञ्जनमुत घृतेनैवेति संशये नियामकशास्त्राभावेन केनाप्यञ्जनमिति वहिः पूर्वपक्षे सिद्धान्तमाह। सन्दिग्ध इति। सन्दिग्धेषु सन्देहविषयभूतेषु विध्यर्थेषु सत्सु वाक्यशेषात् अर्थवादात् "तेजो वै घृतं स तैजसमेवाग्निं चिनुत" इत्यर्थवादेन घृतस्तुत्या विधेयार्थेऽपि घृतमेव सिध्यति। अर्थवादेन विधेयार्थस्यैव स्तुतेरितिभावः। This principle is applied here and the सिद्धान्त vindicated. The refutation is that स्थापन might mean installation of Linga on the ground as much as on the body. The doubt, whether the स्थापन means the installation on the body or an altar, is cleared by what follows, namely, मनन (contained in मन्त्र). If the word मनन means पूजन, as it always does and as is popularly accepted, and if that मनन or पूजन be restricted to पाणि (in पाणि मन्त्रं), स्थापन means the placing of Linga on the hand, i. e. on the body.

 P. 13. नच पाणिना मननात्रायते इति—Here the objecter accepts the meaning of the word मनन as पूजन but wants to justify his objection by explaining पाणिमन्त्रं as पाणिना मननात् पूजनात् त्रायते इति पाणिमन्त्रम् ।

 P. 13. तथा सति etc.—This is the refutation of the foregoing explanation of the objector. Here the सिद्धान्तिन् proves that पाणिना मननात् त्रायते इति पाणिमन्त्रं is not tenable. Such a dissolution of the compound is objectionable grammatically, because it voilates the rule of grammar समर्थः पदविधि (पदसम्बन्धी यः विधिः स समर्थाश्रितः बोध्यः), which means—the rule relating to inflected words should be understood to apply to such words as are connected in sense. But in the dissolution of the compound पाणिना is connected with मनन and and not in any way with त्रायते. मनन stands in need of पाणिना on the one hand and is alone connected with त्रायते on the other. Hence it is defective.

 P. 13. सापेक्षत्वेऽपिगमकत्वात्तथाङ्गीक्रियते—The objecter might defend his position by relying on such compounds as देवदत्तस्य गुरुकुलं and चैत्रस्य दासभार्या, where the words गुरु and दास are dependant on देवदत्त and चैत्र. Still they are compounded with कुल and भार्या respectively, because in spite of their dependance, the sense of the compounds is clear. In the same way, the objecter, might say, पाणिमन्त्रं may be a compounded in spite of the dependance of मनन on पाणि. गमकत्वात्=विग्रहबोध्यार्थबोधनक्षमत्वात्.

 The whole thing has been clearly explained by पण्डित शिवकुमार as follows:—पाणिना मननात् त्रायते इति विग्रहेऽपि मननशब्दार्थस्य पाण्यंशे सापेक्षतया असामर्थ्यात् सुप्सुपेति समासो न भवेदित्याशयः। गमकत्वादिति देवदत्तस्य गुरुकुलं चैत्रस्य दासभार्या इत्यादौ गुर्वादिपदार्थस्य देवदत्ताद्यंशे सापेक्षत्वेऽपि विग्रहबोध्यार्थबोधनक्षमत्वात् समासः स्वीक्रियते। यथोक्तं भर्तृहरिणा—

**सम्बन्धिशब्दः सापेक्षो नित्यं सर्वः समस्यते।  
वाक्यवत्सा व्यपेक्षा हि वृत्तावपि न हीयते॥**

इति। अथवा नित्यसापेक्षस्थले पूर्वपदार्थघटितसम्बन्धेन समुदायेन सहैव देवदत्तादिपदार्थस्यान्वयः प्रथमं विवक्षितः। अवयवार्थान्वयबोधस्तु फलतः सिध्यति। अयं प्रकारोऽप्युक्तः तेनैव यथा—

**समुदायेन सम्बन्धो येषां गुरुकुलादिना।  
संस्पृश्यावयवांस्तेतु युज्यन्ते तद्वता सह॥**

इति एवमिहापि मननस्य क्रियारूपतया कारकाशे नित्यसापेक्षत्वात् निष्प्रत्यूहः समासो भविष्यतीति आशङ्क्य निराकरोति।

 P. 13. चेन्न पाणिपदवैयर्थ्यात्—This is the refutation of the objecter's justification of पणिमन्त्रं as सापेक्षत्वेऽपि समासः। The सिद्धान्तिन् says that such a justification in a round about way is no good; because in such a case the word पाणि is unnecessary and can be well dispensed with, in as much as पाणि as the करण of पूजन is at once implied and requires no special mention. पाणि thus becomes superfluous.

 P. 13. नच मननात्......वाच्यम्—Here the objecter comes forward again and justifies his position by holding that the mention of पाणि is significant in order to restrict the पूजन to पाणि only as करण. There is thus, the objector says, नियमविधि. But the सिद्धान्तिन् says no to this.

 P. 14. तेषामपिकरणत्वप्राप्तेः—This is the refutation of the foregoing explanation of the objecter. The सिद्धान्तिन् says that the means of worship cannot be so restricted to hand only; because on the authority of सिद्धान्तशिखामणि, मनः, वाक्, etc. are all as much the means of worship as the hand.

 P. 14. किं च तृतीयासमासाश्रयणे एकदेशान्वयापत्तीः—Herein the सिद्धान्तिन् points out that according to the objecter's standpoint "hand" alone would be the means of worship i. e. एकदेशान्वयापत्ति. Such a restriction would be undesirable in contradiction to the authority contained in शिवपूजादिकं etc.

 P. 14. नच सप्तमीतत्पुरुषाश्रयणेऽपि etc.—Here the सिद्धान्तिन् anticipates that the objecter might say that even when the word पाणिमन्त्रं was dissolved as पाणौ मन्त्रं, the same would be the contingency, namely, that पूजन should be performed on hand only and no where else as in mind etc. But such objection, says the सिद्धान्तिन्, is quite untenable, because the worship of Linga placed on (the left) hand might be done not only by the (right) hand but by मनः, वाक् etc. The worship of Linga, though restricted to the particular अधिकरण or आधार, can be performed by all means of worship, पाणि, मनः, वाक् etc. Thus Linga, the object of worship; become पाण्यधिकरणविशिष्ट. But पाणि, मनः, वाक् etc. are all means of worship.

 P. 14. यच्चोक्तं etc.—This is the refutation of the meaning given to the word मनन as अभिमन्त्रण by the objecter. The सिद्धान्तिन् says that मुख्यार्थ of मनन is पूजन and अभिमन्त्रण is only its secondary or figurative sense. And whenever मुख्यार्थ is possible figurative is unacceptable, गौणार्थ is to be accepted only when मुख्यार्थ is inapplicable and गौणार्थ unavoidable. This preferabilty of मुख्यार्थ to गौणार्थ has been established, says the सिद्धान्तिन्, in ब्रह्मसूत्राऽ in I-1-5 in सूत्र—गोणश्चेन्न आत्मशब्दात्। There the objecter maintained that ईक्षण could be figuratively attributed to the inanimate प्रधान, in as much as ईक्षण was so attributed to आपः, तेजः, etc. in some passages. This view of the objecter was refuted and it was established that ईक्षण in its primary sense was better attributed to animate आत्मन् and not to प्रधान figuratively.

 P. 14. अत्र ब्रूमः etc.—Here begins the refutation of the second objection by the author himself. Here the author says that स्थापन means the स्थापन of the Linga on the body. When the उपक्रम and the उपसंहार, the beginning and the end of the passage, are to be consistently explained. The

whole passage is—निधनपतये नमः। निधनपतान्तिकाय नमः। ऊर्ध्वायनमः। ऊर्ध्वलिङ्गाय नमः। हिरण्याय नमः। हिरण्यलिङ्गाय नमः। सुवर्णाय नमः। सुवर्णलिङ्गाय नमः। दिव्याय नमः। दिव्यलिङ्गाय नमः। भवाय नमः। भवलिङ्गाय नमः। शर्वाय नमः। शर्वलिङ्गाय नमः। शिवाय नमः। शिवलिङ्गाय नमः। ज्वलाय नमः। ज्वललिङ्गाय नमः। आत्माय नमः। आत्मलिङ्गाय नमः। परमाय नमः। परमलिङ्गाय नमः। एतत्सोमस्य सूर्यस्य सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयति। In this passage the ten Lingas, namely, अखण्ड लिङ्ग or परब्रह्मन, His first three modifications, भावलिङ्ग, प्राणलिङ्ग, इष्टलिङ्ग, and the six further modifications, महालिङ्ग, प्रसादलिङ्ग, चरलिङ्ग, शिवलिङ्ग, गुरुलिङ्ग, and आचारलिङ्ग, are praised by अर्थवाद; and at last it is enjoined that सर्वलिङ्ग (सर्वप्रकारकं लिङ्गं) स्थापयति—Linga in all its forms should be installed. In as much as the Lingas praised in the beginning of the passage are those, that occupy the different चक्राऽ of the body and are to be worshipped by the devotee to attain सायुज्य with परमशिव, it necessarily follows that स्थापन refers to the षट्स्थललिङ्गाऽ and not the स्थावरलिङ्ग. The ten Lingas praised in the passage are identified with षट्स्थललिङ्गा as follows:—

\(1\) ऊर्ध्वलिङ्ग is अखण्डलिङ्ग Or परमशिव. This is परतत्व.  
(2) आत्मलिङ्ग is भावलिङ्ग or ideal Linga.  
(3) हिरण्यलिङ्ग is प्राणलिङ्ग or vital Linga.  
(4) सुवर्णलिङ्ग is इष्टलिङ्ग or gross or physical Linga.  
(5) परमलिङ्ग is महालिङ्ग, the first modification of भावलिङ्ग.  
(6) दिव्यलिङ्ग is प्रसादलिङ्ग, the second modification of भावलिङ्ग.  
(7) ज्वललिङ्ग isचर orजङ्गमलिङ्ग, the first modification of प्राणलिङ्ग.  
(8) शिवलिङ्ग is शिवलिङ्ग, the second modification of प्राणलिङ्ग  
(9) भावलिङ्ग is गुरुलिङ्ग, the first modification of इष्टलिङ्ग,  
(10) शर्वलिङ्ग is आचारलिङ्ग, the second modification of इष्टलिङ्ग.

 The author quotes authorities in support of the identification of the various Lingas, excepting the अखण्डलिङ्ग. But

the following श्लोक from योगराजोपनिषत् may be noted in support of this Linga, namely,

**नवमं व्योमचक्रं स्यादश्रैः षोडषभिर्युतम्।  
संविद्व्रूयाच्च तन्मध्ये शक्तिरुद्धा स्थिता परा॥**

 The whole passage निधनपतये नमः......सर्वलिङ्ग स्थापयति पाणिमन्त्र पवित्रं has been explained in श्रुतिसारभाष्य by शिवलिङ्गशिवयोगिन् as follows:—अस्यार्थः। निधनपतये निर्गतं धनं जीवितं यस्मात्तस्य निधनस्य मरणस्य पतये स्वामिने कालरूपाय नमः। नमस्कारोऽस्तु। निधनपतान्तिकाय निधने मरणे पतानां पतितानाम् अन्तिकाय समीपभूताय रक्षास्वरूपाय नमः। उर्ध्वाय षट्त्रिंशत्तत्वानामुपरिभूताय नमः। ऊर्ध्वलिङ्गाय तस्मादुपरिभूतशून्यलिङ्गस्वरूपाय नमः। हिरण्याय नमः। हेमस्वरूपाय नमः। हिरण्यलिङ्गाय हेममयलिङ्गाय नमः। सुवर्णाय शोभनवर्णवते नमः। सुवर्णलिङ्गाय शोभनवर्णनामाङ्कनभूताय नमः। दिव्याय दिवि प्रकाशे भवम् उत्पन्नं तस्मै तेजोरूपाय नमः। दिव्यलिङ्गाय दिक्षु प्रकाशवतामाधारभूताय नमः। भवाय उत्पत्तेः कर्त्रे नमः। भवलिङ्गाय उत्पत्तिमतां प्राणरूपाय नमः। शर्वाय शृणाति हिनस्तीति शर्वः तस्मै हिंसकाय नमः। शर्वलिङ्गाय हिंसाधर्मवतामधिष्ठात्रे नमः। शिवाय शेते सज्जनानां मनसि अत्रेति शिवः तस्मै नमः। शिवलिङ्गाय मङ्गलानां चिन्हरूपाय नमः। ज्वलाय अग्निरूपाय नमः। ज्वललिङ्गाय अग्नेरन्तर्गताय नमः। आत्माय जीवान्तपरमनामाङ्कितस्वरूपाय नमः। आत्मलिङ्गाय जीवान्तर्गताय नमः। परमाय परैर्गुणैमीयते इति परमःतस्मै उत्कृष्टगुणराजिताय नमः। परमलिङ्गाय उत्कृष्टप्रमाणवतां ज्ञानस्वरूपाय नमः। सोमाय लीलामूर्तेः सूर्यस्य लीलाव्यतिरिक्तस्य शिवस्य सर्वलिङ्गं विश्वात्मकं पाणिमन्त्रं करकमलस्य पावनकारकं तथा चोक्तं—

**पाणौ च मननात्त्रातुं लिङ्गं सर्वशिलामयम्।  
संस्थापयेत्सदा देहे पवित्रमुपवीतवत्॥**

 पवित्रं पावनस्वरूपलिङ्गम् एतत् इदं जगत्कर्तृ स्थापयति प्रतिष्ठाप्य पूजयति। ष्ठा गतिनिवृत्ताविति धातोः। अत्र जम्बुकेश्वरं मत्स्यकेश्वरं पतङ्गेश्वरं कर्मेश्वरं नरसिंहेश्वरम् इत्यादितत्तन्नाम्ना पूजनीयं लिङ्गमेव।. (Every time नमः was followed by नमस्कारोऽस्तु; that has been omitted to avoid tediousness). But it is to be noted that here there is no indication of what लिङ्ग is to be identified with what modified लिङ्ग form of परशिव, as is done by the author. It is to be also noted that the

explanation of पवित्र and पाणिमन्त्रं, as पावनस्वरूपलिङ्गं and करकमलस्य पावनकारकं respectively, differs from that of the author's.

 This passage has been explained by बसवपण्डिताराध्य, the commentator of नारायणोपनिषत्, similarly. And the different लिङ्गाऽ are identified with षट्स्थल लिङ्गाऽ, as the author does. But it is unnecessary to reproduce it here.

 P. 16. विध्यर्थवादयोर्वैय्यधिकरण्यापत्तिः—If it is not admitted that स्थापन is that of the Linga on the body, then there would result disagreement between the अर्थवाद and the final विधि of स्थापन, if this स्थापन were to mean the स्थापन of the Linga on the ground.

 P. 16. किञ्च, उपक्रमोपसंहारौ etc.—When something is to be determined as to what it is or when it is to be determined that a thing refers to a particular thing, it can be so done by the six textual indications, which are (1) उपक्रम and उपसंहार (2) अभ्यास (8) अपूर्वता (4) फल (5) अर्थवाद (6) उपपत्ति. These six have been made clear on page 28 of सिद्धान्तलेशसङ्ग्रह as follows:—

**उपक्रमोपसंहारौ अभ्यासोऽपूर्वता फलम्।  
अर्थवादोपपत्ती च लिङ्गं तात्पर्यनिर्णये॥**

 तत्र—छान्दोग्यषष्ठे सदेव सोम्य इत्यद्वितीयं ब्रह्मोपक्रम्य एतदात्म्यमिदं सर्वम् इत्युपसंहारात्तत्रत्यसकलसन्दर्भस्याद्वितीयब्रह्मपरत्वं निर्णीयते इति आद्यमुपक्रपोसंहाररूपं तात्पर्यलिङ्गम्।तत्त्वमसि इत्यादेर्नवकृत्वोऽभ्यासदर्शनात् अद्वितीयपरत्वानर्णायकं द्वितीयमभ्यासरूपम्। यं वै सोम्यैतमणिमानं न निभालयसे इत्यादिना अद्वितीयब्रह्मणो मानान्तरायोग्यत्वकथनादपूर्वत्वरूपं तृतीयम्। तस्य तावदेव चिरं यावन्न विमोक्ष्ये अथ सम्पत्त्ये इत्यनेन विदुषो विदेहकैवल्यरूपं फलं चतुर्थ तात्पर्य लिङ्ग। अनेन। जीवेनात्मनानुप्रविश्य इत्यादिना अद्वितीयज्ञानार्थो अर्थवादः पञ्चमम्। एकेन मृत्पिण्डेन इत्यादिदृष्टान्तेनोपत्तिरूपषष्टमिति षट् लिङ्गानि भवन्ति। एभिः सर्वत तात्पर्यनिर्णयो भवति।

 The author applies here the first तात्पर्यलिङ्ग to the matter under discussion and concludes that it is the इष्टलिङ्ग that is enjoined and not a स्थावरलिङ्ग. But उपक्रम is strong only when it is not contradicted or when there is nothing that goes against it. This principle of the first तात्पर्यलिङ्ग is discussed in जैमिनि III-3-1, to which the author refers in words वेदो वा प्रायदर्शनात् इति वेदोपक्रमाधिकरणन्यायेन etc. The conclusion of the अधिकरण, referred to above, is briefly stated by रामेश्वरसूरि as असञ्जातविरोधित्वेन प्रबलेन उपक्रमानुसारेण उपसंहारनियमनम्। माधवाचार्य also makes the same statement in words—असञ्जातविरोधित्वेन प्रबलमुपक्रममनुसृत्य तद्वशेनोपसंहारस्य नेतव्यत्वात्।

 P. 16. नच "एतत्सोमस्य सूर्यस्य" etc.—This is another objection to the interpretation of the passage as laying down the wearing or स्थापन of लिङ्ग on the body. The objection is that the salutations are addressed to सोम and सूर्य, the moon and the sun, on account of the pronoun एतत्, which obviously refers to नमस्क्रिया. The interpretation, therefore, of the hymn, as addressing the salutations to the Lingas and finally laying down the wearing of Linga on the body, is unreasonable. The objection is removed by the author by interpreting सोम to mean शिव (उमया सहितः) and सूर्य to mean सूर्यमण्डलातर्वर्तिशिव. This secondary interpretation of सोम and सूर्य, says the author, is unavoidable, because the primary sense of सोम and सूर्य is quite inappropriate and goes against the context (सन्दर्भविरोधापत्या), ( and also because सोम and सूर्य are never praised as Lingas anywhere). In this connection it may be noted that मरितोण्टदार्य also remarks in his वीरशैब्रानन्दचन्द्रिका, page 83, as follows:—सर्वलिङ्गं स्थापयतीति सोमसूर्यस्वरूपशक्तिशिवात्मकं लिङ्गं स्थापयतीति लिङ्गस्य धारणं विधाय etc. etc.

 P. 16 औदुम्बरो यूपो भवति etc.—This is an instance of how unity or agreement has been established between विधि and अर्थवाद. The author produces it here in order to

strengthen his own position, as maintained by him in the foregoing. In the hymn the sentence औदुम्बरो यूपो भवति contains the विधि, according to सिद्धान्तिन्, that the sacrificial post should made of उदुम्बर tree; and the rest is the अर्थवाद or praise of the tree. The objecter comes forward and says that it is not अर्थवाद but the statement of fruit to be attained by the post being made of the tree, because of the word अवरुध्यै in the dative case, which expresses for the sake of (the fruit, ऊर्ज्). The सिद्धान्तिन् refutes this by saying that unless it is actually laid down that the sacrificial post should be made of उदुम्बर tree, no फल can be expected. But the verb not being in the imperative or potential mood, no विधि can be said to be laid down.The objecter may say, if it was not laid down actually, it might be inferred to be laid down. But the inference can be made, says the सिद्धान्तिन्, only from the अर्थवाद of the उदुम्बर tree. Hence औदुम्बरा यूपो भवति is the विधि and the rest is अर्थवाद. The whole thing is lucidly discussed by माधवाचार्य in his जैमिनिन्यायमालाविस्तर in I–2–2 as follows:—

 इदमान्मायते—"औदुम्बरो यूपो भवति, ऊर्ग् वा उदुम्बरः, ऊर्क् पशवः, ऊर्जैवास्मा उर्जं पशून् प्राप्नोत्ति, ऊर्जो अवरुध्यै" इति। अमृतशद्बाभिधेयः अत्यंसारभूतः सूक्ष्मोऽन्नरस ऊर्गुच्येत। उदुम्बररूपयोर्जा यजमानार्थमध्वर्युः पशुरूपामूर्जमाप्नोति ततो यूपस्यौदुम्बरत्वमूर्जः सम्पादनाय भवतीत्यर्थः। अत्र अवरुध्यै इति तादर्थ्ये चतुर्थी। तया फलत्वं गम्यते। धनलाभाय राजसेवा इत्यादौ तद्दर्शनात्। नच फलपरस्य वचनस्य स्तावकत्वं युज्यते। अन्यथा स्वर्गकामः इत्यत्र स्वर्गस्यापि ज्योतिष्टोम स्तावकत्वप्रसङ्गात् इति प्राप्ते ब्रूमः अयमूर्जो अवरोधः कस्य फलं स्यात् किमविहितस्यौदुम्बरत्वस्य, उत विहितस्य। नाद्यः। अनुष्ठानमन्तरेण द्रव्यमात्रात्फलानुत्पत्तेः। द्वितीये किमत्र विधिः प्रत्यक्षः उत उन्नेयः। नाद्यः औदुम्बरो यूपो भवतीत्यत्र लिङ्प्रत्ययाश्रवणात्। द्वितीये स्तुत्या स उन्नेयः। न चात्र स्तुतिमङ्गीकरोषि। अथोच्येत विधानायौदुम्बरत्वं स्तूयते तत्फलं चावबोध्यते इति। तर्हि वाक्यं भिद्येत। ततः फलविधिवन्निगद्यमानमप्येतद्वाक्यं स्तावकमेव।

 P. 17. अथैवमप्यस्य etc—This is an objection that लिङ्गधारण is enjoined on those that seek मोक्ष and not on all, because सिद्धान्तशिखामणि says clearly—मोक्षकाङ्क्षिभिः मुनिभिः बाद्र्यमाभ्यन्तरं लिगन्धारणं कर्तव्यम्।. The author refutes this by saying that such an objection is untenable, in as much as सन्ध्योपासना would also be restricted to those only that seek सन्ध्योपासना ब्रह्मलोक and not general. But the restriction will go against the general custom of सन्ध्या being practised by all. This is merely a technical objection and not that all do not or should not seek मोक्ष.

 P. 17.अनुपास्य etc.—Here the objecter comes forward to prove that सन्ध्योपासना is laid down in the negative form in that the non-performance of सन्ध्यावन्दन is productive of degradation. The meets this by saying that his position is similarly sound, because failure to wear Linga also leads to degradation. The verse—भक्तस्याविभ्रतो लिङ्गं etc. is taken from वीरमाहेश्वराचारसङ्ग्रह Vol. I, page 90.

 P. 17. आर्थवादिकफलश्रवणेन—The fruit of सन्ध्यावन्दन is not direct but is gathered through अर्थवाद or praise.

 P. 17. बिभेत्यल्पश्रुताद्वेदो etc.—These verses are taken from महाभारत, as noted by शिवकुमार, who remarks—वेदार्थमुपबृंहयेदित्यादिभिर्भारतोक्तिभिः सर्वत्र वेदार्थसंशये इतिहारपुराणसहायेननिर्णयकरणस्यौचित्यात्। It is thought that इतिहासाऽ and पुराणाऽ only reiterate what the Vedas say; they, therefore, elucidate the doubts that may arise regarding the meaning of Vedic sentences, which are many times cryptic in their sense. शिवपुराण agrees with महाभारत and similarly says—

**या विद्याच्चतुरो वेदान् साङ्गोपनिषदः द्विजः।  
न चेत्पुराणं संविद्यात् नैव स स्याद्विचक्षणः॥**

**इतिहासपुराणाभ्यां वेदं समुपबृंहयेत्।  
बिभेत्यल्पश्रुताद्वेदो मामयं प्रतरिष्यति॥**

 But वायुपुराण and मत्स्यपुराण go to the length of saying that ब्रह्मा first cogitated on पुराणाऽ and then the Vedas were delivered by him, in words—

**प्रथमं सर्वशास्त्राणां पुराणं ब्रह्मणा स्मृतम्।  
अनन्तरं च वक्येभ्यो वेदास्तस्य विनिःसृताः॥**

 The following may also be noted about the importance of पुराण and इतिहास—

 (a) नाम वा ऋग्वेदो यजुर्वेदः सामवेद आथर्वणश्चतुर्थम् इतिहासपुराणः पञ्चमो वेदानां वेदः पित्र्यो राशिः दैवो निधिर्शको वाक्यम् एकायनं दवविद्या ब्रह्मविद्या क्षत्रविद्या नक्षत्रविद्या सर्वदेवयजनविद्या नामैवैतन्नामापास्स्वेति।

(छान्दाग्योपनिषत् VII 1-3).

 (b) सोऽब्रवीत्। यो मामितिहासं ग्राहयेत् वरमस्मै दद्यामिति। ततो ब्राह्मणः संयोगं संयुयुजे। तमादित्यात्पुरुषो भास्करवर्णो निष्क्रम्य स एनं ग्राहयाञ्चकार। तमपृच्छत्। कोऽसीति वा वृषादर्विरिति। तस्माद्य इममितिहसमाधीते। आदित्यलोके स कामचारो भवति। तस्माद्य इममितिहासमुपनीतो माणवको गृण्हीयात्। गृहीत्वाऽथ ब्राह्मणाच्छ्रावयत्। मेधावी भवत। वर्षशतं च जीवेत्। षडङ्ग च वेदमवाप्नुयात्।

(इतिहासोपनिषत्—Unpublished Upanishads, page 10)

 It will not be out of place here to note a few instances of how पुराणा reiterate the Vedas. The following will be the instances.

 (a) The whole of chapter III of केनोपनिषत् has been stated in शिवपुराण-वायुसंहिता as follows:—

**पुराकिल सुराः सेन्द्रा विवदन्तः परस्परम्।  
असुरान् समरे जित्वा जेताहमहमित्युत॥  
तदा महेश्वरस्तेषां मध्यतोऽवरवेषधृक्।  
स्वलक्षणैर्विहीनाङ्गः स्वयं यक्ष इवाभवत्॥  
स तानाह सुरानेकं तृणमादाय भूतले।**

**य एतद्विकृतं कर्तुं क्षमेत स तु दैत्यजित्॥  
यक्षस्य वचनं श्रुत्वा वज्रपाणिः शचीपतिः।  
किञ्चित्क्रुद्धो विहस्यैनं तृणमादातुमुद्यतः॥  
न च तत्तृणमादातुं मनसापि न शक्यते।  
यथा तथापि तच्छेत्तुं वज्रं वज्रधरोऽसृजत्॥  
तद्वज्रं निजवज्रेण संसृष्टमिव सर्वतः।  
तृणेनाभिहतं तेन तिर्यगग्रं पपात ह॥**

** X X X**

**एवं देवैः समारब्धं तृणमुद्दिश्य यत्नतः।  
व्यर्थमासीदहो कृष्ण यक्षस्याज्ञाबलेन वै॥**

** X X X**

**तदन्तरे हैमवती देवी दिव्यशुचिस्मिता।  
आविरासीन्नभोमध्ये शोभमाना शुचिस्मिता॥ etc. etc.**

 (b) The same has been repeated in ब्रह्मगीता 4th अध्यायः as—

**अविज्ञाय महादेववैभवं परिमोहिताः।  
वयं विजयमापन्नाः असुराश्च पराजिताः॥ etc. etc.**

 (c) The following words of तैत्तिरीयोपनिषत्–भृगुवल्ली are stated in मानवपुराण; the words are:—

 यतो वा इमानि भूतानि जायन्ते येन जातानि जीवन्ति यत्प्रत्यभिसंविशन्ति तद्ब्रह्मेति विजिज्ञासस्व।

Says मानवपुराण—

**तथा विज्ञायमानं च ब्रह्मेति महदादरात्।  
ततस्तल्लक्षणं प्राह श्रुतिस्तस्य महात्मनः॥  
यस्मादिमानि भूतानि जायन्ते येन तानि च।  
जीवन्ति यत्र लीयन्ते तद्ब्रह्मेत्यादरेण च॥**

 (d) The words of जाबालोपनिषत्—य एषोऽनन्तोऽव्यक्त आत्मा सोऽविमुक्ते प्रतिष्ठित इति।...उत्तमं चास्य स्थानं भवति भ्रुवोर्घ्राणस्य च मध्ये यः।—are repeated in सूतसंहिता as follows:—

**वाराणसी महाप्राज्ञ भ्रुवोर्घ्राणस्य मध्यमे।  
भ्रुवोर्घ्राणस्य या सन्धिरिति जाबालिकी श्रुतिः॥ **

मानवपुराण also says:—

**भ्रुवोर्घ्राणस्य या सन्धिस्तत्र वाराणसी स्थिता।  
तत्राविमुक्तकं क्षेत्रं तस्मिन्रुद्रः सदा स्थितः॥**

 (e) The first मन्त्र of ऋग्वेद—अग्निमीळे पुरोहितं etc. is repeated in कारणागम as follows:—

**योऽग्निः स भस्म न परो अग्निमीळे पुरोहितम्।  
रुग्दाहाकाशगः शम्भुः योग्रजाग्निरिति श्रुतिः॥**

 (f) तस्य भासा सर्वमिदं विभाति is stated in सिद्धान्तशिखामणि as—

**यद्भासा भासते विश्वं यत्सुखेनानुमोदते।  
नमस्तमै गुणतीतविभवाय परात्मने॥**

 Instances like these can be given ad infinitum. But this does not mean that पुराणाऽ do not contradict the Vedas. They often do so and therefore जैमिनि lays down the rule that विरोधे त्वनपेक्षं स्यात्। असति ह्यनुमानम्. Many times they distort and overstate.

 The author, therefore, quotes from पुराणाऽ in support of his position here as well as elsewhere.

 P. 19. नन्वशुचिकालेऽपि etc.—This is an objection raised against the statement made in previous verses that there is no सूतक or आशौच. The objecter says that if लिङ्गपूजा is to be performed during the times of impurity, why not all other religious rites? The author repudiates this objection by saying that लिङ्गधारण and लिङ्गपूजा are not only unobjectionable but imperative even at times of impurity, like मन्त्रोच्चारण, मन्त्रपाठ and वेदपाठ, while सन्ध्यावन्दन, वैश्वदेव, and दर्शपूर्णमासेष्टि are performed even during the times of impurity, in as much as they are नित्य and are to be performed daily irrespective of आशौच. The author quotes from मनुस्मृति II-106, in support of his standpoint and stultifies the objecter.

 But it is not possible to understand what the author means by, कर्मान्तरानुष्ठान as, according to the Lingayat Religion, there are no religious rites नैमित्तिक or काम्य, except those prescrided in relation to अष्टावरण and षट्स्थल. But they being नित्य are always imperative. From this suspicion arises that the author was an आराध्य, who stands midway between the Lingayata and the Bramhin of Hiuduism and who observes some of the Bramhanic rites, in addition to those of Lingayatism Moreover, when Lingayatas or Veerashaivas have not to observe आशौच or पञ्चसूतकाऽ, so meticulously observed by Hindus, there should arise no question of अशुचिकाल for कर्मातरानुष्ठान, if any.

 P. 20. नन्वास्ताम् etc.—This is an objection against the use of the left hand, on which लिङ्गार्चन is performed. The objecter means that the left hand is employed for doing the dirty work of the body and is thus unfit lor the holy work of लिङ्गपूजा. The author removes the objection by arguing that the left hand is used in conjunction with the right hand for (1) पान or holy drink (2) for holding the full pot to make an offering to the holy fire (3) for साष्टाङ्गप्रणाम, (4) and for पुष्पाञ्जलिप्रदान. If the use of the left hand is unexceptionable for these holy rites, says the author, why should it be considered unfit for लिङ्गपूजा?

 The verse—उरसा शिरसा दृष्ट्या etc. occurs in लिङ्गपुराण with a slight variation as follows:—

**उरसा शिरसा दृष्ट्या मनसा श्रद्धया गिरा।  
पद्भ्याम् उभाभ्यां बाहुभ्यां प्रणामोऽष्टाङ्ग उच्यते॥**

 According to बौद्धायन, therefore, the अष्टाङ्गाऽ are (1) उरस् (2) शिरस् (3) दृष्टि (4) मनस् (5) वचस् (6) पादौ (7) करौ and (8) कर्णौ. But according to Lingapurana they are (1) उरस्

\(2\) शिरस् (3) दृष्टि (4) मनस् (5) गौर् or वचस् (6) पादौ (7) right arm (8) and the left arm. The latter seems to be correct, for there can be no use of ears for प्रणाम.

 P. 20. ननु भवदुक्तरीत्या लिङ्गधारण etc—This is the last objection raised against the position of the सिद्धान्तिन्. The objection is that विद्यारण्य, the great and authoritative commentator of the Vedic literature, has not written his commentary on the passage, which, according to the सिद्धान्तिन्, lays down the wearing of Linga. If विद्यारण्य says the objecter, had interpreted the text agreeably to the सिद्धान्तिन् 's exposition, all he said would have been acceptable. But there being no such interpretation by विद्यारण्य, the सिद्धान्तिन् 's exposition is doubtful and unacceptable. The author argues against this that विद्यारण्य might have omitted the
passage uncommented on two considerations (1) That it was not useful for his purposes (2) That a different interpretation would be sinful. He argues further that (3) If all what is left uncommented by विद्यारण्य were unauthoriative, many things e g. दुर्गामन्त्र, given by the author, would be unacceptable (4) The passage has been interpreted by other equally authoritative exegetes like कालहस्तिदीक्षित, मञ्चण्णपण्डित. and बसवपण्डिताराध्य (5) and lastly that लिङ्गधारण has been laid down by बादरायण in the पुराणाs
(already quoted). And there can be no higher authority, विद्यारण्य included, than बादरायण. And that शिव, the परब्रह्मन्, has written the वेदाऽ and the आगमाऽ containing लिङ्गधारण, which, therefore, is irrefragable. So also लिङ्गधारण is laid downly the very authoritative works like सिद्धान्तशिखामणि.

 The author's line of reasoning as to why विद्यारण्य left, the passage uncommented is very fanciful, as it is impossible to divine the motives why he left the passage uncommented. It is at the same time not possible for any–

body to understand what purposes of विद्यारण्य would not be served (प्रकृतार्थेऽनुपयोगात्). The second reasoning that a different interpretation would be produtive of sin, is very strange. Because विद्यारण्य has obviously has not interpreted, agreeably to the author's exposition, other passages interpreted by the author in other sections, e.g. पवित्रं ते वितत ब्रह्मणस्पते etc. and अयं मे हस्तो भगवान् etc., as will be noted in the relevant sections. The author's reasoning, therefore, is unsound in this regard. As for other things, said by the author, are reasonable.

 In contrast with these remarks, it may be noted what reasonable explanation has been given by नञ्जण्णार्य, the author of वेदान्तसारवीरशैचिन्तामणि, as to why सूत्रकाराऽ like बोधायन and अपस्तम्ब have not said anything about लिङ्गधारण as follows:—ननु लिङ्गधारणं बोधायनापस्तम्बीयादिसूत्रकारैरनुक्तत्वादननुष्ठेयमिति चेदत्र ब्रूमः चतुर्वेदतात्पर्यसङ्ग्रहे—

**प्रत्यक्षवेदविदितं प्रथितं पुराण बोधायनेन मुनिना बहुशः प्रणीतम्।  
सर्वार्थदर्शनविधेरपि गृह्यकारैः यज्ञोपवीतमिव विस्मृतमर्चयन्ते॥**

 इति हरदत्ताचार्यवाक्यात् गृह्यकारैर्नानावेदागमपुराणेतिहासप्रसिद्धशिवार्चनं यज्ञोपवीतमिव विस्मृतं तद्वदेव लिङ्गधारणमपि तैर्विस्मृतमिति ज्ञानव्यम्।

 The editor has not been able to avail himself of the works of कालहस्तिदीक्षित and मञ्चण्णपण्डित referred to by the author. But बसवपण्डिताराध्या's exhaustive commentary on नारायणोपनिषत् is available. He has been referred to by the author previously and has written his commentary on पुरुषसूक्त and other उपनिषत्ऽ like तैत्तिरीय and others; and extracts from his commentary are quoted in different relations.

 P. 21. अत्रैवोपनिषदि मन्त्रान्तरेणापि तत्सिद्धिः (बहिर्लिङ्गधारणसिद्धिः):—Here the author continues the discussion on the creed and principle of wearing Linga on the body. He is not content with establishing the principle of wearing Linga, on the strength of the solitary text. He, therefore, proceeds to interpret different texts of the same and other Upanishads and Vedas. The hymn "ॐ नमो ब्रह्मणे etc." is taken from the same उपनिषत् (नारायणोपनिषत्). It forms the ninth section of the उपनिषत्. It, therefore, precedes the hymn, already discussed and proved to lay down the wearing of Linga on the body. This hymn has been explained by वीरशैवानन्दचन्द्रिका, chapter III, as enjoining the wearing of Linga as follows:—
