अस्यार्थस्तु यत्प्रतियोगिताकसादृश्यवद्यत् श्रूयते तत्प्रतियोगिताकसादृश्यवत्तदपि गम्यते। अर्थादुपमेये उपमानस्य इव उपमानेऽप्युपमेयस्य सादृश्यं तुल्यवत्ता वेद्यतया प्रतीयते इति भावः।

 The author expresses the same thing in his own words as—भस्मनो बहिःशरीरधार्यत्वेनोपमानभूतशिवलिङ्गस्यापि तथात्वसिद्धेः।

 P. 47. एतत्समार्थक.....तत्सिद्धिः।—Here the author proves the भस्मनः बहिर्धारणत्व on the authority of the extract of कालाग्निरुद्रोपनिषत् as follows:—

 अथ सनत्कुमारः प्रमाणमस्य पप्रच्छ त्रिपुण्ड्रधारणस्य। त्रिधा रेखा आललाटादाचक्षुषोरामूर्ध्नोराभ्रुवोर्मध्यतश्च। या अस्य प्रथमा रेखा सा गार्हपत्यश्चाकारो रजः स्वात्मा क्रियाशक्तिर्ऋग्वेदः प्रातःसवनं माहेश्वरो देवतेति। या अस्य द्वितीया रेखा सा दक्षिणाग्निरुकारः सत्त्वमन्तरात्मा चेच्छाशक्तिर्यजुर्वेदो माध्यन्दिनं सवनं सदाशिवो देवतेति। या अस्य तृतीया रेखा सा आवहनीयो मकारस्तमः परमात्मा ज्ञानशक्तिः सामवेदस्तृतीयसवनं महादेवो देवतेति। त्रिपुण्ड्रविधिं भस्मना करोति यो विद्वान्......स सर्वान्वेदानधीतो भवति स सर्वान् देवान् ज्ञातो भवति......स सकलभोगान्भुङ्के देहं त्यक्त्वा शिवसायुज्यं प्राप्नोति।

 P. 47. इत्यादिना...कैमुतिकन्यायसिद्धम्। Here the author maintains that if the three stripes of भस्म applied on the forehead represent the three fires, the three constituent letters of the syllable ॐ, the three गुणाऽ of माया, the three Vedas (exclusive of अथर्ववेद), the threefold manifest deity aspects of परमशिव, the threefold power of शिवा's activity, the three सवनाऽ (purificatory ablutions), and if the wearing of the three–striped भस्म leads to शिवसायुज्य, how much more is लिङ्गधारण to be preferred and how much more will लिङ्गधारण be conducive to शिवसायुज्य? The importance of लिङ्गधारण as a means of मोक्ष is, therefore, beyond description. It is no wonder then that लिङ्गधारण (बाह्यलिङ्गधारण) should be laid down by the Vedas.

IX

 In this section the author proceeds to establish the principle of wearing Linga on the body from its being a means of मुक्ति or at–one–ment of अङ्ग, जीव or the individual soul, with लिङ्ग, the परब्रह्मन् or परशिव. This at–onement is called शिवजीवैक्य or लिङ्गाङ्गसामरस्य, which the author calls लिङ्गाङ्गसम्बन्ध. सामरस्य is a particular kind of सम्बन्ध or union, and may be called तादात्म्यसम्बन्ध; but there is difference between तादात्म्य and सामरस्य, which will be noted bereafter shortly. The two sentences establishing such a सम्बन्ध are लिङ्गाङ्गसम्बन्धबोधकवाक्यद्वय. The sentences that declare such oneness of the individual soul with the Highest soul or परब्रह्मन् are "तत्त्वमसि" and "अहं ब्रह्मास्मि". These are Upanishadic sentences, and all schools of Vedanta philosophy base their doctrine of मुक्ति on these two sentences and interpret them in accordance with their idea of मुक्ति or मोक्ष, the final beatitude. The Veerashaiva philosophers do the same in accordance with their doctrine or idea of मुक्ति or मोक्ष. The doctrine of मुक्ति of Veerashaivism is peculiar to itself and forms a distinctive and distinguishing feature of the religion. This doctrine लिङ्गाङ्गसामरस्य Or शिवजीवैक्य. सामरस्य is derived from समरस, the mixture of two separate quantitias of the same liquid. For instance a quantity of milk is divided into two separate quantities; and when the two separated quantities of milk are mixed there is सामरस्य of the two quantities. From the instance given it will be clear that for सामरस्य the mixture or union of two separate quantities of the same liquid the same thing is necessary; but two quantities of different liquids or things will not do. A quantity of milk may be mixed with a quantity of water and the mixture or union of milk and water may be seemingly indistinguisha–

ble but not really indistinguishable, i. e. there will be no complete at–one–ment of the two liquids. In such a mixture the quantity of water will lose itself into the quantity of milk and becomes one with milk. Here the nature of milk prevails and that of water is lost or destroyed. So also if a lump of salt is dissolved in water salt disappears and becomes one with water. Here is तादात्म्य but not सामरस्य. In the mixture of two different liquids or of some soluble salt with a liquid, there may be तादात्म्य but not सामरस्य, which is complete and indistinguishable at–one–ment. In the case of two different liquids, the nature of the liquid stronger and greater in quantity will prevail over the weaker and lesse in quantity. In the case of two different liquids of equal strength, the nature of that greater in quantity will prevail over that of the lesser in quantity: Hence in the mixture of two different liquids, one of the liquids has to lose its individuality and the mixture will be heterogeneous one. For really through and indistinguishable mixture or at–one–ment of liquids or things two separate quantities of the same liquid or thing is necessary. It is on this account that the author says on the next page नीरनीरसंयोगवत् अविभाज्यसंयोगः". So also the commentator of शिवयोगप्रदीपिका says क्षीरक्षीरसंयोगवत् सामरस्यम् (see 3rd पटल).

 From this analogy it will be clear that there will be सामरस्य of जीव or अङ्ग, the individual soul with शिव or लिङ्गं, the परब्रह्मन्, on account of their being innately one. It is the शक्ति of परब्रह्मन्, the wonderful capability or inscrutable activity, that causes the परब्रह्मन् to appear in the forms of of Lingas, the higher modifications of परशिव, and अङ्गाऽ, the lower modifications of परशिव or परब्रह्मन्. परब्रह्मन् or परशिव could not remain tranquill or content in His unmodified from

and desired out of sportiveness or to assume the manifest form of the Universe. It is from this sportive desire of परशिव, His tranquillity is disturbed and His शक्ति begins to work and causes all higher and lower modifications of परब्रह्मन् and the Universe to arises out of him. The higher modification is लिङ्ग or His उपास्य form and the lower modification is अङ्ग or His उपासक form. So it is said in शिवाद्वैतमञ्जरी "एवं च विमर्शपरिबृंहितं ब्रह्मैव स्वक्रीडार्थं लीलया शिवशक्तिसदाशिव.......भूमय इति षट्त्रिंशत्तत्त्वात्मना भिद्यते" and "एवंविधपरिपूर्णाहन्तात्मकपरमशिवस्य पूज्यपूजकलीलायां...ईषच्चलनमुत्पद्यते....न्यूनाधिकभावेन स द्विधा भिद्यते... तत्र... प्रविष्टमूलप्रणवरूपशिवचैतन्यमेव लिङ्गतत्त्वं भवति... तथैव न्यूनीभूतप्रकाशतायां प्रविष्टशिवचैतन्यमेव अङ्गतत्त्वं भवति."

 His शक्ति the also concurrently modifies herself along with परशिव correspondingly into कला and भक्ति. कला, the creative activity of परशिव goes or is associated with लिङ्ग and भक्ति, the reunifying activity, goes with or is associated with अङ्ग. The six modifications of Linga and the six corresponding modifications of Anga, associated with the corresponding modifications of शक्ति (as कला and भक्ति) form the basis of षट्स्थल philosophy, on which are built the spiritual or religious practices for the sake of the (Veerashaiva) devotee that he may seek reunion or at-one-ment with परब्रह्मन्. The at–one–ment of the individual soul with परब्रह्मन् is सामरस्य.

 भक्ति, which is only a form of शक्ति, enables the devotee to rise higher and higher gradually by stages by graded spiritual practices into ultimate at–one–ment with परशिव, शक्ति, the creative activity of परशिव, is अधोमुखी, in as much as it tends to the creation of the universe associated with the vale of miseries; and भक्ति is ऊर्ध्वमुखी in as much it tends to the upliftment of the individual soul into thorough union

or at–one–ment with परशिव by making him (जीव) free from मलत्रय. Thus the doctrine of मुक्ति, as already remarked, is the distinctive and outstanding feature of the Lingayata religion. सामरस्य is also sometimes called सायुज्यमुक्ति in the sense described heretofore. This short explanation and description of the doctrine of मोक्ष is all that is treated briefly in this section by the author; and it will be pretty clear when it is carefully read through.

 The following श्लोकाऽ from अनुभवसूत्र may be read to understand well what is said above about लिङ्ग, अङ्ग, शक्ति, and भक्ति.

**एकमेव परं ब्रह्म सच्चिदानन्दलक्षणम्।  
शिवतत्वं शिवाचार्याः स्थलमित्याहुरादरात्॥१॥**

**सर्वेषां स्थानभूतत्वाल्लयभूतत्वतरत्ततः।  
तत्वानां महदादीनां स्थलमित्यभिधीयते॥२॥**

**यत्रादौ स्थीयते विश्वं प्राकृतं पौरुषं यतः।  
लीयते पुनरन्ते च स्थलं तत्प्रोच्यते ततः॥३॥**

**स्थकारः स्थानवाची स्याल्लकारो लयवाचकः।  
तयोः कारणभूतं यत्तदेव स्थलमुच्यते॥४॥**

**अधिष्ठानं समस्तस्य स्थावरस्य चरस्य च।  
जगतो यद्भवेत्तत्वं तद्धि वै स्थलमुच्यते॥५॥**

**आधारं सर्व शक्तीनां ज्योतिषामखिलात्मनाम्।  
यत्तत्वं भवति प्राज्ञैः स्थलं तत्परिगीयते॥६॥**

**आलयः सर्व भूतानां लोकानां सर्वसम्पदाम्।  
यद्भवेत्परमं ब्रह्म स्थलं तत्प्राहुरक्षरम्॥७॥**

**परनिर्वाणनिष्ठानां यद्भवेत्परमं पदम्।  
तदाहुः स्थलमद्वैतं शिवज्ञानैकतत्पराः॥८॥**

**स्वशक्तिक्षोभमात्रेण स्थलं तद्विविधं भवेत्।  
एकं लिङ्गस्थलं प्रोक्तमन्यदङ्गस्थलं स्मृतम्॥९॥**

**लिङ्गाङ्गोपाधिभेधेन द्विधा भिन्नं परस्परम्।  
महान् घटप्रभेदेन यथाऽकाशः प्रतीयते॥१०॥**

**प्रतीयते यथा चैव स्थलं लिगाङ्गभेदतः।  
स्थलं नाम परं तत्त्वं शिवरुद्रादिसञ्ज्ञकम्॥११॥**

**उपास्योपासकत्वेन स्वयमेव द्विधाभवेत्।  
लिङ्गस्थलमुपास्यं स्यादङ्गस्थलमुपासकम्॥१२॥**

**उपास्यत्वं तथोपासकत्वं च परमः शिवः।  
अप्रच्युतात्मभेदेन स्थितस्सन्नात्मलीलया॥१३॥**

**स्वशक्त्या युगपत्साक्षात् प्राप्त एव स्वयं क्रमात्।  
नाशिवस्य शिवोपास्तिर्घटते जन्मकोटिभिः॥१४॥**

**शिवस्यैव शिवोपास्तिरिति नानाश्रुतिस्थितिः।  
चिन्मात्रं परमं साक्षात्स्थलं सत्यादिलक्षणम्॥१५॥**

**लिङ्गाङ्गत्वशिवात्मत्वभेदेनैव स्वयं स्थितम्।  
शिवत्वेनात्मरूपेण तथा चिन्मात्रमुच्यते॥१६॥**

**लिङ्गत्वेनाङ्गरूपेण स्थलमात्रं तथोच्यते।  
स्थालं चिन्मात्रमेव स्याल्लिङ्गं स्याच्छिव एव हि॥१७॥**

**तथाङ्गं जीव एव स्यादिति शैवी परा स्थितिः।  
यथा स्थलं द्विधा भूतं तथा शक्तिर्द्विधा भवेत्॥१८॥**

**शक्तिरप्रतिमा साक्षाच्छिवेन सहधर्मिणी।  
साक्षिणी सत्यसम्पूर्णा निर्विकल्पा महेश्वरी॥१९॥**

**सा शक्तिद्विविधा भूता स्वस्वातन्त्र्यबलेन तु।  
समानधर्मिणीत्वेन विभक्ता भक्तवसला॥२०॥**

**लिङ्गस्थलाश्रया काचित्काचिदङ्गरस्थलाश्रया।  
लिङ्गस्थलाश्रया शक्तिः कलारूपा प्रकीर्तिता॥२१॥**

**अङ्गस्थलाश्रया शक्तिर्भक्तिरूपा भवापहा।  
यथा महानलज्वाला विभक्ता दीपिकाकृतिः॥२२॥**

**तथा महेश्वरी शक्तिर्विभक्ता भक्तिरूपिणी।  
ज्वाला तपस्विनी यस्माद्दीपिका सुप्रकाशिनी॥२३॥**

**तस्मात्सवासना शक्तिर्भक्तिर्निवासना मता।  
भर्क्तिर्महत्तरा शुद्धा सुसूक्ष्मा शोभना परा॥२४॥**

**सच्चिदानन्दरूपा स्याद्भुक्तिमुक्तिफलप्रदा।  
शक्तिरेव स्मृता भक्तिर्भक्तिरेव हि सा सदा॥२५॥**

**शक्तिभक्त्योर्न भेदोऽस्ति परमार्थनिरूपणे।  
शक्तिः प्रवृत्तिराख्याता निवृत्तिर्भक्तिरीरिता॥२६॥**

**शक्त्या प्रपञ्चसृष्टिः स्याद्भक्त्या तद्विलयो मतः।  
शक्तिवैचित्र्यतो रूपं निरूपमपि याति हि॥२७॥**

**भक्तिवैचित्र्यतो रूपमपि नीरूपतामियात्।  
प्रपञ्चवृध्यपेक्षा हि शक्तेः स्वाभाविको गुणः॥२८॥**

**प्रपञ्चसङ्क्षयापेक्षा भक्तेः स्वाभाविको गुणः।  
अधोमुखी भवेच्छक्तिर्भक्तिरुर्ध्वमुखी भवेत्॥२९॥**

**समाया शक्तिराख्याता निर्माया भक्तिरीरिता।  
अपि तर्कप्रमाणाभ्यां शक्तेर्भक्तिर्गुणाधिका॥३०॥**

**उपास्यत्वं भवेच्छक्त्या भक्त्योपासकता भवेत्।  
तस्माल्लिङ्गस्थले शक्तिर्भक्तिरङ्गस्थले स्थिता॥३१॥**

**लिङ्गस्थलं शिवस्साक्षाज्जीवात्माऽगस्थलं भवेत्।  
लिङ्गाङ्गस्थलयोरैक्यं शिवजीवैक्यमेव हि॥३२॥**

 In order to understand how distinct is this doctrine of मोक्ष of the Veerashaiva religion is, it will be proper here to note the idea of मोक्ष of the different school of philosophy as follows:—

 अथ कीदृशं मोक्षस्वरूपं तत् मतभेदेन प्रदर्श्यते—स्वातन्त्र्यं मृत्युर्वा मोक्ष इति चार्वाकाः। आत्मोच्छेदो मोक्ष इति शून्यवादिनो माध्यमिकाः। निर्मलज्ञानोदयो मोक्ष इति इतरे बौद्धाः। कर्मकृतस्य देहस्वरूपस्यावरणस्याभावे जीवस्य सततोर्ध्वगमनं मोक्ष इति जैनाः। सर्वकर्तृत्वव्यतिरिक्तानां सर्वज्ञत्वादीनां परमात्मगुणानां प्राप्तिर्याथात्म्येन भगवत्स्वरूपानुभवश्च मोक्ष इति रामानुजीयाः। जगत्कर्तृत्वलक्ष्मीपतित्वश्रीवत्सप्राप्तिरहितं दुःखामिश्रितं पूर्णं सुखं मोक्ष इति माध्वाः। परमैश्वर्यप्राप्तिर्मोक्ष इति नकुलीशपाशुपताः। शिवत्वप्राप्तिरिति शैवाः। पूर्णात्मतालाभ इति प्रत्यभिज्ञावादिनः। पारदरसेन देहस्थैर्ये जीवनन्मुक्तिरेव मोक्ष इति रसेश्वरवादिनः। अशेषगुणोच्छेदो मोक्ष इति वैशेषिकाः। आत्यन्तिकी दुःखनिवृत्तिरिति नैयायिकाः। नैयायिकैकदेशिनस्तु न तत्र केवला दुःखनिवृत्तिः किन्तु सुखमप्यस्तीति आहुः। स्वर्गादिप्राप्तिर्मोक्ष इति मीमांसकाः। मूलचक्रस्थाया परानामिकाया ब्रह्मरूपायां वाचो दर्शनं मोक्ष इति पाणिनीयाः। प्रकृत्युपरमे पुरुषस्य स्वरूपेण अवस्थानं मोक्ष इति साङ्ख्याः। कृतकर्तव्यतया पुरुषार्थशून्यानां सत्वरजस्तमसां

मूलप्रकृतावत्यन्तलयः प्रकृतेर्मोक्षः। चितिशक्तेस्तु निरुपाधिकस्वरूपेणावस्थानं मोक्ष इति पातञ्जलाः। मूलाज्ञाननिवृत्तौ स्वस्वरूपाधिगमः इति अद्वैतवेदान्तिनः। इत्थं प्रमाणप्रभृतिमोक्षपर्यन्तानां पदार्थानां स्वरूपं तत्तद्दर्शनभेदेन प्रतिपादितम्।

(see सर्वदर्शनसङ्ग्रहोपोद्घात by अभ्यङ्करशास्त्री, page 112).

 From this extract it will be clear how different are the ideas of मोक्ष of different schools of philosophy. Of these in the case of the Kashmere School of Shaiva philosophy, namely, प्रत्यभिज्ञाशास्त्र, and the शाङ्कर school of अद्वैत philosophy, there is the idea of सायुज्यमुक्ति. But these schools of philosophy do not term it सामरस्य, which is, therefore, a special term for मोक्ष according to the शक्तिविशिष्टाद्वैत philosophy of the Veerashaiva or Lingayata religion. This religion and the school of philophy is, therefore, the fourth aspect or school of Shaivism and Shaktivishistad waitism.

 सामरस्य is possible in the case of केवलाद्वैत of Shankar school of philosophy. But except for this possibility there is a world of difference, as noted by शिवाद्वैतमञ्जरी as follows:—

 एवंविधलिङ्गाङ्गसामरस्यं विवर्तवादे न सम्भवति। मानसादिभूम्यन्तप्रमेयषट्कस्याविद्याकार्यत्वेन मिथ्यात्वादिति विमर्शनीयम्।

 वीरशैवानन्दचन्द्रिका also states the idea of सामरस्यमोक्ष in contrast with the idea of मोक्ष of other schools of philosophy as follows:—

 ननु पदार्थतत्वज्ञानपूर्वकपरमात्मविशेष्यकेतरभिन्नत्वप्रकारकज्ञानमेकविंशतिविधस्वसमानाधिकरणदुखप्रागभावासमानकालीनदुःखध्वंसरूपमोक्षसाधनमिति तार्किका मन्यन्ते।

 प्रकृतिपुरुषविवेकज्ञानं सात्विकधर्मज्ञानवैराग्यैश्वर्यनिमित्तकप्रकृतिविलयोऽप्रतिहतगतिफलकक्रमिकोर्ध्वगमनरूपमोक्षसाधनमिति निरीश्वरसाङ्ख्यसम्प्रदायविदः।

 पातञ्जलास्तु प्रकृतिपुरुषोभयाधिष्ठातृत्वेन सकलेन्द्रियसमष्टिव्यष्टिसूत्रात्मनो हिरण्यगर्भस्य तद्व्यतिरिक्तत्वेनापि ज्ञानं मोक्षसाधनमिति मन्यन्ते।

 चोदनालक्षणधर्मानुष्ठानद्वारा देहात्मभेदज्ञानं दुःखप्रागभावपरिपालनरूपमोक्षसाधनमिति पूर्वमीमांसकाः।

 पञ्चविधभेदघटितजगत्सत्यत्वज्ञानपूर्वकतारतम्यकृतसेव्यसेवकभावभेदज्ञानपूर्विका सर्वदेवोत्तमत्वेन प्रकृतेः परत्वेन च नारायणे ज्ञानपूर्विका भक्तिर्निरावरणनिजसुखानुभवरूपमोक्षसाधनमिति भेदवादिनो माध्वा।

 तापादिपञ्चसंस्कारयुक्ततया वीरवैष्णवत्वं प्राप्य, श्रवणमनननिदिध्यासनद्वारा निखिलप्रचञ्चशरीरस्य परव्यूहविभवार्च्यान्तर्यामिरूपस्य परवासुदेवस्य सर्वशेषत्वेन सर्वशरीरत्वे। भक्तिरूपविशिष्टाद्वैतज्ञानम् अण्डभित्तेरुपर्यप्राकृतवैकुण्ठे अप्राकृतसहस्रस्तम्भमण्डपे द्राविडप्रबन्धकर्तृनम्माल्वर्प्रभृतिबहुप्रपन्नाधिष्ठिते सुदर्शनद्वारा ब्रह्माण्डादुद्गत्य विरजामुत्तीर्य अमानवकरस्पर्शपरिपूततया लक्ष्मीपतित्वरहितेतरसर्वसारूप्यदास्यानुभवनैरन्तर्येणावस्थानुरूपमुक्तिसाधनमिति विशिष्ठद्वैतवादिनो रामानुजाः।


नीलकण्ठत्वत्रिनेत्रत्वोमासाहायत्वचन्द्रशेखरत्वादिविशिष्टनित्यदिव्यमङ्गलविग्रहषडङ्गदशाव्ययषड्गुणेश्वर्यपरिपूर्णस्य षट्त्रिंशत्तत्वोतीर्णस्वभावसिद्धनित्यनिर्मलनिरतिशयसर्वार्थज्ञानक्रियाशक्तियुक्ततया पञ्चकृत्यपरिनियामकस्य पतिशब्दितस्य परमशिवस्य प्रपञ्चशरीरस्य सर्वशेषत्वरूपविशिष्टाद्वैतज्ञानं शिवप्रसादजन्यभक्तिद्वारा ब्रह्माण्डाब्दहिः परमपदाख्याप्राकृतकैलासे नन्द्यादिप्रमथगणपरिष्कृते पार्वतीपतित्वरहितपरमशिवसाम्येनावस्थानरूपमोक्षसाधनमिति नीलकण्ठाचार्यमताबलम्बिनः।


प्रपञ्चप्त्यानिर्वचनीयत्वरूपमिथ्यात्वेनान्तःकरणप्रतिबिम्बरूपजीवनिर्विशेषब्रह्मैक्यज्ञानम् अज्ञाननिवृत्तिद्वारा सच्चिदानन्दरूपकूटस्थस्वरूपावाप्तिरूपकैवल्यासाधनमिति अद्वैतवादिनः।

 इत्येतादृशबहुविधभेदाभेदमुक्तिजनकज्ञानोपदेशकगुरूणां बहूना विद्यमानत्वाद्वीरमाहेश्वरगुरोरेव सर्वोत्तमत्वमिति कथमध्यवसीयत इति चेदुच्यते।

 लिगाङ्गसामरस्यरूपमुक्तिसाधनीभूततत्सामरस्यानुसन्धानस्यासाधरणतया मोक्षहेतुत्वेन तादृशविशिष्टाद्वैतज्ञानप्रदायकस्य....... गुरोरेवेतरापेक्षयाभ्यर्हितत्वम्।

 From the two extracts given above in the idea of मोक्ष there is an element common to all Vedic schools of Indian philosophy, i. e. all schools exclusive of चार्वाक, बौद्ध, and जैन schools; it is either निरतिशयसुख or दुःखाभाव. In the case of माध्व school मोक्ष consists in सालोक्य and सामीप्य in addition to

सुख; in the case रामानुजीयवैष्णवाऽ, मोहश्वराऽ (the followers of नीलकण्ठाचार्य) and लकुलीशपाशुपताऽ मोक्ष is सारूप्य and not सायुज्य. In the case of प्रत्यभिज्ञा Shaiva school, and also in the case of शाङ्कर school, मोक्ष is सायुज्य, but according to Veerashaivas it is सामरस्य.

 It will be in the fitness of matters here to say briefly how जीव or the individual soul will attain at–one–ment with शिव or परब्रह्मन्. The devotee has to undergo in the first instance the दीक्षा ceremony of the three kinds. He is then taught by his गुरु the principles and practices of the religion. Thereafter he has got to perform the उपासना of worship of शिवलिङ्ग daily, which उपासना is अंहग्रहोपासना and not the प्रतीकोपासना. He has to begin with the उपासना of इष्टलिङ्ग or the लिङ्ग in its gross form, and then to advance step by step to the worship of प्राणलिङ्ग, the vital Linga, and भावलिङ्ग the ideal Linga. भक्ति also rises into the higher forms, from श्रद्धा to नैष्टिक form etc., corresponding to the higher forms of Linga and अङ्ग in a particular stage. He has to offer to the Linga everything that he takes, not only the food but all things that he enjoys for personal comfort. This is लिङ्गसमर्पण; and the things offered thus become the प्रसाद of the लिङ्ग. When it is said that the devotee should begin with the उपासना of इष्टलिङ्ग and advance to the उपासना of other Lingas by stages, it does not mean that in the initial stage the devotee is to be concerned with इष्टलिङ्ग only, but it means that the devotee has to worship इष्टलिङ्ग or gross Linga, as the visible outward Linga, connected with Lingas located in the चक्राऽ internally in the body. इष्टलिङ्ग and its two modifications, आचारलिङ्ग and गुरुलिङ्ग, are themselves located in the चक्राऽ of the body, namely, आचारलिङ्ग in the आधारचक्र and गुरुलिङ्ग in स्वाधिष्ठानचक्र. इष्टलिङ्ग is, therefore, only the visible

symbol of the inner corresponding Linga and its further twofold divisions or modifications. Even during the उपासना of इष्टलिङ्ग in the initial stage the devotee has to be conscious of other Lingas, प्राणलिङ्ग and भावलिङ्ग and their further twofold modifications. During the advanced stages as well the devotee is not to lose sight of इष्टलिङ्ग but has to begin with इष्टलिङ्ग proceed to the higher, which, during that higher stage, is a short affair. The devotee has to practise अष्टावरणाऽ or the eightfold protective fences all along. In the advanced stages some of the अष्टावरणाऽ, that are also symbolic, assume an ideal form. The devotee then has not to be very keen on outward symbols during the advanced stages. The devotee has also to observe all through life the पञ्चाचाराऽ fivefold code of conduct, namely, शिवाचार, लिङ्गाचार, भृत्याचार, गणाचार, and सदाचार. All these are necessary for the devotee to reach at-one-ment with परमशिव. It is, therefore, said in शिवाद्वैतदर्पण, page 3—अत्र अथ शद्वार्थोऽष्टावरणविशिष्टपञ्चाचाररूपपतिधर्मप्राप्त्यानन्तर्यवचनः। So also it is said in क्रियासार, page 18—अत्याश्रमिणो वीरशैवास्तु पञ्चाचाराष्टावरणैरेव नैयत्यं सम्पाद्य "ज्ञानेन निष्ठया वस्तु साधयेत्" इति कर्मब्रह्मज्ञानोभयसाधनाभ्यां मोक्ष इत्यधिकार्यनुगुणमोक्षवैचित्र्यं साक्षात्परम्परया इति तादृशं फलं च शक्तिविशिष्टब्रह्मीभावः।

 शिवाचार is the Veerashaiva's attitude towards the deity that शिव, is the परब्रह्मन्; and he has not to worship any other deity. शिव, being the परब्रह्मन्, he has not to take any other deity, as परब्रह्मन्. He should maintain all through his life the idea of शिव as the only Godhead and deity to the exclusion of others. Whatever devotional acts he does, whatever devotional prayers he offers, and whatever devotional thoughts he thinks, they must be all about Shiva and none else. This is शिवाचार, लिङ्गाचार is the devotee's

daily religious practices, prayers, and thoughts about Linga, as शिवा's appearance in all the forms of Linga, which are the basis of his religious and spiritual life. शिव is the highest deity and is निर्गुण; but लिङ्ग is his सगुण form and is the उपास्यदेवता of the devotee, to enable the devotee to offer his devotions to the निर्गुणबह्मन्, शिव, through this सगुणदेवता, लिङ्ग. This is लिङ्गाचार, भृत्याचार is the devotee's attitude of complete humility towards शिव and his forms of लिङ्ग. So also he has to maintain the attitude of humility towards his गुरु, who gives him all guidancce in the spiritual life and practices, the जङ्गम, who is शिव being a जीवन्मुक्त person, and the शिवशरणाऽ, the great and renowned devotees of शिव. He has to adopt the attitude of service towards all Veerashaivas, all human beings and the animal world. गणाचार is the devotee's of conduct towards the society and the community. He has to live well as a member of the community, strive for the upliftment, increase and service of the community. Lastly सदाचार is the code of devotee's ethical or moral life in his behaviour as an honest and strengaiforward being. All these forms of पञ्चाचार or the code of conduct, religious and moral, are meant for the Veerashaivas all through their lives with the ultimate goal of attaining at–one–ment with परशिव, which at–one–ment in the technical language of the Veerashaiva religion is लिङ्गाङ्गसामरस्य or शिवजीवैक्य. It may be noted that the author does not say anything about the अष्टावरणाऽ and पञ्चाचाराऽ in the devotee's religious life. But the brief description of these पञ्चाचाराऽ has been given above, in order that the reader may understand how they are helpful to the devotee for his uniformly religious and moral life to attain the ultimate goal of शिवैक्य. Readers may be referred to the introduction for information about अष्टावरणाऽ.

 P. 47. लिङ्गाङ्गसम्बन्धरूप.....लिङ्गधारणसिद्धिर्निःप्रत्यूहैव—This is the assertion of the सिद्धान्तिन् that लिङ्गधारण is beyond cavil on account of the लिङ्गाङ्गसम्बन्ध. By लिङ्गाङ्गसम्बन्ध the author means लिङ्गाङ्गसामरस्य or शिवजीवैक्य, though he does not say so in so many words. But that he means so is clear from the words coming later, namely, "जीवब्रम्हैक्यापरनामक....अविभाज्यसंयोगः" "तयोरविभाज्ययोगः" on pages 48 and 49.

 P. 47. ननु तत्त्वमसि...न लिङ्गाङ्गसम्बन्धः।—Here is the objection advanced against the assertion of the सिद्धान्तिन् contained in the foregoing. The objection seems to come from a केवलाद्वैतिन् of शङ्कर School, as may be gathered from the words "अविद्योपहितं ब्रम्हैव जीवः" But शङ्कराचार्य never speaks of परब्रम्ह as परशिव, particularized as "नीलकण्ठचन्द्रशेखरात्वादिमाङ्गल्यगुणकसर्वज्ञतादिषडङ्गविशिष्टः परमशिवः". He always speaks of परब्रह्म as having only three positive attributes सत्, चित्, आनन्द, and none else. The षडङ्गाऽ, in addition to सत्, चित् and आनन्द, are always said to have been possessed by परशिव by all schools of Shaivas. The षडङ्गाऽ are mentioned in—

**सर्वज्ञता तृप्तिरनादिबोधः स्वतन्त्रता नित्यमलुप्तता च।  
अनन्तशक्तिश्च विभोर्विधिज्ञाः षडाहुरङ्गानि महेश्वरस्य॥**

 Here it seems that the author refers to the doctrine of the school of माहेश्वराऽ. The doctrine of मोक्ष, given last in the extract from वीरशैवानन्दचन्द्रिका above, may be seen in this connection. There it is said "नीलकण्ठाचार्यमताबलम्बिनः" i. e. माहेश्वराऽ.

 P. 47. लक्ष्यकत्वमङ्गीकृत्य—All schools of Vedanta philosophy resort to लक्ष्यार्थ of the two sentences (तत्त्वमसि and अहं ब्रम्हास्मि) on account of the वाच्यार्थ being inapplicable to the statement contained in them. The following exrract from वेदान्तसार of सदानन्द, a follower of शङ्कराचार्या's केवलाद्वैत philosophy, may be noted.

 आभ्यामध्यारोपापवादाभ्यां तत्त्वम्पदार्थशोधनमपि सिद्धं भवति। तथाहि। अज्ञानादिसमष्टिरेतदुपहितं सर्वज्ञत्वादिविशिष्टं चैतन्यमेतदनुपहितं चैतत्त्रयं तत्पदवाच्यार्थो भवति। एतदुपाध्युपहिताधारभूतमनुपहितं चैतन्यं तत्पदलक्ष्यार्थो भवति। अज्ञानादिव्यष्टिरेतदुपहिताल्पज्ञत्वादिविशिष्टचैतन्यमेतदनुपहितं चैतत्त्रयं तप्तायःपिण्डवदेकत्वेनावभासमानं त्वम्पदवाच्यार्थो भवति। एतदुपाध्युपहिताधारभूतमनुपहितं प्रत्यगानन्दं तुरीयं चैतन्यं त्वम्पदलक्ष्यार्थो भवति।

 अथ महावाक्यार्थो वर्ण्यते। इदं तत्त्वमसिवाक्यं सम्बन्धत्रयेणाखण्डार्थबोधकं भवति। सम्बन्धत्रयं नाम पदयोः सामानाधिकरण्यं पदार्थयोर्विशेषणविशेष्यभावः प्रत्यगात्मलक्षणयोर्लक्ष्यलक्षणभावश्चेति। तदुक्तम्।

**"सामानाधिकरण्यं च विशेषणविशेष्यता।  
लक्ष्यलक्षणसम्बन्धः पदार्थप्रत्यगात्मनाम्" इति॥**

सामानाधिकरण्यसम्बन्धस्तावद्यथा सोऽयं देवदत्त इत्यस्मिन्वाक्ये तत्कालविशिष्टदेवदत्तवाचकसशब्दस्यैतत्कालविशिष्टदेवदत्तवाचकायंशब्दस्य चैकस्मिन्पिण्डे तात्पर्यसम्बन्धः। तथा च तत्त्वमसीति वाक्येऽपि परोक्षत्वादिविशिष्टचैतन्यवाचकतत्पदस्यापरोक्षत्वाविशिष्टचैतन्यवाचकत्वम्पदस्य चैकस्मिंश्चैतन्ये तात्पर्यसम्बन्धः। विशेषणविशेष्यभावसम्बन्धस्तु यथा तत्रैव वाक्ये सशब्दार्थतत्कालविशिष्टदेवदत्तस्यायंशब्दार्थैतत्कालविशिष्टदेवदत्तस्य चान्योन्यभेदव्यावर्त्तकतया विशेषणविशेष्यभावः। तथात्रापि वाक्ये तत्पदार्थपरोक्षत्वादिविशिष्टचैतन्यस्य त्वम्पदार्थापरोक्षत्वादिविशिष्टचैतन्यस्य चान्योन्यभेदव्यावर्तकतया विशेषणविशेष्यभावः। लक्ष्यलक्षणसम्बन्धस्तु यथा तत्रैव सशद्बायंशब्दयोस्तदर्थयोर्वा विरुद्धैतत्कालैतत्कालविशिष्टत्वपरित्यागेनाविरुद्धदेवदत्तेन सह लक्ष्यलक्षणभावः। तथात्रापि वाक्ये तत्त्वम्पदयोस्तदर्थयोर्वा विरुद्धपरोक्षत्वापरोक्षत्वादिविशिष्टत्वपरित्यागेनाविरुद्धचैतन्येन सह लक्ष्यलक्षणभावः। इयमेव भागलक्षणेत्युच्यते।

अस्मिन्वाक्ये नीलमुत्पलमिति वाक्यवद्वाक्यार्थो न सङ्गच्छते। तत्र तु नीलपदार्थनीलगुणस्योत्पलपदार्थोत्पलद्रव्यस्य च शौक्ल्यपटादिभेदव्यावर्त्तकतयान्योन्याविशेषणविशेष्यभावसंसर्गस्यान्यतरविशिष्टस्यान्यतरस्य तदैक्यस्य वा वाक्यार्थत्वाङ्गीकारे प्रमाणान्तरविरोधाभावात्तद्वाक्यार्थः सङ्गच्छते। अत्र तु तदर्थपरोक्षत्वादिविशिष्टचैतन्यस्य त्वमर्थापरोक्षत्वादिविशिष्टचैतन्यस्य चान्योन्यभेदव्यावर्तकतया विशेषणविशेष्यभावसंसर्गस्यान्यतरविशिष्टस्यान्यतरस्य तदैकस्य च वाक्यार्थत्वाङ्गीकारे प्रत्यक्षादिप्रमाणविरोधाद्वाक्यार्थो न सङ्गच्छते। तदुक्तम्।

**"संसर्गो वा विशिष्टो वा वाक्यार्थो नात्र सम्मतः।  
अखण्डैकरसत्वेन वाक्यार्थो विदुषां मत" इति॥**

 अत्र गङ्गायां घोषः प्रतिवसतीतिवाक्यवज्जहल्लक्षणापि न सङ्गच्छते। तत्र तु गङ्गाघोषयोराधाराधेयभावलक्षणस्य वाक्यार्थस्याशेषतो विरुद्धत्वाद्वाक्यार्थमशेषतः परित्यज्य तत्सम्बन्धितीरलक्षणाया युक्तत्वाज्जहल्लक्षणा सङ्गच्छते। अत्र तु परोक्षापरोक्षचैतन्यैकत्वलक्षणस्य वाक्यार्थस्य भागमात्रे विरोधाद्भागान्तरमपि परित्यज्यान्यलक्षणाया अयुक्तत्वाज्जहल्लक्षणा न सङ्गच्छते। न च गङ्गापदं स्वार्थपरित्यागेन तीरपदार्थं यथा लक्षयति तथा तत्पदं त्वम्पदं वा स्वार्थपरित्यागेन त्वम्पदार्थं तत्पदार्थं वा लक्षयत्वतः कुतो जहल्लक्षणा न सङ्गच्छत इति वाच्यम्। तत्र तीरप्रतीत्यपेक्षायामपि तत्त्वम्पदयोः श्रूयमाणत्वेन तदर्थप्रतीतौ लक्षणया पुनरन्यतरपदेनान्यतरपदार्थप्रतीत्यपेक्षाभावात्।

 अत्र शोणो धावतीतिवाक्यवदजहल्लक्षणापि न सम्भवति। तत्र शोणगुणगमनलक्षणस्य वाक्यार्थस्य विरुद्धत्वात्तदपरित्यागेन तदाश्रयाश्वादिलक्षणया तद्विरोधपरिहारसम्भवादजहल्लक्षणा सम्भवति। अत्र तु परोक्षत्वापरोक्षत्वादिविशिष्टचैतन्यैकत्वस्य वाक्यार्थस्य लक्षितत्वेऽपि तद्विरोधपरिहारासम्भवादजहल्लक्षणा। न च तत्पदं त्वम्पदं वा स्वार्थविरुद्धांशपरित्यागेनांशान्तरसहितं त्वम्पदार्थं तत्पदार्थं वा लक्षयत्वतः कथं प्रकारान्तरेण भागलक्षणाङ्गीकरणमितिवाच्यम्। एकेन पदेन स्वार्थांशपदार्थान्तरोभयलक्षणाया असम्भवात्पदान्तरेण तदर्थप्रतीतौ लक्षणया पुनस्तत्प्रतीत्यपेक्षाभावाच्च।

 तस्माद्यथा सोऽयं देवदत्त इति वाक्ये तदर्थो वा तत्कालैतत्कालविशिष्टदेवदत्तलक्षणस्य वाक्यार्थस्यांशे विरोधाद्विरुद्धतत्कालैतत्कालविशिष्टांशं परित्यज्याविरुद्धदेवदत्तांशमात्रं लक्षयति तथा तत्वमसीतिवाक्यं तदर्थो वा परोक्षत्वापरोक्षत्वाविशिष्टचैतन्यैकत्वलक्षणस्य वाक्यार्थस्यांशे विरोधाद्विरुद्धपरोक्षत्वापरोक्षत्वविशिष्टांशं परित्यज्याविरुद्धमखण्डचैतन्यमात्रं लक्षयतीति।

 अथाधुनाहं ब्रह्मास्मीत्यनुभववाक्यार्थो वर्ण्यते। एवमाचार्येणाध्यारोपापवादपुरस्सरं तत्त्वम्पदार्थौ शोधयित्वा वाक्येनाखण्डार्थेऽवबोधितेऽधिकारिणोऽहं नित्यशुद्धबुद्धमुक्तसत्यस्वभावपरमानन्दानन्ताद्वयं ब्रह्मास्मीत्यखण्डाकाराकारिता चित्तवृत्तिरुदेति। सा तु चित्प्रतिबिम्बसहिता सती प्रत्यगभिन्नमज्ञातं परं ब्रह्म विषयीकृत्य तद्गताज्ञानमेव बाधते तदा पटकारणतन्तुदाहे पटदाहवदखिलकारणेऽज्ञाने बाधिते सति तत्कार्यस्याखिलस्य बाधितत्वात्तदन्तर्भूताखण्डाकाराकारिता चित्तवृत्तिरपि बाधिता भवति। तत्र प्रतिबिम्बितं

चैतन्यमपि यथा दीपप्रभादित्यप्रभावभासनासमर्था सती तयाभिभूता भवति तथा स्वयम्प्रकाशमानप्रत्यगभिन्नपरब्रह्मावभासनानर्हतया तेनाभिभूतं सत्स्वोपाधिभूताखण्डवृत्तेर्बाधितत्वाद्दर्पणाभावे मुखप्रतिबिम्बस्य मुखमानत्ववत्प्रत्यगभिन्नपरब्रह्ममात्रं भवति।

 I. The extract from शिवाद्वैतदर्पण in explanation of शिवजीवैक्य, reproduced later below, may also be noted in comparison with this extract.

 P. 47. अभिप्रायमनगतवानसि etc.—Here the author says that the non–Veerashaivas have not been able to understand the meaning of लिङ्गाङ्गसामरस्य or शिवजीवैक्य. He, therefore, proceeds to explain what is meant by it.

 P. 48. हस्तमस्तकसंयोगरूपवेधादीक्षया etc.—Here the author refers briefly to the three kinds of दीक्षा, वेधा, मनु, and क्रिया. The three दीक्षा ceremonies are connected with the three primary modifications of अङ्ग, योगाङ्ग, भोगाङ्ग and त्यागाङ्ग.

 The three primary अङ्गाऽ have been explained by अनुभवसूत्र as—

**शिवयोगसुखावाप्त्या योगाङ्गमिति कथ्यते।  
शिवेन सह भोगेन भोगाङ्गतिति गीयते।  
संसारभ्रान्तिसन्त्यागात्त्यागाङ्गमिति पठ्यते॥**

 The three kinds of दीक्षा ceremony are meant for introducing the novice into the principles of षट्स्थल by the explanation being given of the three primary अङ्गाऽ. The meaning of दीक्षा, as given by अनुभवसूत्र, is—

**दीयते लिङ्गसम्बन्धः क्षीयते च मलत्रयम्।  
दीयते क्षीयते यस्मात्सा दीक्षेति निगद्यत्ते॥  
पाशानशेषान्कर्तर्या छित्वा लिङ्गं समादिशेत्।  
कर्तरी शिवदीक्षा स्याद्यया पाशाञ्च्छिनत्त्यसौ॥**

 The three दीक्षा ceremonies are explained in the same book as follows:—

**सा दीक्षा परमा शैवी त्रिधा भवति निर्मला I  
एका वैधात्मिका साक्षादन्या मन्त्रात्मिका मता॥**

**क्रियात्मिका परा काचित् एवमेव त्रिधा भवेत्।  
हस्तमस्तकसंयोगच्छलाद्वेधेति गीयते॥  
गुरुणोदीरिता कर्णे या सा मान्त्रीति कथ्यते॥  
शिष्यपाणितले दत्ता या दीक्षा सा क्रियोच्यते॥**

 All these ceremonies are well treated in शिद्धान्तशिखामणि (pp. 86 88), particularly the last one, which readers may see for information.

 The three दीक्षाऽ are meant for the three primary अङ्गाऽ (योगाङ्ग, भोगाङ्ग and त्यागाङ्ग) that the devotee may worship the three corresponding लिङ्गाऽ, beginning with his devotion to इष्टलिङ्ग and advance by steps to higher लिङ्गाऽ.

 P. 48. लीनं गच्छति etc.—These contain the explanation of what is meant by लिङ्ग. The explanation given by अनुभवसूत्र of लिङ्ग will be elucidating as—

**शिव एव स्वयं लिङ्गमिति लिङ्गस्य वैभवम्।  
नान्यच्चिन्हादिकं लिङ्गं तत्सर्वं कृतकं च यत्॥**

**लीयते गम्यते यत्र येन सर्वं चराचरम्।  
तदेतल्लिङ्गमित्युक्तं लिङ्गतत्वपरायणैः॥**

**लयगत्यर्थयोर्हेतुभूतत्वात्सर्वदेहिनाम्।  
लिङ्गमित्युच्यते साक्षाच्छिवः सकलनिष्कलः॥**

 P. 48. मायोपाधिकलिङ्गरूपशरीरावछिन्नब्रम्हैव—Here माया means शिवा's शक्ति in all her manifold and multifarious forms and not the माया of केवलाद्वैत school. माया is called आदिमाया, the शक्ति of परमशिव. शैवीमाया is well explained in मृगेन्द्रागम, which readers may read for full information.

 P. 48. अमिति etc.—These lines contain the explanation of अङ्ग or the individual soul. The following श्लोक from अनुभवसूत्र may be read for understanding अङ्ग better:—

अं भवेत्परमं ब्रह्म तद्गतं तत्परायणम्।  
अङ्गस्थलमिति प्राहुरङ्गतत्वविशारदाः॥

 P. 48. विग्रहं देवदेवस्य etc.—This verse gives briefly some idea of पशु and पाश, which imply पति. The three are invariably associated with one another and form the very essence of पाशुपत religion. These three are given very great prominence in प्रत्यभिज्ञा system of Kashmere Shaivism, which is also called the त्रिक sysetm or the system of the three, पति, पशु and पाश. In other Shaiva systems the idea of the three is there; but they are not given as much prominence as in the Kashmere system. In corroboration of what is said above the following may be noted:—

**शैवागमेषु मुख्यं पतिपशुपाशा इति क्रमात्त्रितयम्।  
तत्र पतिः शिव उक्तः पशवो ह्यणवोऽर्थपञ्चकः पाशः॥**

(माहेश्वरदर्शन in सर्वदर्शनसङ्ग्रह page 187, Oriental Sans. series)

**विदधतु मतिमस्मिन्वीरशैवा विशिष्टाः।  
पशुपतिमतसारे पण्डितश्लाघनीये॥**

(सिद्धान्तशिखामणि—part I, page 10).

 P. 49. इतिबुद्ध्या....विजानाति—This contains the explanation of how by लक्ष्यार्थ the innate oneness of अङ्ग and लिङ्ग or जीव and शिव is easily explained. But जीवाऽ or individual souls come to be entangled with पाशाऽ and look so different from शिव. Hence the primary sense of तत् and त्वम् being identical is impossible. And, therefore, the sentences तत्त्वमसि and

अहं ब्रह्मास्मि are to be explained by having recourse to the secondary sense of तत् and त्वम्. Otherwise the two sentences become meaningless.

 The author's explanation is very brief. The following extract from शिवाद्वैतदर्पण will be found very elucidating.

(see शिवाद्वैतदर्पणः pp. 51–57)

 एवं पूर्वोक्तदिशा तत्त्वमस्यादिमहावाक्यार्थशोधनमपि सिद्धं भवति। तथाहि नामरूपविभागार्हसूक्ष्मचिदचिदूपशक्तिस्तद्विशिष्ठं सर्वज्ञत्वादिधर्मविशिष्टं चैतन्यं नामरूपविभागानर्हसूक्ष्मचिदचिद्रूपशक्तिविशिष्टं शुद्धचैतन्यं चैतत् त्रयम् अहिकुण्डलवेदकत्वेन प्रतीयमानं सत् तत्पदवाच्यार्थो भवति। नामरूपविभागार्हसूक्ष्मचिदचिद्रूपशक्तितद्विशिष्टसर्वज्ञत्वादिधर्मिकचैतन्ययोराधारभूतं यन्नामरूपविभागानर्हसूक्ष्मचिदचिद्रूपशक्तिविशिष्टं शुद्धचैतन्यं तत् तत्पदलक्ष्यार्थो भवति। एवं स्थूलचिदचिद्रूपशक्तिस्तद्विशिष्टं किञ्चिज्ञत्वादिधार्मिकचैतन्यं नामरूपविभागानर्हसूक्ष्मचिदचिद्रपशक्तिविशिष्टं शुद्धचैतन्यं चैतत् त्रयं त्वम्पदवाच्यार्थो भवति। स्थूलचिदचिद्रूपशक्तितद्विशिष्टकिञ्चिज्ञत्वादिधार्मिकचैतन्ययोराधारभूतं यन्नामरूपविभागानर्हसूक्ष्मचिदचिद्रूपशक्तिविशिष्टं शुद्धचैतन्यं तत् त्वम्पदलक्ष्यार्थो भवति। इदं तत्वमसीति महावाक्यं शिवजीवयोर्विरुद्धधर्मविशिष्टयोः साक्षादैक्यप्रतिपादकत्वाभावेऽपि लक्षणया सम्बन्धत्रयेणैकार्थं प्रतिपादयति। सम्बन्धत्रयञ्च पदयोः सामानाधिकरण्यं पदार्थयोर्विशेषणविशेष्यभावः प्रत्यगात्मतद्धर्मयोर्लक्ष्यलक्षणभावश्चेति। तत्र पदयोः सामानाधिकरण्यं च भिन्नप्रवृत्तिनिमित्तयोः पदयोरेकस्मिन्नर्थे वृत्तिः। तच्च सोऽयं देवदत्त इत्यस्मिन् वाक्ये तत्कालतद्देशवैशिष्ट्यं तत्पदप्रवृत्तिनिमित्तम्। तथा च भिन्नप्रवृत्तिनिमित्तयोः सोऽयं पदयोरेकस्मिन् देवदत्तपिण्डे तात्पर्यसम्बन्धः। तद्वत् तत्वमसीति वाक्येऽपि सूक्ष्मचिदचिद्रूपशक्तिवैशिष्ट्यं तत्पदप्रवृत्तिनिमित्तम्। स्थूलचिदचिद्रूपशक्तिवैशिष्ट्यं त्वम्पदप्रवृत्तिनिमित्तम्। तथा च भिन्नप्रवृत्तिनिमितयोः तत्त्वम्पदयोरेकस्मिन्नामरूपविभागानर्हसूक्ष्मचिदचिद्रूपशक्तिविशिष्टे शुद्धचैतन्ये तात्पर्यसम्बन्धः सामानाधिकरण्यमिति॥१॥

 पदार्थयोर्व्यावर्तकव्यावर्त्यभावसम्बन्धो विशेषणविशेष्यभावसम्बन्धः। स च सोऽयं देवदत्त इत्यत्र अयम्पदवाच्यो सोऽसावेतत्कालैतद्देशविशिष्टो देवत्तदपिण्डो अयं स इति तच्छब्दवाच्यात् तत्कालतद्देशविशिष्टाद्देवदत्तपिण्डात् भिन्नो नेति यदा प्रतीयते तदातच्छब्दार्थस्याऽयम्पदवाच्यार्थनिष्ठभेदव्यवार्तकतया विशेषणम् अयम्पदार्थस्य व्यावर्त्यत्वाद्विशेष्यत्वम्।

तथा स इति तत्पदवाच्यस्तत्कालतद्देशविशिष्टो देवदत्तपिण्डः सोऽयमित्ययम्पदवाच्यात् एतत्कालैतद्देशविशिष्टादस्माद्देवदत्तपिण्डात् भिन्नो नेति यदा प्रतीयते तदाऽयंशब्दवाच्यस्य तत्पदार्थनिष्ठभेदव्यावर्तकतया विशेषणत्वं तत्पदार्थस्य व्यावर्त्यत्वाद्विशेष्यत्वम्। तथा चायमेव स स एवायम् इत्यन्योन्यभेदव्यावर्तकतया सोऽयं पदार्थयोः परस्परं विशेषणविशेष्यभावः। तद्वदत्रापि तत्वमसीति महावाक्ये यत्त्वम्पदवाच्यं स्थूलचिदचिद्रूपशक्तिविशिष्टं चैतन्यं तत् तत्पदवाच्यात् सूक्ष्मचिद्रूपशक्तिविशिष्टचैतन्यात् भिन्नो नेति यदा प्रतीयते तदा तत्पदार्थस्य त्वं पदार्थनिष्ठं भेदव्यावर्तकतया विशेषणत्वं त्वं पदार्थस्य व्यावर्त्यत्वात् विशेष्यत्वम्। तथा तत्पदवाच्यं यत् सूक्ष्मचिदचिद्रूपशक्तिविशिष्ठं चेतन्यं तत् त्वम्पदवाच्यात् स्थूलचिदचिद्रूपशक्तिविशिष्टचैतन्यात् भिन्नं नेति यदा प्रतीयते तदा त्वं पदार्थस्य तत्पदार्थनिष्ठभेदव्यावर्तकतया विशेषणत्वं तत्पदार्थस्य व्यावर्त्यत्वाद्विशेष्यत्वम्। तथा च त्वं तदसि तत्वमसि इति तत्त्वम्पदार्थयोः परस्परं भेदव्यावर्तकतया विशेषणविशेष्यभावसम्बन्धोऽवगन्तव्यः॥२॥

 लक्ष्यलक्षणभावसबन्धस्तु प्रत्यगात्मतद्गतधर्मयोः लक्ष्यलक्षणभावसम्बन्धः। असाधारणधर्मप्रतिपादकवाक्यं लक्षणवाक्यं तत्प्रतिपाद्यमवशिष्टं लक्ष्यम्। तथा च सोऽयं देवदत्तः इत्यस्मिन्वाक्ये सोऽयं पदयोस्तदर्थयोर्वा विरुद्धतत्कालतद्देशविशिष्टत्वैतत्कालैतद्देशविशिष्टत्वयोः परित्यागेनाविरुद्धदेवदत्तत्वविशिष्टदेवदत्तपिण्डेन सह देवदत्तत्वविशिष्टदेवदत्तवाचकशद्वस्य लक्ष्यलक्षणभावसम्बन्धः। तद्वत् तत्त्वमसीतिमहावाक्ये तत्त्वं पदयोस्तदर्थयोर्वा विरुद्धसूक्ष्मस्थूलचिदचिद्रूपशक्तिविशिष्टत्वपरित्यागेन तत्त्वम्पदाभ्यां लक्ष्याविरुद्धनामरूपविभागानर्हसूक्ष्मचिदचिद्रूपशक्तिविशिष्टचैतन्येन सह तत्त्वम्पदयोर्लक्ष्यलक्षणभावसम्बन्धः। इयमेव भागलक्षणेत्युच्यते। अत्र तत्त्वं पदयोस्तदर्थयोश्च त्यक्तविरुद्धाशययोर्लक्षणत्वम् अखण्डपरशिवचैतन्यस्य लक्ष्यत्वमिति मन्तव्यम्॥३॥

 तत्राभिधेयाविनाभूतप्रवृत्तिर्लक्षणा। सा च त्रिधा जहल्लक्षणा–अजहल्लक्षणा–जहदजहल्लक्षणा चेति। वाच्यार्थमशेषतः परित्यज्य तत्सम्बन्धिन्यर्थान्तरे वृत्तिर्जहल्लक्षणा। सा च गङ्गायां घोषः इति वाक्यवदत्र न सङ्गच्छते तत्र तु गङ्गाघोषयोराधाराधेयभावलक्षणस्य वाक्यार्थस्याशेषतो विरुद्धत्वात् वाक्यार्थमशेषतः परित्यज्य तत्सम्बन्धिन्यर्थान्तरे तीरे लक्षणाया युक्तत्वात् जहल्लक्षणा सङ्गच्छते। अत्र तु सूक्ष्मस्थूलप्रपञ्चाकारशक्तिविशिष्टचैतन्यैकत्वलक्षणस्य वाक्यार्थस्य भागमात्रे विरोधात् भागान्तरमपि परित्यज्यान्यलक्षणाया अयुक्तत्वाज्जहल्लक्षणा न सङ्गच्छते॥४॥

 वाच्यार्थपरित्यागेन तत्सम्बन्धिनिवृत्तिरजहल्लक्षणा। सा च 'शोणो धावति' इत्यादिवाक्यवत् अत्र न सङ्गच्छते। तत्र शोणगुणगमनलक्षणस्य वाक्यार्थस्य विरुद्धत्वात्तदपरित्यागेन तदाश्रयाश्वादिलक्षणया तद्विरोधपरिहारसम्भवादजहल्लक्षणा सङ्गच्छते। अत्र तु सूक्ष्मस्थूलप्रपञ्चाकारशक्तिविशिष्टचैतन्यैकत्वलक्षणस्य वाक्यार्थस्य विरुद्धत्वात् तदपरित्यागेन तत्सम्बन्धिनो यस्य कस्यचिदर्थस्य लक्षितत्वेऽपि तद्विरोधपरिहारासम्भवादजहल्लक्षणा न सङ्गच्छते। तस्माज्जहदजहल्लक्षणयैव महावाक्यर्थबोध इति। तथा चोक्तं गुरुचरणैः

**एकात्मकत्वाज्जहती न सम्भवेत्।  
तथाऽजहल्लक्षणता विरोधतः॥**

**सोऽयम्पदार्थाविव भागलक्षणा।  
युज्येत तत्वम्पदयोरदोषतः॥**

इति महावाक्यार्थबोधने जहदजहल्लक्षणैव स्वीकृता॥५॥

 तत्र वाच्यार्थैकदेशपरित्यागनैकेदेशवृत्तिर्जहदजहल्लक्षणा सा च यथा सोऽयं देवदत्त इति वाक्यं तदर्थो वा तत्कालैतत्कालविशिष्टदेवदत्तलक्षणस्य वाक्यार्थस्यांशे विरोधात् विरुद्धतत्कालतत्कालादिविशिष्टांशं परित्यज्याविरुद्धदेवदत्तांशमात्रं लक्षयति। तथा तत्वमसीति वाक्यं तदर्थो वा सूक्ष्मस्थूलप्रपञ्चाकारशक्तिविशिष्टचैतन्यैकत्वलक्षणस्य वाक्यार्थांशे विरोधात् विरुद्धसूक्ष्मस्थूलांशं परित्यज्याविरुद्धनामरूपविभागानर्हसूक्ष्मचिद्रूपशक्तिविशिष्टं शुद्धचैतन्यमात्रं लक्षयति तथा चोक्तं

**"तत्पदेनोच्यते लिङ्गं त्वं पदेनाङ्गमीरितः।  
अनयोरैक्यभावोऽयं सम्बन्धोऽसिपदेन च॥**

 अहं ब्रम्हास्मीत्यत्र पूर्वोक्ततत्त्वमसीति महावाक्यगतत्वम्पदगृहीतस्थूलचिदचिद्रूपशक्तिविशिष्टो जीवोऽहम्पदेनोच्यते। तथा सूक्ष्मचिदचिद्रूपशक्तिविशिष्टः परशिवः ब्रम्हपदेनोच्यते। एवम् असिपदोक्तं तयोरैक्यमेवात्रास्मिपदेनोच्यते। तथा च परोक्षावबोधिततत्त्वमसीति महद्वाक्यार्थ एवापरोक्षेणाहं ब्रह्मास्मीति अखण्डवाक्येन अनुभूयते इति। तदुक्तं etc.

 This explanation may be compared with the explanation given above in the extract from वेदान्तसार.

 P. 49. स्कान्दे शङ्करसंहितायाम् etc.—The verses quoted from शङ्करसंहिता treat of लिङ्ग and अङ्ग, their innate oneness, their at one–ment, of how वाच्यार्थ is inapplicable and how लक्ष्यार्थ is, therefore, to be resorted to to explain away the two sentences

declaring the oneness of जीव and शिव, and how जीव attains at–one–ment with शिव. In short the verses quoted support the standpoint of and the assertion made by the author.

 P. 49. अशिवश्चेत्सदानन्दस्ततो नैव विलोकयेत्—This is a difficult line. We have translated it as "If जीव, that is really शिव, is not शिव, then he will not look up for (i. e. never attain) at–one–ment. The constrution is as—यदि जीवात्मा परमार्थतः सदानन्दः सन् अशिवः न शिवः चेत् ततो मोक्षं नैव विलोकयेत् मोक्षं न प्राप्नुयात्। पण्डितशिवकुमार comments upon this as follows:—जीवात्मा चाङ्गरूपकः अशिवः चेत् सदानन्दस्ततो नैव विलोकयेत्। अत्र विलोकयेदित्यत्रकर्मणः कर्तृत्वविवक्षा बोद्धव्या। अधिगच्छति शास्त्रार्थः स्मरति (it ought to be शास्त्रार्थं) श्रद्दधाति चेतिवत्। अयं भावः लक्ष्याङ्गरूपो जीवः अशिवश्चेत् शिवभिन्नः चेत् तदा जीवात्मनः आनन्दरूपत्वाभावात् मा न भूवं हि (not quite clear) भूयासमित्यात्मविषयकपरप्रेमसूचितात्मनः आनन्दरूपता न दृश्येत्। दृश्यते त्ववश्यम्। यद्वा नैव विलोकयेत् न चेतयेत् ब्रह्मभिन्नस्य जडत्वादिति तात्पर्यम्। इदं च परमानन्दत्वमानं सामान्यरूपेणैव भवतीति तस्मान्न सर्वथा शिवाभिन्नो जीवात्मा। सांसारिकविषयस्पृहाप्युपपद्यते। तदुक्तं विद्यारण्यैः।

**अभानेन परं प्रेम भानेन विषये स्पृहा।  
तस्मादभानं भानेऽपि प्रतिबन्धन युज्यते॥  
अध्येतृवर्गमध्यस्तपुत्राध्ययनशब्दवत्॥ इत्यादि.**

 P. 50. सोऽविद्योपाधिको भूवा भवत्यङ्गस्वरूपवान्—Here अविद्या means माया (ignorance), the sixth of the thirty six तत्वाऽ (principles) of Shaiva philosophy. The fifth is विद्या or शुद्धविद्या; from this proceeds माया. This has been explained in शिवाद्वैतमञ्जरी as—अथैवंविधशुद्धविद्यातत्त्वमेव अण्डरसन्यायेन स्वान्तर्लीनेषु भवनक्रियोन्मुखषु भावेषु अन्योन्याभावनिबन्धनभेदबुद्धिप्रधानं सत् मायातत्त्वं भवति. From this माया proceed कला, विद्या or अशुद्धविद्या, राग, काल, and नियति, which are called पञ्चकञ्चुकाऽ and which envelope the जीव and make him entangled with भवभाव (भवभावनियन्त्रितः). The in–

dividual soul is, therefore, called the कञ्चुकिन्. The जीव is required to have recourse to भक्ति as the उपासक of लिङ्ग and attain at–on–ement with शिव by उपासना.

 P. 50. इदानीं....तथात्वं प्रदर्श्यते—Here ought to begin the next section for proving the बाह्यलिङ्गधारण on the authority of स्मृतिऽ. But by some mistake, it seems, मङ्गलाचरण of this section comes later. We have not interfered with the arrangement of the text, which is, therefore, printed as found in the three copies of the text consulted.

 This section is intended to refute the second objection advanced against लिङ्गधारण, as stated in विषयावतार in words "न वा वेदमूलस्मृत्यादिगम्यत्वम्। मन्त्रादिस्मृतिषु तत्प्रापकवचनादर्शनात्। The author here vindicates the principle and creed of बाह्यलिङ्गधारण on the authority of स्मृतिऽ. It is however to be noted that many of the verses produced in authority from different स्मृतिऽ are not found in the available texts of those printed स्मृतिऽ, e. g. The verses शिवध्यानरतो भूत्वा शिवलिङ्गाङ्गसंयुतः etc. (quoted on page 52) cannot be traced in the मनुस्मृति in the three or four different printed editions of the therefore, seems that there must have been different editions or recensions of स्मृतिऽ in southern India. It has been already noted on page 62 of the notes that a verse from शातातपस्मृति "भस्मछन्नो भस्मशय्याशयानो etc." has been quoted by लिङ्गधारणचन्द्रिका on page 11 of the text; but the verse is not found in any printed text of the स्मृति. However भट्टभास्कर quotes this very verse from शातातपस्मृति in the introductory portion of his commentary on रुद्राध्याय. Similarly two verses produced by लिङ्गधारणचन्द्रिका, (pp. 51, 52) one from बोधायनस्मृति, viz. सर्वसङ्गनिवृत्तस्य etc, and the other from शातातपस्मृति, viz. वानप्रस्थं योगिनं च etc. cannot be traced in these स्मृतिऽ. But these very verses from the same sources

have been quoted in स्मृतिचन्द्रिका by देवणभट्ट (see आशौचखण्ड page 187). Hence our remark that there must have been different recensions of स्मृतिऽ in south India.

 It is not strange that there should be such different recensions or editions, when we find that two श्लोकाऽ; found in मनुस्मृति printed by निर्णयसागर, should not be found in the copy of मनुस्मृति printed by वेङ्कटेश्वर press, Bombay. The श्लोकाऽ are—

**जन्मना जायते शूद्रः संस्कारैर्द्विज उच्यते।  
विद्यया याति विप्रत्वं त्रिभिः श्रोत्रिय उच्यते॥**

**जात्या कुलेन वृत्तेन स्वाध्यायेन श्रुतेन च।  
एभिर्युक्ता हि यस्तिष्ठेन्नित्यं स द्विज उच्यते॥**

p\. s. ब्रह्म जानाति ब्राह्मणः is a variant of त्रिभिः श्रोत्रिय उच्यते।

 P. 50. तस्य तथात्वं प्रदर्श्यते—तस्य लिङ्गधारणस्य (वेदार्थप्रतिपादकगौतममन्वादिस्मृतिविहितत्वेन) तथात्वं स्मृतिविहितत्वं प्रदर्श्यते स्थाप्यते।

 P. 51. नन्वत्र etc.—This contains an objection to the statement made in the verse given above. The verse mentions six special characteristics of being a Brahmin. But, says the objecter, that there are many persons that are Brahmins but are not having these characteristics (लक्षण) e. g. माध्वाऽ and रामानुजाऽ. The author refutes this objection by saying that the verse makes a statement about a particular section of ब्राह्मणाऽ and not all (varieties of) ब्राह्मणाऽ. By–the–bye it is to be noted that the opinion of the author लिङ्गाधारिन्ऽ are ब्राह्मणाऽ. i. e. लिङ्गिब्राह्मणाऽ. This ambition of a section of Veerashaivas or Lingayatas, particularly the आराध्याऽ, to prove that Veerashaivas or Lingayatas are a kind or variety of ब्राह्मणाऽ, has given rise to a great deal of controversy and controvertial literature about the nomenclature and has made confusion worst confounded, regarding the status of Lingayatas and their religion among the Hindus and their religion or religions.

 Two instances have been given by the author of the restrictive nature of some injunctions, namely, 1(I) the injunction to perform the श्राद्ध ceremony as being inapplicable to माध्व Brahmins, if it fell on an एकादशी day (2) the injunction to observe the महाशिवरात्र vigil as being inapplicable to रामानुजवैष्णवाऽ. Though the two injunctions are general still they are limited in operation. In the same way says the author, the ब्राह्मणलक्षण given by the verse is limited in its operation.

 P. 51. नन्वेवमपि लिङ्गाङ्गिब्राह्मणोद्देशेन... तात्पर्यावसायात्—Here the objecter rightly points out in objection that any person of any वर्ण (caste or community) can be a Brahmin, if he possess the sixfold लक्षण or characteristic of Brahmanism, as set forth in the verse (मुखे मन्त्रो etc.). But the author refutes this objection by maintaining that word restricts the sixfold लक्षण to Brahmins only. The author thus indirectly means that those, that are born as Brahmins or pass as such in the public, can be लिङ्गाङ्गिब्राह्मणाऽ. His attitude, thus, betrays itself as being very narrow and against the very broadminded and broadbased spirit of the religion, which actuated the founder of the religion (the Lingayata religion) to level up all classes and give them equal opportunities to practise spiritual self–culture and attain realization of the Highest Self, i. e. लिङ्गाङ्गसामरस्य. It may, therefore, be safely concluded that the author must have been an आराध्यब्राह्मण or some other Brahmin, converted to Lingayatism, and that he lacked the real spirit of Lingayatism—विश्वबन्धुत्व prevading the religion. Moreover the author's construction— ब्राह्मणत्वे सति षड्विधधर्मवत्त्वं—seems ultravires, as there is nothing in the verse to warrant such construction.

 P. 52. तत्रैव—तत्रैव गौतमस्मृतौ. This verse is better suited to refute the third objection stated in the विषयावतार, namely, सकलजनाचरितत्वेन etc., because it says that अक्षपाद and others wore Linga on their heads, i. e. there was the custom of wearing Linga on the body. And अक्षपाद and others being great persons, the wearing of Linga was a शिष्टाचार.

 P. 52. मनुस्मृतौ etc.—In the passage (from मनुस्मृतौ upto लिङ्गधारणसिद्धिः) the author proceeds to prove the wearing of Linga on the body on the ground that burial was prescribed for wearers of Linga, as known from the verses occuring in the very autboritative स्मृतिऽ (मनुस्मृति, बोधायनस्मृति and the शातातपस्मृति). The universal custom among the Veerashaivas of burying the dead bears this out, says the author. The whole of the 2nd part of लिङ्गधारणचन्द्रिका is devoted to establish this custom as being a Vedic injunction. The words लिङ्गाङ्गसंयुत and लिङ्गैक्य in the verses, taken from मनुस्मृति and शातातपस्मृति, show that there must have been wearers of Linga in conformity with Vedic injunctions to wear it.

 P. 53. नन्वेतेनोदाहृतश्रुतिस्मृतिकदम्बकेन......पूजाद्यकरणापत्तेः।—Here there are two objections raised against the conclusion arrived at heretofore. (1) The first is that the wearing and worship of Linga on the body being an all time duty, it is to be worn and worshipped even by women in menses, when they are temporarily in a state of untonchability and when they are not even to be seen and talked with. That women should wear and worship Linga in such a state is quite an improper thing. (2) the second is that Linga, handled for worship in such a state of impurity, comes to be defiled, even after bath is taken by such women (women in menses).

 P. 53. किञ्च...कथञ्चित् योज्यम्—This contains the third objection against the principle and creed of wearing Linga established heretofore. The objection is based on the statements contained in the verses given. The words लिङ्गिन्, लिङ्गधारिणम्, लिङ्गाङ्कित, and लिङ्गिना, occurring in the verses show that the wearers of Linga are altogether an undesirable set and imply unmistakably that Linga is not to be worn at all. The author proceeds to refute these objections in order upto the end of this section.

 P. 53. मैवं etc.—Before proceeding to refute the three objections noted above, the author first establishes the propriety and necessity of performing rites, which, he says, are imperative in spite of all actions and rites being condemned by some Vedic sentences like "न कर्मणा न प्रजया धनेन etc." (taken from नारायणोपनिषत्); because there are other Vedic sentences like "सोमेन यजेत" etc. that enjoin he performance of rites. In the same way, says the author, the wearing and worship of Linga is an imperative religious duty for the attainment of final beatitude on the authority of Vedic injunction discussed heretofore.

 In connection with the ban put on all कर्म or rites and ritualism, the following from वसवपुराण, page 44 may be noted:—

**ज्योतिष्टोमादिकर्मौघः प्रजया च धनेन च।  
न लभ्यते ब्रह्मविद्या त्यागेन मुनिसत्तम॥**

**त्यक्त्वा समस्तकर्माणि मुनयो ब्रह्मवादिनः।  
प्राप्ता पूर्वेऽमृतत्वं तु शिवध्यानपरायणाः॥**

 P. 54. गृहे यस्मिन्प्रसूता स्त्री etc.—These verse declare and establish that Linga–wearing women are not untouchable, as non–Linga–wearing women are. Here begins the refutation of the 1st objection. The Linga–wearing women being free from रजःसूतक, the wearing and worship of Linga during menses is beyond cavil and is unexceptionable. It is to be noted that the Veerashaivas or Lingayatas, being अतिवर्णाश्रमिन्ऽ, have not got to observe the पञ्चसूतकाऽ or five kinds of impurity, namely, (1) जातिसूतक (2) जननसूतक (3) मरणसूतक (4) रजःसुतक and (5) उच्छिष्टसूतक. Moreover रजःसूतक is not to be observed by those that follow वर्णाश्रमधर्म (1) during the पौण्डरीकसत्र, which lasts for a long time and also (2) during the marriage ceremony of a grown up girl, if the girl is in menses in the middle of the marriage ceremony; (3) so also no मरणसूतक is to be observed by Brahmins, when they are busy with performing a पैतृकश्राद्ध. If the impurities are not to be observed by those that follow the वर्णाश्रमधर्माऽ, it is no wonder, the author means, that the Veerashaivas being अतिवर्णाश्रमिन्ऽ have not got to observe the impurities or सूतकाऽ.

 Regarding the inapplicability of रजःसूतक to a grown up girl during her marriage ceremony the author quotes in authority verses from चतुर्वर्गचिन्तामणि (on page 201) by हेमाद्रिभट्ट (there is a manuscript copy of the work in the Oriental Library, Mysore). The following authorities may be noted in addition—

स्मृतिचन्द्रिका—Vol. I, page 233—अत्र यज्ञपार्श्वः।—

**विवाहे वितते तन्त्रे होमकाले उपस्थिते।  
कन्याया ऋतुरागच्छेत्कथं कुर्वन्ति याज्ञिकाः॥  
स्नापयित्वा तु तां कन्याम् अर्चयित्वा यथाविधि।  
युञ्जान आहुतिं हुत्वा ततस्तन्त्रं प्रवर्तयेत्॥**

 So also आपस्तंवस्मृति says (आनन्दाश्रम edition, page 40) :—

**विवाहे वितते यज्ञे संस्कारे च कृते तदा।  
रजस्वला भवेत्कन्या संस्कारस्तु कथं भवेत्॥**

**स्नापयित्वा तदा कन्यामन्यैर्वस्त्रैरलङ्कृताम्।  
पुनर्मेत्याहुतिं हुत्वा शेषं कर्म समाचरेत॥**

 In addition to these the following from स्मृतिचन्द्रिका (आशौचखण्ड, pages 66–67) regarding the avoidence of आशौच on all these occassion are to be noted:—

** आशौचापवादाः—**

**ऋत्विजां दीक्षितानां च यज्ञीयं कर्म कुर्वताम्।  
सत्रिव्रतिब्रह्मचारिदातृब्रह्मविदां तथा॥**

**दाने विवाहे यज्ञे च सङ्ग्रामे देशविप्लवे।  
आपद्यपि च कष्टायां सद्यःशौचं विधीयते॥**

**अग्न्याधेयं पाकयज्ञमग्निष्टोमादिकान्मखान्।  
यः करोति वृतो यस्य स तस्यर्त्विगिहोच्यते॥**

**तावद्गृहीतयज्ञस्य त्रैविद्यस्य महामखे।  
स्नानं त्ववभृते यावत्तावत्तस्य न सूतकम्॥**

**सत्री गृहीतनियमो यज्ञे ज्ञाने च दीक्षितः।  
चान्द्रायणाद्यनुष्ठाता व्रती तु ब्रह्मचार्यपि॥**

 P. 55. तनुत्रयगतानादि etc.—Here the author states that लिङ्गधारण and the दीक्षा ceremony being a life long व्रत, the avoidance of सूतकाऽ (including रजःसूतक) becomes an exception.. all through life, i. e. the सूतकाऽ are not to be observed by the Lingayatas (see words तत्पूजायाश्च etc. on page 56).

 P. 56. अत एवाशौचनिर्णयार्थं etc.—The verses "सूतकात्प्राक् समारब्धं" etc. occur in षडशीतिस्मृति (a manuscript copy of which is in the Oriental Library, Mysore,) But in the colophon of the book it is said to have been written by सुब्रह्मण्यशास्त्रिन्. It seems that the शास्त्रिन् must have written the book in the name of कौशिकादित्य.

 P. 56. प्रारम्भो वरणं यज्ञे etc.—This verse occurs in ब्रह्माण्डपुराण (as noted by विवाहकौमुदी). This is also quoted in संस्कारमयूख by नारायणभट्ट.

 P. 56. रजस्वलानां धारणाद्यंशे etc.—Here the author states the difference of opinion about how far women in menses are to be considered pure and in regard to what duties. One set of thinkers hold that such women are pure and free to do all duties. While another set hold that they are to be considered pure and free to perform लिङ्गापूजा only.

 P. 57. अन्यशरीरनिष्ठीवनादौ etc.—This is rather obscure. I have taken it as अन्येन शरीरे निष्ठीवनेन etc. and interpret it as—when some body spits on the body of another, the person so spat on is free to mutter मन्त्राऽ only after gurgling water etc., but is not free to do any other holy rite without taking another bath.

 P. 57. यदुक्तं etc.—In this paragraph the author refutes the 2nd objection that Linga worn on the body is made impure by its contact with a person in an impure condition. He says (in the sentence पूर्वोदाहृत...तथात्वं दूरापास्तमेव) that when the body itself is never polluted or made impure, there is no possibility of Linga being made impure.

 P. 57. तथाप्यभ्युपेत्य वादेन निराक्रियते—After stating in the way aforesaid the impossibility of Linga becoming impure, he takes up the objection for the sake of argument only, that Linga becomes impure and refutes it. He reduces the objection to twofold alternatives and asks (1) whether परशिव or परब्रह्मन्, residing in the Linga, is made impure or (2) whether लिङ्ग itself, the residence of परशिव, becomes impure. Both these possible alternatives are not tenable,

says the सिद्धान्तिन्. The first is untenable because परशिव or परब्रह्मन् is maintained by the Upanishads as being निष्कल, निरञ्जन etc. and is, therefore, above pollution. The second also is untenable, because Linga, the residence of परशिव, is in intimate association with the परशिव (the Being above pollution) and is, therefore, above pollution.

 It is to be noted that all schools of philosophy hold that परब्रह्मन् is निष्कल, निरञ्जन, etc. and above being affected in any way.

 P. 57. यदि मन्यसे क्रियादीक्षासमये etc.—Here is anticipated another possible objection, that might be raised, that Linga is consecrated by the गुरु at the time of the क्रियादीक्षा ceremony of a शिष्य. But the effects of consecration are destroyed by when the wearer enters a state of impurity.

 In propriety such a question should not arise at all, when Linga is proved to be beyond pollution, i. e. above संस्कार of any kind or above being affected in any way. Still, it seems, this also is taken up for the sake of argument only.

 P. 57. कूर्मरमणीदुग्धपानमेवानुकरोति—As impossible as the drinking of milk obtained from a female tortoise. कूर्मरमणीदुग्ध is as much a myth as शशविषाण or वन्ध्यापुत्र. The objection, says the सिद्धान्तिन्, is as good as this.

 P. 57. तथाहि etc.—Here the सिद्धान्तिन् refutes the objection by saying that the consecration or संस्कार of Linga is as permanent as the अपूर्व arising from यज्ञाऽ performed. अपूर्व, according to मीमांसकाऽ, is the unseen merit produced by the sacrifices performed and attaches to the यजमान and his wife.

The अपूर्व of a person accumulates and takes the यजमान and his wife to heaven after their death. If the अपूर्व, says the सिद्धान्तिन्, remains in tact, in spite of any kind of impurity or सूतक, that might beset either the यजमान of his wife, there is no reason why the consecration, which is similar to the अपूर्व, should be destroyed by any kind of impurity besetting the person wearing Linga.

 P. 57. शक्तिरूपस्थायिसंस्कारजनन—The permanent संस्कार, full of potentiality and arising (out of दीक्षा ceremony).

 P. 57. यद्यतोऽपि नाशः कल्प्यते etc.—If the संस्कार from the दीक्षा ceremony is considered liable to be destroyed, अपूर्व should be similarly liable.

 P. 57. गङ्गास्नान etc.—गङ्गास्नान or a bath in the holy river Ganges is considered to be productive of merit attaching to the person taking the bath. The merit is similar to the लिङ्गसंस्कार. If the latter is considered liable to be destroyed, the merit arising from गङ्गास्नान is also liable to be destroyed. In such a case the injunctions to have a bath in a holy river would be meaningless; and the position of the followers of the Vedas would be similar to that of the followers of Buddha, who are heretics and have discarded all Vedic rites. The following श्लोकाऽ from स्मृतिचन्द्रिका are to be noted regarding the bath in the river Ganges, or a holy river or in any holy pond of water:—

**वैशाखे शुक्लपक्षे तु तृतीयायां तथैव च।  
गङ्गातोये नरः स्नात्वा मुच्यते सर्वकिल्बिषैः॥**

**शिवलिङ्गसमीपे तु यत्तोयं पुरसंस्थितम्।  
शिवगङ्गेति तज्ज्ञेयं तत्र स्नात्वा दिवं व्रजेत॥**

**जान्हवीस्नानमात्रेण गोसहस्रफलं भवेत्॥**

(see vol. II, page 321).

 P. 58. किञ्च etc.—This is an instance of how food addressed to शिवलिङ्ग becomes प्रसाद and is distributed among the devotees as the grace of God. Such प्रसाद is above impurity. If it is so, says the सिद्धान्तिन्, the शिवलिङ्ग itself should be much more so, and there should be no dispute about it.

 P. 58. न च स्थावरेऽपि etc.—In this para the author shows the difference between the संस्कार of स्थावरलिङ्ग and that of इष्टलिङ्ग. The former is temporary and liable to be destroyed at any time by any kind of impurity; while the latter is permanent, in as much as the इष्टलिङ्ग is to be worn on the body all the life and is to be worshipped at all times. In the case of स्थावरलिङ्ग the संस्कार is not so. And that is why reconsecration of स्थावरलिङ्ग is enjoined, when it comes to be defiled by the touch of an impure person, or animal etc.

 पण्डित शिवकुमार explains this finely as—

 'वैषम्यादिति' स्थावरलिङ्गप्राणलिङ्गयोरित्यर्थः। स्थावरलिङ्गे प्रतिदिनं पूज्यताप्रयोजकसंस्कारेण प्रयुक्तपूजायां कर्त्तृनियमाभावादेकस्याशुचित्वे पुरुषान्तरेण तत्पूजायाः निर्वाहात् तत्र परमेश्वरेणाऽशुचिस्पर्शनं न सह्यते। प्राणलिङ्गे तु एकपुरुषस्यैव यावज्जीवं पूजनीयताप्रयोजकसंस्कारस्य दीक्षाकाले गुरुणा विहितत्वादशौचावस्थायां तत्पुरुषस्पर्शे न तन्नाशः। यावज्जीवपूजाया बाधापत्या व्रतभङ्गभिया च तादृशसंस्कारस्य तेनैतन्मतेऽनाश्यत्वकल्पनादिति।

 P. 58. यच्चोक्तं etc.—From यच्चोक्तं up to the end of this section the author goes on refuting the third objection based on the verses quoted on page 53 of the text. The objection becomes threefold, when analysed as—(1) शैवाऽ and पाशुपताऽ are an undesirable religious set or sect sand, therefore, they are to be avoided. (2) the food of लिङ्गिन् or लिङ्गधारिन्ऽ is not to be taken; their very sight

should be avoided. (3) The लिङ्गिन्ऽ, being पाषण्डाऽ or heretics, are a fallen set of people and as such their association should be altogether avoided. This threefold objection goes to prove, says the objector, that Linga is not to be worn. The सिद्धान्तिन् refutes this objection in all its three parts in order. But it is to be noted first that the author's refutation is based on the interpretation of the word लिङ्ग (in लिङ्गिन्, लिङ्गधारिन् etc.) as a portable image or प्रतिमा of a deity, made of metal, stone, or some other material and not as इष्टलिङ्ग worn on the body by Veerashaivas. He interprets लिङ्ग as लीनम् अर्थं गमयतीति व्युत्पत्त्या तत्तद्देवताभिव्यक्तिस्थानशिलाताम्रादिप्रतिमा (see page 59 of the text). That लिङ्ग means चिन्ह is evident from the following words of शङ्करविजय—

**अद्वैतद्रोहिणः केचिल्लिङ्गाङ्कितभुजद्वयाः।  
केचित्फाले त्रिशूलं च डमरुं च भुजद्वये॥**

**केचित्फाले लिङ्गचिन्हं हृदि शूलं तथापरे।  
केचित्फाले भुजद्वन्द्वे हृदि नाभौ च लिङ्गिनः॥  
एवं महेशचिन्हानि धारयित्वा समागताः॥ (page 17 )**

 Similarly on page 62 it is said—ध्रुवः केन लिङ्ग्यभूत् तस्य ब्रह्मालयपर्यन्ते स्थितिः। द्रौपदी केन चिन्हेनाङ्किता तस्याः स्मरणमात्रेण वस्त्रचयदाता भूत्। अतो व्यासदास मूढबुद्धिं परित्यज्य बहिलिङ्गं त्यक्त्वा etc. So also लिङ्ग is used in the sense of चिन्ह in the book in various places. लिङ्गिन् or लिङ्गधारिन्, therefore, means any person that wears such a mark or चिन्ह or प्रतिमा of a deity and exhibits it for earning livelihood.

 P. 58. तत्र शिवसम्बन्धिनः शेषाः etc.—In refutation of the first part of the objection the author says that the verses refer to the शैवाऽ and पाशुपताऽ, that wear भस्म and रुद्राक्ष and are a kind of devotees of शिव. Here the लकुलीशपाशुपताऽ and

कालामुखशैवाऽ seem to be referred to, who by there immoral practices and life had become a disgrace themselves and brought disgrace on the community. They have been well described in the शङ्करविजय and शङ्करदिग्विजय. Their devotional practices have been briefly given by the author in words—प्रदोषकालीनशिववन्दन भस्मधारणवताम्.

 P. 58. आनन्दतीर्थीयानां च ग्रहणं भविष्यति—The author brings माध्ववैष्णवाऽ in line with शैवाऽ and पाशुपताऽ as an undesirable set on the authority of the word चक्रिन् (a wearer of a disk-mark), occurring in the verses. But it is not, possible to understand why he should all at once bring in माध्वाऽ leaving रामानुजाऽ, specially when we see that he refutes in a previous section the रामानुजवैष्णवाऽ only without referring to the माध्वाऽ, though both of these wear a disk-mark.

 In connection with the शैवाऽ and पाशुपताऽ, spoken of here, the brief discussion about such undesirable set of Shaivas as वाम, लाकुल, पाशुपत and भैरव is to be noted on pages 74 and 75. The religion of such people is considered to be मोहशास्त्राणि.

 P. 58. न चावयवशक्त्या... इति वाच्यम्— Here is the objection against the interpretation of शैवाऽ as merely wearers of भस्म and रुद्राक्ष or some such kind of शैवाऽ and not वीरशैवाऽ. The objector says that शैवाः means all Shaivas in general including the बीरशैवाऽ.

 P. 58. विशिष्यतद्बोधकलिङ्गिपदसत्वात्—The author refutes the preceding objection by saying that the words लिङ्गिन् and लिङ्गधारिन् etc. are mentioned in the verses specially and are intended to signify a different set of people. And if Veerashaivas are to be expressed they are to be expressed

by these special words and not by the word शैवाऽ. Except for this there is no meaning in using the special words लिङ्गिन् etc. But the author immediately explains the word लिङ्गिन् to mean "a bearer of a mark or प्रतिमा of a deity" and thus proves that Veerashaivas are excluded from amongst the undesirable sets or sects of people to be avoided.

 P. 58. न च...चकारविरोधात्—The author further says that the word लिङ्गिन् is not a qualifying word of an individual शैव, belonging to a शैव sect; because there is the conjunctive particle च, that adds पाशुपताऽ to शैवाऽ. So also the word तथा at the end of the line brings together लिङ्गिन्ऽ and व्रतिन्ऽ with शैवाऽ and पाशुपताऽ. The conjunctions च and तथा add one set of people to another and none of them can be qualifier of another.

 P. 58. पाषण्डपदमपि.—The author defines and explains the word पाषण्ड as meaning a लिङ्गिन्, on the authority of lexicons that state these words to be synonyms. लिङ्गिन् means, as explained immediately, a चिन्हधारिन् or प्रतिमाधारिन् and not an इष्टलिङ्गधारिन् or a Veerashaiva.

 P. 59. आदित्यं etc.—The verse,

**आदित्यमम्बिकां विष्णुं गणनाथं महेश्वरम्।  
ब्राह्मणः पूजयेत्पञ्च पञ्चयज्ञपरायणः॥**

is found in शङ्कराविजय, page 14 in तृतीय प्रकरण and enjoins the worship of the five deities in their ais, as may be unmistakably known from the context.

 P. 59. न च पाषण्डलिङ्गिपदयोः etc—Here is the refutation of the argument that लिङ्गिन् means an इष्टलिङ्गधारिन्, i e. a Veerashaiva; because ṭhe author says that लिङ्ग means a चिन्ह and

not an इष्टलिङ्ग and लिङ्गिन् means a चिन्हवान्. Therefore, there can be no relation of a qualifier and the qualified between पाषण्ड and Linga. The author is at great pains to explain away the word लिङ्गिन् or लिङ्गधारिन् as the bearer of लिङ्ग or चिन्ह. His explanation might find support in the information and discussion contained in शङ्करविजय. It is undoubted that during the times of शङ्कराचार्य all people bore marks (चिन्हाऽ) of their Godhead to an excessive and revolting degree without the spirit of real religion; and the great philosopher had to carry on a compaign against the practice and dissuade people from wearing the marks. But it is also undoubted that later the Vaishnavas condemned the wearing of लिङ्ग (इष्टलिङ्ग), as the Veerashaivas did the wearing of a 'disk-mark. There was thus mutual condemnation and vilification by Vaishnavas and Veerashaivas, after the latter developed into powerful rival religious community. Hence it is also clear that लिङ्ग might mean an इष्टलिङ्ग and a mark also. But the author says no and strives his best to prove that लिङ्ग means a चिन्ह and not an इष्टलिङ्ग in all condemnatory sentences.

 P. 59. पाषण्डं पतितं व्रात्यं.. स्मार्तपरमेव–The author here fixes up the meaning of पाषण्ड as the bearer of a mark (चिन्हधारी) and says that it means a स्मार्त. By-the-bye he refutes the 2nd objection.

 P. 59. "मुनेरन्नं न भोक्तव्यं" इत्यपि etc.—Hereafter the author refutes the 3rd objection that food from लिङ्गिन्ऽ (लिङ्गिनां, i. e. इष्टलिङ्गधारिन्) should not be taken. He says in refutation that it does not mean an इष्टलिङ्गधारिन् but a चिन्दधारिन्; because the list of persons, food offered by whom is banned as unacceptable by मनु, does not contain the person wearing इष्टलिङ्ग on his body. It is, therefore, evident, he says, that a लिङ्गिन् does not mean a Veerashaiva,

 P. 59. वस्तुतस्तु etc.—After refuting the objections the author maintains the soundness of the position of इष्टलिङ्गधारिन् on the ground of the Vedic injunction to wear Linga on the body. Hence, he says, even if there be any स्मृतिऽ and पुराणाऽ condemning Veerashaivas and इष्टलिङ्गधारण, they are not an authority against the Vedas and they are to be disregarded. The स्मृतिऽ and पुराणाऽ cannot stand opposition to Vedas, and much less establish any doctrine in contradiction to the Vedas. When the Vedas contradict a statement of a स्मृति and पुराण, that statement is to be discarded as unauthoritative. If a statement of a स्मृति (or a पुराण) is not contradicted by a Vedic sentence, a Vedic statement to that effect may be presumed, स्मृति (or a पुराणाऽ), therefore, are authoritative when they do not contradict Vedas. If they contradict, they are not only unauthoritative but are to be discarded wholly and solely. The
श्रुतिप्राबल्य is established in पूर्वमीमांसा, I-3-3, in सूत्र—विरोधेत्वनपेक्ष्यं स्यादसतिह्यनुमानम्। The commentary of रामेश्वरसूरि on the सूत्र well explains the सूत्र briefly as—उपलभ्यमानश्रुतिविरोधे अनपेक्ष्यं स्मृतिप्रामाण्यम् अनादरणीयं कल्प्यश्रुत्यपेक्षया प्रत्यक्षश्रुतेः प्रबलत्वेन बाधाद्यसम्भवादिति भावः। विरोधेऽसति श्रुतिकल्पकमनुमानं प्रवर्तते।

 It is said in गौतमस्मृति (quoted by स्मृतिचन्द्रिका vol. I, page 16)—

**श्रुतिस्मृत्योर्विरोधे तु श्रुतिरेव गरीयसी।  
अविरोधे सदा कार्यं स्मार्तं वैदिकवत्सदा॥  
तथैव लौकिकं वाक्यं स्मृतिबाधात्परित्यजेत्॥**

The author seems to be conscious that लिङ्गिन् could well be construed as इष्टलिङ्गधारिन् on the authority of various sentences condemning लिङ्गधारण. Hence he seeks the shelter of श्रुति to silence the objector. On the whole the author does not

seem to have succeeded in establishing the creed of wearing Linga on the body on the authority of स्मृतिऽ and पुराणाऽ, on account of numerous contradictory statements therein.

 P. 59. महाभारते etc.—Here the author establishes the creed of wearing Linga on the authority of the statements contained in महाभारत. In the verses quoted there are words "लिङ्गिनं ब्राह्मणं" "लिङ्गिने," which, the author says, establish the wearing of Linga. But it is not known if लिङ्ग here means इष्टलिङ्ग exactly. Some explain लिङ्ग as आश्रमादिचिन्ह, as कुल्लकभट्ट does in his commentary on मनुस्मृति He explains "अलिङ्गी लिङ्गवेषेण यो वृत्तिमुपजीवति" as—यो ब्राह्मणो ब्रह्मचर्यादिलिङ्ग मेखलाजिनदण्डादिवेषोपलक्षितः तद्वृत्या भ्रमणादिना जीविति। (see page 167 of मनुस्मृति, वेङ्कटेश्वर press, Bombay). However many Veerashaiva authors set store by these verses of महाभारत and say there were इष्टलिङ्गधारिन्ऽ in the times of महाभारत. They thus try to date back लिङ्गधारण to long past. It is no wonder that they should do so, when they trace the injunction to wear Linga in the Vedic and Upanishadic sentences, which have been so elaborately discussed in previous sections.

 P. 60. परन्तु... इत्येव पूर्ववद्वक्तव्यम्—In this passage the author attempts to prove that the undesirability attached to those persons that were fickle and deserted one deity for another for the sake of livelihood and exchanged चिन्हाऽ accordingly. Such deserters were निन्द्य, because there was no sincerity and fixity of devotion in them for want of principles. Such people alone are meant to be excommunicated and avoided. On the whole that author takes लिङ्ग to mean a चिन्ह and not the इष्टलिङ्ग worn by the Veerashaivas and says that whenever a लिङ्गिन् is condemned as an undesirable person and to be altogether avoided, it means a चिन्हधारिन्.

P. 61. कृमिकीटकपतङ्गेभ्य etc.—The first five verses quoted are the well known verses from वीरागम. These also occur in other आगमाऽ also and are alway quoted in authority by Veerashaiva writers. They give the gradation in the scale of living creatures on the criterion of intelligence and sense or understanding. The gradation from the lowest to the highest is as follows: –(1) कृमि etc. कृमिकीटकपतङ्ग, the jusects or small living beings, little gnats, moths etc., other various similar creatures and birds. This class includes all स्वेदजाऽ and अण्डजाऽ, out of the four classes of creatures, according to Hindu philosophy. The four classes of beings are (a) उद्भिज, or sprouts springing from the earth, that is, plant life or vegetation in general. The list omits plant life in general from living creatures. Vegetation, therefore, seems to have been excluded from living intelligent creatures. (b) स्वेदज or those spring from atmosphere (warm vapour or sweat). (c) अण्डज or the creatures coming of out eggs or oviparous. (d) जरायुज or born of foetus alive i. e. viviparous animals.

  (II) All viviparous animals excluding human beings

 (III) Human beings.

 (IV) द्विजाऽ or twice-born. The persons that, after physical birth, enter spiritual life along with the life of intellectual culture after the उपनयन ceremony, which ceremony is considered to give them second birth, called spiritual birth, in the case of those that follow वर्णाश्रमधर्म of Hindus. But in the case of those that do not follow वर्णाश्रमधर्म, दीक्षा ceremony gives them the second birth. In the case of the

latter intellectual life (called education) might begin previously to दीक्षा ceremony; but it continues some time after the ceremony.

 (V) विप्र is one that receives education and attains intellectual culture.

 मनुस्मृति defines विप्र as follows:—(see निर्णयसागर edition).

**जन्मना जायते शुद्रः संस्कारैर्द्विज उच्यते।  
विद्यया याति विप्रत्वं त्रिभिः श्रोत्रिय उच्यते॥**

 (VI) कृतबुद्धिः—he that becomes a man of principles after receiving education.

 (VII) कर्ता—he that acts on those principles.

 (VIIl) सन्न्यासिन्—he that rises above वर्णाश्रमधर्म, i. e. he that discards these and leads a life on broad principles of humanity and religion, and not a person in the fourth stage of life according to वर्णाश्रमधर्म.

 (IX) विज्ञानिन्—a man of profound learning or one that attains intellectual and spiritual height.

 (X) शङ्करपूजक—a विज्ञानी शैव

 (XI) लिङ्गाङ्गसङ्गिन्–A shaiva that wears शिवलिङ्ग on the body; a Veerashaiva.

 (XII) षट्स्थलज्ञानवान्—A Veerashaiva that understands the षट्स्थल—philosophy and practises षट्स्थलाचार or षट्स्थल principles to attain मोक्ष. This means that mere wearing of Linga will not do for a Veerashaiva; he must grasp the principles of षट्स्थल philosophy and practise them.

वीररहस्य or पारमेश्वरागम contains these verses, but in addition gives the gradation in a different form:—

**भूतानां प्राणिनः श्रेष्ठा बुद्धिमन्तस्ततोऽधिकाः।  
ततस्ततोऽपि च बृहद्वृहदाकृतयो घनाः॥**

**सर्वोत्तमा हि मनुजास्तत्र विप्रा महोत्तमाः।  
वेदिनः कर्मकर्तारस्तदर्थज्ञा विशेषतः॥**

**ततो वेदाङ्गसारज्ञास्ततः सन्न्यासिनः पराः।  
ततः पाशुपताः श्रेष्ठा लिङ्गिनस्तु ततोऽधिकाः॥**

**ततोऽधिका महाश्रेष्ठा वीरमाहेश्वराः शिवे।  
न तेभ्यो ह्यधिकः कश्चिद् वीरशैवाश्रयात्परः॥**

**तावान्माहमसम्पन्नो वीरशैवः परः शिवः।  
साक्षान्मद्रूपतामेत्य वीरशैवमती भवेत्॥**

 In comparison with the above and in explanation of the four classes of creatures, the following श्लोकाऽ from मनुस्मृति may be noted:—

**पशवश्च मृगाश्चैव व्यालाश्चोभयतोदतः।  
रक्षांसि च पिशाचाश्च मनुष्याश्च जरायुजाः॥**

**अण्डजाः पक्षिणः सर्पाः नक्रा मत्स्याश्च कच्छपाः।  
यानि चैवं प्रकाराणि स्थलजान्योदकानि च॥**

**स्वेदजं दंशमशकं यूकामक्षिकमत्कुणम्।  
उष्मणश्चोपजायन्ते यच्चान्यत्किञ्चिदीदृशम्॥**

**उद्भिजाः स्थावराः सर्वे बीजकाण्डप्ररोहिणः।  
ओषध्यः फलपाकोता बहुपुष्पफलोपगाः॥**

**भूतानां प्राणिनः श्रेष्ठा प्राणिनां बुद्धिजीविनः।  
बुद्धिमत्सु नराः श्रेष्ठाः नरेषु ब्राह्मणाः स्मृताः॥**

**ब्राह्मणेषु च विद्वांसो विद्वत्सु कृतबुद्धयः।**  
**कृतबुद्धिषु कर्तारः कर्तृषु ब्रह्मवेदिनः॥**

 The वीरागम adds to the gradation given by मनु (1) शङ्करपूजक or a शैव (2) लिङ्गाङ्ग पङ्गिन् (3) षट्स्थलज्ञानवान्, even though there is some difference between the gradation previous to this addition. The upshot of the verses is that the followers of Veerashaivism stand at the top cf all human beings, particularly in respect of religion. The author concludes on the authority of these that the wearers of अष्टलिङ्ग, being so highly praised, cannot be included among the undesirable and condemned class of beings, Therefore, whenever a लिङ्गिन् is condemned, it means that a प्रतिमाधारक or चिन्हधारक is condemned. It is to be noted all Veerashaiva writers base all their discussion on these verses regarding the religious and communal status of Lingayatas among the Hindus in India.

 Verses 69 say that non-Linga-weares as well as कुण्ड, गोलक, and देवलक are a condemned class of persons. Verses 10-13 state that those that do not worship far, that is, those that are not Shaivas, can never expect to attain They remain fallen until and unless they become shaivas and especially Veerashaivas. All the statements contained in the last four verses are, it seems, due to high sectarian spirit and fervour.

 P. 62. ननु.... पाषण्डपदं तत्परमेवेति चेत्— This contains an objection to the interpretation by the author of the word पाषण्ड, as a heretic, a bearer of a चिन्ह, one that changes one religion for another and the mark of that religion for that of the other. But the objector quotes verses from विष्णुपुराण descriptive of पाषण्डाऽ and says that the interpretation of the word पांषड as made by the सिद्धान्तिन्, the author, was wrong. But the word is has been explained by ब्रह्मवैवर्तपुराण quoted by वीरशैवोत्कर्षप्रदीपिका as—

**पाशद्वेन त्रयीधर्मो जगतः पालनात्स्मृतः।  
तं खण्डयन्ति यस्मात्तं पाषण्डास्तेन हेतुना॥**

so also पांषड is explained as—पाति रक्षति दुरितेभ्यः पाः वेदधर्मः तं षडयति निष्फलं करोति इति। (see वाचस्पत्यं, page 4323) अमर also gives वेदाचारत्यागिन् as the synonym of पाषण्ड. "The meaning of the word पाषण्ड seems to be very general and it would amount to "any one that acts contrary to Vedic rules in any respect is a पाषण्ड". The author's refutation, therefore, that just follows seems to be right.

 P. 62. चेन्मैवं etc.—Here the सिद्धान्तिन् refutes the above objection by saying that the word पाषण्ड has a variety of meanings and is very elastic. The meaning of the word is not fixed but very wide so as to include in it any kind of heretic or an undesirable person. Different पुराणाऽ treat the word पाषण्ड in different senses. Hence it is no use, says the author, taking stand on the authority of one पुराण and reject the sense of the word as given in others. Hence any person degraded morally or religiously is a q and as such is an undesirable and unsociable person.

 P. 63. एवं पूर्वोदाहृतनिषेधवचनानां etc.— Upto the end of the section now the author makes a general statement that the sentences of condemnation found in different पुराणाऽ of the things mentioned in condemnation, are not really intended to condemn but are meant as an indirect praise of the subject-matter under treatment. Hence not much importance is to be attached to the condemnatory verses and they are to be taken as a foil to set off the importance of the matter treated in a particular section of the पुराण, This practice of the पुराणऽ eulogising a particular thing is peculiar and conforms of the न्याय viz. नहि निन्दा निन्दितुमिष्टे परं स्तुत्यं स्तौति (in the words of the author). This is taken from शबरभाष्य on a पूर्वमीमांसासूत्र (II-4-20). The exact wording of the न्याय there is—नहि निन्दा निन्दितुं प्रयुज्यत। किं तर्हि—निन्दितादितरत् प्रशंसितुम्। (Blame is not employed in order dispraise something that is blame- 

worthy, but rather to praise something other than that, i. e. the topic under consideration. यामुनमुनि illustrates this न्याय so well in his आगमप्रामाण्यम् (page 49) as follows:—

 ननु चेदं वेदमूलत्वं पञ्चरात्रतन्त्राणामनुपपन्नं वेदनिन्दादर्शनात्। उक्तं हि चतुर्षु वेदेषु पुरुषार्थमलभमानः शाण्डिल्य इदं शास्त्रमधीतवानिति। अनवगत वचनव्यक्तेरयं पर्यनुयोगः। न हि निन्दा निन्द्यं निन्दितुं प्रवर्तते अपि तु निन्दितादितरत्प्रशंसितुं यच्चैतरेयकब्राह्मणे ‘प्रातः प्रातरनृतं ते वदन्ति' इत्यनुदित होमनिन्दा उदितहोमप्रशंसार्थेति लभ्यते। यथा मानवे (iv 124),

**'ऋग्वेदो देवदैवत्यो यजुर्वेदस्तु मानुषः।  
सामवेदस्तु पित्र्यः स्यात्तस्मात्तस्याशुचिर्ध्वनिः॥**

इति सामवेदनिन्दा इतवेदप्रशंसार्था। यथा वा भारते।

**चत्वार एकतो वेदा भारतं चैकमेकतः।  
समागतै तु ऋषिभिस्तुलयारोपितं पुरा॥**

**महत्त्वे च गुरत्वे च ध्रियमाणं यतोऽधिकम्।  
महत्त्वाच्च गुरुत्वाच्च महाभारतमुच्यते॥'**

 इति महाभारतप्रशंसार्थेति गृह्यते न वेदनिन्देति। एवं पञ्चरात्रप्रशंसति गम्यते।

 रामेश्वरसूरि also puts it briefly as—नहि निन्दा निन्दायां तालर्थं किन्तु पक्षान्तरविधौ.

 Except for this there would be no end to a controversy based on Pauranic sentences, which are mutually condemnatory and contradictory. In illustration of what the author says, he quotes verses condemnatory of the यतिऽ or सन्यासिन्ऽ, which if followed closely there would be an end to the religious life of both माध्व and रामानुज Vaishnavas. The author adopts a very sensible course, indeed. He, therely, shows himself to be conscious of irreconcilable statements made by the पुराणाऽ.

 The verses quoted here are aptly descriptive of the pseudo-ascetics or pseudo-anchorites and they are fully borne out by the conditions seen generally at present. But they are applicable to all ascetics Vaishnava as well as Shaiva; though they have been taken from Shaiva पुराणाऽ, they are, I think, descriptive of false ascetics of all sects and sections of the public and not merely Vaishnava यतिऽ.

 This is a miscellaneous sectibn containing the treatment of different topics as follows. (1) An objection to लिङ्गधारण, that is, इष्टलिङ्गधारण, based on the interpretation of लिङ्गवृत्ति by लिङ्गभट्ट's commentary on अमरकोश, (2) Establishment of लिङ्गधारण on the authority of पुराणाऽ. (3) Establishing the custom of wearing Linga by great and distinguished persons and Gods and Goddesses, that is, proving that लिङ्गधारण was a शिष्टाचार. (4) Lastly the refutation by some objections raised against the conclusions arrived at on the authority of Pauranic sentences in support of the custom or शिष्टाचार of wearing इष्टलिङ्ग

 P. 64. ननु... मा भूदिति चेत्—Here the objection based upon the interpretation of लिङ्गवृत्ति is stated. The word लिङ्गवृत्ति occurs in अमरकोश (ब्रह्मवर्ग, stanza 54). The interpretation of the word लिंवृत्ति, quoted by the author fordiscussion, is by लिङ्गभट्ट, a commentator of अमरकोश (see अमरकोश with the commentary called लिङ्गाभट्टीय by लिङ्गभट्ट page 466, Andhra edition). In contrast with the interpretation of the word by लिङ्गभट्ट, the interpretation of the word by क्षीरस्वामिन् and भानुजिदीक्षित (commentators of अमरकोश) should be noted as follows:—

 क्षीरस्वामिन्ः—धर्मः ध्वजः चिन्हम् इवास्य धर्मध्वजी। लिङ्गाज्जटादेः वृत्तिजीविका यस्य सः। अत्र अन्तेर कीर्णं रेतः अस्य अवकीर्णौ। ब्रह्मवार्यवकर्णिस्यात् कामतस्तु स्त्रियं व्रजेत्। इति.
